## Chess-TUI Crate Exploration Report

### 1. **End-Game UX** ‚ÄĒ Win/Loss/Draw Banner Rendering

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`

- **Game-over banner function:** `game_over_banner()` at **line 1173**. Returns `Option<Vec<Line<'static>>>` keyed on `GameStatus` enum variants (`Ongoing`, `Won {winner, reason}`, `Drawn {reason}`).
- **Win banner** (lines 1176‚Äď1199): Renders "‚ėÖ  GAME OVER  ‚ėÖ" (yellow bold), winner name (side-colored), reason via `win_reason_label()`, and footer ("Press 'n' for new game, 'u' to take back, 'q' to quit.").
- **Draw banner** (lines 1201‚Äď1217): Renders "‚ÄĒ  DRAW  ‚ÄĒ" (yellow bold) with reason.
- **Where rendered:** Both `draw_sidebar()` (**line 834**) and `draw_sidebar_net()` (**line 940**) call the banner function and inject it at the top of the status sidebar.
- **GameStatus enum:** Defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` at **line 20**. WinReason includes `Checkmate`, `Stalemate`, `Resignation`, `OnlyOneSideHasPieces`, `Timeout`, `GeneralCaptured`.
- **Net rematch flow:** In `app.rs` at **line 414**, pressing 'n' post-game sends `ClientMsg::Rematch` via `n.client.cmd_tx.send()`. Sets `last_msg = "Rematch requested. Waiting for opponent‚Ä¶"`.

### 2. **Initial Menu / Variant Picker**

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` and `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`

- **Picker UI:** `draw_picker()` at **ui.rs line 46**. Renders title "chess-tui" (bold), instructions "Choose a variant. Arrow keys + Enter; q to quit.", then 7 entries.
- **Picker entries:** Defined in `app.rs` **line 28** as `enum PickerEntry` with 7 options: `Xiangqi`, `XiangqiStrict`, `BanqiPurist`, `BanqiTaiwan`, `BanqiAggressive`, `ConnectToServer`, `Quit`. Each has a `.label()` method (lines 50‚Äď60) with Chinese + English text.
- **Display style:** Cursor prefix "‚Ė∂ " on selected row, yellow+bold for active entry, gray otherwise. No ASCII art title‚ÄĒjust text.
- **Pre-game screens:** Picker ‚Üí (if ConnectToServer) ‚Üí HostPrompt (line 166) ‚Üí LobbyView (line 199). LobbyView shows live room list, password-lock entry, and create-room flow.

### 3. **Animation / Draw Loop**

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs`

- **Draw loop:** `run_loop()` at **line 225** is **event-driven with poll intervals**, not a fixed tick timer. Each iteration:
  1. Calls `app.tick_net()` (drains WebSocket events)
  2. Calls `terminal.draw(|frame| ui::draw(frame, app))?` (redraws)
  3. Blocks on `event::poll(Duration::from_millis(poll_ms))` (lines 239‚Äď244)
- **Poll intervals:** 60ms for Net/Lobby screens (responsive to server pushes), 200ms for local games. **No timer-based confetti animation‚ÄĒonly redraws on input or poll timeout.**
- **Implication for confetti:** Would require adding a **frame counter or animation state** that advances each draw and a **shorter poll interval** during animation (or spawning a background task to trigger `should_redraw` flag every N ms).

### 4. **Check/ŚįáŤĽć Detection**

**Files:** 
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` line 232
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/rules/xiangqi.rs` line 257

- **Exposed API:** `GameState::is_in_check(side: Side) -> bool` at **state/mod.rs:232**. Delegates to `crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check()` at **xiangqi.rs:257**.
- **Check banner in TUI:** Already rendered in `draw_sidebar()` at **ui.rs line 884**: `if g.state.is_in_check(view.side_to_move)` ‚Üí displays "‚ö† CHECK ŚįáŤĽć ‚ÄĒ your general is under attack" (red bold). Only shown when game is `Ongoing` and xiangqi variant (line 884).
- **Net mode:** No check banner in `draw_sidebar_net()` ‚ÄĒ would need to be added if desired (currently only banners are game-over and status).

### 5. **Settings / Config Plumbing**

**Files:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs` and `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`

- **CLI struct:** `Cli` at **main.rs line 40**. Flags like `--style {cjk,ascii}` (line 44) and `--no-color` (line 48) are parsed via `clap` and passed to `AppState::new_*()` constructors.
- **State plumbing:** Both `style: Style` and `use_color: bool` are stored as fields in `AppState` (line 212‚Äď213) and threaded through all screen constructors (`new_picker`, `new_game`, `new_net`, etc.) at lines 243‚Äď302.
- **Renderer consumption:** `ui::draw()` reads `app.style` and `app.use_color` and passes them to drawing functions. E.g., `draw_game()` at **ui.rs line 67** extracts both before calling `draw_board()` and `draw_sidebar()` (lines 78‚Äď79, 96).
- **Pattern for new flags:** Add field to `Cli`, add to `AppState`, thread through constructors, read in `ui.rs` drawing functions. A `--no-confetti` or `--no-check-banner` flag would follow this exact pattern.

---

**Key takeaway:** Confetti/banner features have all scaffolding in place. Check detection is live. Banners are rendered in sidebar. The draw loop redraw-on-timeout means animations need either (a) a per-frame counter in AppState + shorter poll interval during animation, or (b) a background timer task to force redraws. CLI flags thread cleanly through AppState to the renderer.

## Final Report: Chess-Web Crate Architecture Mapping

### 1. **End-game UX Today** (Lines: play.rs:230-235, sidebar.rs:43-64)
- **Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs:43-64`
- **Current Implementation**: `GameStatus` enum from chess-core (imported at line 9) drives three outcomes:
  - `Ongoing` ‚Üí "Ongoing" status text (line 46)
  - `Won { winner, reason }` ‚Üí formatted as "{side} wins ‚ÄĒ {reason}" (line 52) with class `status.won`
  - `Drawn { reason }` ‚Üí formatted as "Drawn ‚ÄĒ {reason}" with class `status.drawn`
- **CSS File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/style.css` (lines 290-293)
  - `.status.won { color: var(--accent); }`
  - `.status.drawn { color: var(--muted); }`
  - `.status.ongoing { color: var(--muted); font-weight: 400; }`
- **Play page**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs:230-238` also checks `GameStatus::Won | GameStatus::Drawn` to disable rematch/resign buttons
- **No banner overlay yet** ‚ÄĒ just inline text in sidebar.

---

### 2. **Initial Picker / Lobby UI** (Lines: picker.rs, lobby.rs)
- **Picker Page**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs:8-24`
  - Title: "Chinese Chess" (h1, line 11)
  - Subtitle explains URL encoding & variant selection
  - **Grid of 4 cards** (line 16): Xiangqi (strict/casual radio), Banqi (preset + house rules checkboxes), Three-Kingdom (WIP link), Online (lobby link)
  - Form-heavy UX with `.variant-card--form` class for interactive cards
- **Lobby Page**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/lobby.rs:59-86`
  - Text inputs for room name + password (plain HTML)
  - Connection banner (ConnBanner component, lines 147-160) with status class (ok/pending/error)
  - Room list shows seats, variant, status badges (lines 89-141)
  - Plain form-based, no branding imagery
- **Global Stylesheet**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/style.css`
  - CSS vars for colors (--bg, --fg, --accent, --red, --black, --green, etc.) at top
  - Picker grid: `.picker-grid` (lines 39-43), `.variant-card` (lines 45-59)
  - Lobby: `.lobby` (lines 316-327), `.rooms-list` + `.room-row` (lines 338-357)

---

### 3. **Animation Feasibility**
- **No existing @keyframes** ‚ÄĒ CSS has only `.variant-card:hover` and `.btn:hover` transitions (0.12s ease)
- **No canvas usage** ‚ÄĒ pure SVG board rendering (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs`)
- **Leptos signals everywhere** ‚ÄĒ view! macro with reactive `create_signal`, `create_memo`, `create_effect`
- **Feasibility**: CSS animations are natural fit; can attach Leptos signals to trigger `.confetti-burst` or similar class on game-over

---

### 4. **Check/ŚįáŤĽć Detection Plumbing**
- **Chess-core function available**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:232-233`
  ```rust
  pub fn is_in_check(&self, side: Side) -> bool {
      crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check(self, side)
  }
  ```
- **But NOT exposed in chess-web client** ‚ÄĒ neither sidebar nor board components call it
- **TUI uses it**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs:884` calls `g.state.is_in_check(view.side_to_move)` for Xiangqi only
- **Web client limitation**: `GameState` is not directly accessible from `PlayerView` (only read-only projection). Would need either:
  - Expose `is_in_check()` function in WASM bindings, OR
  - Server broadcasts check status in `ServerMsg::Update`

---

### 5. **Settings / Preferences**
- **No client-side preferences system** ‚ÄĒ no localStorage, no settings panel
- **URL-driven entirely**: 
  - Picker routes encode rules into URL: `build_local_href()` in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs`
  - Play page reads `?password=` and `?role=spectator` query params (play.rs:22-27)
- **For confetti toggle**: Would need to add query param (e.g., `?confetti=off`) or localStorage via Leptos `create_local_resource()`

---

### 6. **Three-Kingdom WIP Overlay** (REUSABLE PATTERN!)
- **Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs:136-147`
- **CSS**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/style.css:201-228`

- **Leptos**: `<Show when=move || wip>` guard (local.rs:136) ‚ÄĒ toggles overlay on `wip: bool` prop
- **VICTORY overlay would follow same pattern**: dark scrim + centered banner with `border: 1px solid var(--accent)`, can add `@keyframes pulse` or `scale-up` animation

40	#[derive(Parser, Debug)]
41	#[command(name = "chess-tui", about = "ratatui frontend for chess-core")]
42	struct Cli {
43	    /// Render style.
44	    #[arg(long, value_enum, default_value_t = StyleArg::Cjk)]
45	    style: StyleArg,
46	
47	    /// Disable ANSI color (keeps the chosen glyph set).
48	    #[arg(long)]
49	    no_color: bool,
50	
51	    /// Render from this side's perspective (debug; default RED). Ignored
52	    /// when `--connect` is set ‚ÄĒ the server assigns a side on join.
53	    #[arg(long = "as", value_enum, default_value_t = SideArg::Red)]
54	    observer: SideArg,
55	
56	    /// Connect to a chess-net-server instead of starting a local game.
57	    /// Example: --connect ws://127.0.0.1:7878 (the trailing /ws is added
58	    /// automatically if missing). Use ws://host/ws/<room-id> to target a
59	    /// specific room on a multi-room server; pair with --password if the
60	    /// room is locked.
61	    #[arg(long)]
62	    connect: Option<String>,
63	
64	    /// Open the lobby browser for the given chess-net server. Example:
65	    /// --lobby ws://127.0.0.1:7878 ‚ÄĒ picks a room from the live list (or
66	    /// creates one) instead of joining a fixed URL. Mutually exclusive
67	    /// with --connect; if both are provided, --lobby wins.
68	    #[arg(long)]
69	    lobby: Option<String>,
70	
71	    /// Password for password-locked rooms when paired with --connect. The
72	    /// in-TUI lobby browser prompts for the password directly, so this
73	    /// flag is mostly for scripted reconnects.
74	    #[arg(long)]
75	    password: Option<String>,
76	
77	    #[command(subcommand)]
78	    cmd: Option<Cmd>,
79	}
80	
81	#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]
82	enum Cmd {
83	    /// Xiangqi (9√ó10). Casual rules by default ‚ÄĒ moves that leave your own
84	    /// general capturable are allowed; the game ends when the general is
85	    /// actually captured. Pass `--strict` for the standard self-check filter.
86	    Xiangqi {
87	        /// Strict mode: standard rules. Moves that would leave your own
88	        /// general in check are rejected.
89	        #[arg(long)]
90	        strict: bool,
91	    },
92	    /// Banqi (4√ó8 face-down).
93	    Banqi {
94	        /// Preset bundle of house rules.
95	        #[arg(long, value_enum)]
96	        preset: Option<PresetArg>,
97	        /// Comma-separated house rules: chain,dark,rush,horse-diagonal,cannon-fast.
98	        #[arg(long)]
99	        house: Option<String>,
100	        /// Deterministic shuffle seed.
101	        #[arg(long)]
102	        seed: Option<u64>,
103	    },
104	}
105	
106	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, ValueEnum)]
107	enum StyleArg {
108	    Cjk,
109	    Ascii,
110	}
111	
112	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, ValueEnum)]
113	enum SideArg {
114	    Red,
115	    Black,
116	}
117	
118	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, ValueEnum)]
119	enum PresetArg {
120	    Purist,
121	    Taiwan,
122	    Aggressive,
123	}
124	
125	fn main() -> Result<()> {
126	    let cli = Cli::parse();
127	
128	    let style = match cli.style {
129	        StyleArg::Cjk => Style::Cjk,
130	        StyleArg::Ascii => Style::Ascii,
131	    };
132	    let observer = match cli.observer {
133	        SideArg::Red => Side::RED,
134	        SideArg::Black => Side::BLACK,
135	    };
136	    let use_color = !cli.no_color;
137	
138	    let app = if let Some(host) = cli.lobby.as_deref() {
139	        let url = normalize_lobby_url(host).map_err(|e| anyhow!(e))?;
140	        // Strip the /lobby suffix before storing as `host` so the lobby
141	        // can re-derive `/ws/<id>` for joins.
142	        let host = url.trim_end_matches("/lobby").to_string();
143	        AppState::new_lobby(host, style, use_color, observer)
144	    } else if let Some(url) = cli.connect.as_deref() {
145	        let mut url = normalize_connect_url(url).map_err(|e| anyhow!(e))?;
146	        if let Some(pw) = cli.password.as_deref() {
147	            let sep = if url.contains('?') { '&' } else { '?' };
148	            url = format!("{url}{sep}password={}", crate::url::urlencode(pw));
149	        }
150	        AppState::new_net(url, style, use_color)
151	    } else {
152	        match &cli.cmd {
153	            None => AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer),
154	            Some(Cmd::Xiangqi { strict }) => {
155	                let rules = if *strict { RuleSet::xiangqi() } else { RuleSet::xiangqi_casual() };
156	                AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer)
157	            }
158	            Some(Cmd::Banqi { preset, house, seed }) => {
159	                let rules = build_banqi_rules(preset.as_ref(), house.as_deref(), *seed)?;
160	                AppState::new_game(rules, style, use_color, observer)
161	            }
162	        }
163	    };
164	
165	    run(app)
166	}
167	
168	fn build_banqi_rules(
169	    preset: Option<&PresetArg>,
170	    house: Option<&str>,
171	    seed: Option<u64>,
172	) -> Result<RuleSet> {
173	    let mut flags = HouseRules::empty();
174	    if let Some(p) = preset {
175	        flags |= match p {
176	            PresetArg::Purist => chess_core::rules::PRESET_PURIST,
177	            PresetArg::Taiwan => chess_core::rules::PRESET_TAIWAN,
178	            PresetArg::Aggressive => chess_core::rules::PRESET_AGGRESSIVE,
179	        };
180	    }
181	    if let Some(s) = house {
182	        for tok in s.split(',') {
183	            flags |= match tok.trim() {
184	                "chain" => HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE,
185	                "dark" | "dark-chain" => HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN,
186	                "rush" | "chariot-rush" => HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH,
187	                "horse-diagonal" | "diag" => HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL,
188	                "cannon-fast" | "fast-cannon" => HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE,
189	                other => return Err(anyhow!("unknown house rule: {other}")),
190	            };
191	        }
192	    }
193	    let flags = chess_core::rules::house::normalize(flags);
194	    Ok(match seed {
195	        Some(s) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(flags, s),
196	        None => RuleSet::banqi(flags),
197	    })
198	}
199	
200	fn run(mut app: AppState) -> Result<()> {
201	    let mut terminal = setup_terminal()?;
202	    let result = run_loop(&mut terminal, &mut app);
203	    teardown_terminal(&mut terminal).ok();
204	    result
205	}
206	
207	fn setup_terminal() -> Result<Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>> {
208	    enable_raw_mode().context("enable raw mode")?;
209	    let mut stdout = io::stdout();
210	    execute!(stdout, EnterAlternateScreen, crossterm::event::EnableMouseCapture)
211	        .context("enter alt screen")?;
212	    let backend = CrosstermBackend::new(stdout);
213	    let terminal = Terminal::new(backend).context("init terminal")?;
214	    Ok(terminal)
215	}
216	
217	fn teardown_terminal(terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>) -> Result<()> {
218	    disable_raw_mode().ok();
219	    execute!(terminal.backend_mut(), LeaveAlternateScreen, crossterm::event::DisableMouseCapture)
220	        .ok();
221	    terminal.show_cursor().ok();
222	    Ok(())
223	}
224	
225	fn run_loop(terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>, app: &mut AppState) -> Result<()> {
226	    loop {
227	        // Drain ws events first so the next draw shows server-pushed state.
228	        // No-op outside Net mode.
229	        app.tick_net();
230	
231	        terminal.draw(|frame| ui::draw(frame, app))?;
232	        if app.should_quit {
233	            return Ok(());
234	        }
235	
236	        // Shorter poll in Net / Lobby modes keeps server pushes feeling
237	        // snappy (we redraw at most every poll interval). 200ms in
238	        // single-process modes is fine ‚ÄĒ no async source to drain.
239	        let poll_ms = if matches!(app.screen, app::Screen::Net(_) | app::Screen::Lobby(_)) {
240	            60
241	        } else {
242	            200
243	        };
244	        if event::poll(Duration::from_millis(poll_ms))? {
245	            let mode = app.input_mode();
246	            let quit_confirm = app.quit_confirm_open;
247	            let action = match event::read()? {
248	                Event::Key(k) if k.kind == event::KeyEventKind::Press => {
249	                    from_key(k, mode, quit_confirm)
250	                }
251	                Event::Mouse(m) => from_mouse(m),
252	                Event::Resize(_, _) => Action::None,
253	                _ => Action::None,
254	            };
255	            app.dispatch(action);
256	        }
257	    }
258	}
259
```
</details></tool-use>

1	use chess_core::coord::Square;
2	use chess_core::piece::Side;
3	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
4	use chess_core::state::GameStatus;
5	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
6	use chess_net::protocol::{ChatLine, ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
7	use leptos::*;
8	use leptos_router::{use_params_map, use_query_map};
9	
10	use crate::components::board::Board;
11	use crate::components::chat_panel::ChatPanel;
12	use crate::config::room_url;
13	use crate::routes::variant_slug;
14	use crate::state::{find_move, truncate_front, ClientRole};
15	use crate::ws::{connect, ConnState, WsHandle};
16	
17	const CHAT_HISTORY_CAP: usize = 50;
18	
19	#[component]
20	pub fn PlayPage() -> impl IntoView {
21	    let params = use_params_map();
22	    let query = use_query_map();
23	    let room = move || params.with(|p| p.get("room").cloned().unwrap_or_default());
24	    let password = move || query.with(|q| q.get("password").cloned().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()));
25	    let watch_only = move || {
26	        query.with(|q| q.get("role").map(|r| r.eq_ignore_ascii_case("spectator")).unwrap_or(false))
27	    };
28	
29	    // Open the WS once on mount. Spectator opt-in propagates into the URL.
30	    let url = room_url(&room(), password().as_deref(), watch_only());
31	    let (handle, incoming, conn) = connect(url);
32	
33	    let (rules, set_rules) = create_signal::<Option<RuleSet>>(None);
34	    let (role, set_role) = create_signal::<Option<ClientRole>>(None);
35	    let (view_signal, set_view) = create_signal::<Option<PlayerView>>(None);
36	    let (chat, set_chat) = create_signal::<Vec<ChatLine>>(Vec::new());
37	    let (toast, set_toast) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
38	
39	    create_effect(move |_| match incoming.get() {
40	        Some(ServerMsg::Hello { observer, rules: r, view: v, .. }) => {
41	            set_role.set(Some(ClientRole::Player(observer)));
42	            set_rules.set(Some(r));
43	            set_view.set(Some(v));
44	            set_toast.set(None);
45	        }
46	        Some(ServerMsg::Spectating { rules: r, view: v, .. }) => {
47	            set_role.set(Some(ClientRole::Spectator));
48	            set_rules.set(Some(r));
49	            set_view.set(Some(v));
50	            set_toast.set(None);
51	        }
52	        Some(ServerMsg::Update { view: v }) => {
53	            set_view.set(Some(v));
54	        }
55	        Some(ServerMsg::ChatHistory { lines }) => {
56	            set_chat.set(lines);
57	        }
58	        Some(ServerMsg::Chat { line }) => {
59	            set_chat.update(|buf| {
60	                buf.push(line);
61	                truncate_front(buf, CHAT_HISTORY_CAP);
62	            });
63	        }
64	        Some(ServerMsg::Error { message }) => {
65	            set_toast.set(Some(message));
66	        }
67	        Some(ServerMsg::Rooms { .. }) | None => {}
68	    });
69	
70	    let chat_handle = handle.clone();
71	    let on_chat_send: Callback<String> = Callback::new(move |text: String| {
72	        chat_handle.send(ClientMsg::Chat { text });
73	    });
74	
75	    let handle_for_board = handle.clone();
76	    let handle_for_sidebar = handle;
77	    let room_label = room();
78	
79	    view! {
80	        <section class="game-page">
81	            <div class="board-pane">
82	                <Show
83	                    when=move || view_signal.get().is_some() && role.get().is_some()
84	                    fallback=move || view! { <ConnPlaceholder room=room() conn=conn/> }
85	                >
86	                    <BoardWrapper
87	                        view_signal=view_signal
88	                        role=role
89	                        handle=handle_for_board.clone()
90	                    />
91	                </Show>
92	                <Show when=move || toast.get().is_some()>
93	                    <div class="toast" on:click=move |_| set_toast.set(None)>
94	                        {move || toast.get().unwrap_or_default()}
95	                    </div>
96	                </Show>
97	            </div>
98	            <div class="right-column">
99	                <OnlineSidebar
100	                    room=room_label
101	                    role=role
102	                    view_signal=view_signal
103	                    rules=rules
104	                    conn=conn
105	                    handle=handle_for_sidebar
106	                />
107	                <ChatPanel
108	                    role=role.into()
109	                    log=chat.into()
110	                    on_send=on_chat_send
111	                />
112	            </div>
113	        </section>
114	    }
115	}
116	
117	#[component]
118	fn ConnPlaceholder(room: String, conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>) -> impl IntoView {
119	    let label = move || match conn.get() {
120	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting‚Ä¶".to_string(),
121	        ConnState::Open => "Waiting for server greeting‚Ä¶".to_string(),
122	        ConnState::Closed => "Disconnected ‚ÄĒ refresh to reconnect.".to_string(),
123	        ConnState::Error => "Connection error ‚ÄĒ is the server running?".to_string(),
124	    };
125	    view! {
126	        <div class="conn-placeholder">
127	            <p class="muted">{format!("Room: {}", room)}</p>
128	            <p>{label}</p>
129	        </div>
130	    }
131	}
132	
133	#[component]
134	fn BoardWrapper(
135	    view_signal: ReadSignal<Option<PlayerView>>,
136	    role: ReadSignal<Option<ClientRole>>,
137	    handle: WsHandle,
138	) -> impl IntoView {
139	    let view = Signal::derive(move || view_signal.get().expect("guarded by Show"));
140	    let resolved_role = role.get_untracked().expect("guarded by Show");
141	    let obs = resolved_role.observer();
142	    let shape = view.with_untracked(|v| v.shape);
143	
144	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
145	    // Spectators see-only ‚ÄĒ no click handlers on the board for them.
146	    let is_spectator = resolved_role.is_spectator();
147	
148	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
149	        if is_spectator {
150	            return;
151	        }
152	        let v = view.get();
153	        if v.observer != v.side_to_move {
154	            return;
155	        }
156	        let cur = selected.get();
157	        if cur == Some(sq) {
158	            selected.set(None);
159	            return;
160	        }
161	        if let Some(from) = cur {
162	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
163	                handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
164	                selected.set(None);
165	                return;
166	            }
167	        }
168	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
169	        match cell {
170	            VisibleCell::Revealed(p) if p.piece.side == v.side_to_move => {
171	                selected.set(Some(sq));
172	            }
173	            VisibleCell::Hidden => {
174	                if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
175	                    handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
176	                    selected.set(None);
177	                }
178	            }
179	            _ => selected.set(None),
180	        }
181	    });
182	
183	    view! {
184	        <Board
185	            shape=shape
186	            observer=obs
187	            view=view
188	            selected=selected
189	            on_click=on_click
190	        />
191	    }
192	}
193	
194	#[component]
195	fn OnlineSidebar(
196	    room: String,
197	    role: ReadSignal<Option<ClientRole>>,
198	    view_signal: ReadSignal<Option<PlayerView>>,
199	    rules: ReadSignal<Option<RuleSet>>,
200	    conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>,
201	    handle: WsHandle,
202	) -> impl IntoView {
203	    let role_label = move || match role.get() {
204	        Some(ClientRole::Player(Side::RED)) => "You play Red ÁīÖ".to_string(),
205	        Some(ClientRole::Player(Side::BLACK)) => "You play Black ťĽĎ".to_string(),
206	        Some(ClientRole::Player(_)) => "You play Green Á∂†".to_string(),
207	        Some(ClientRole::Spectator) => "Spectator ‚ÄĒ read-only".to_string(),
208	        None => "Awaiting seat assignment".to_string(),
209	    };
210	    let role_class = move || match role.get() {
211	        Some(ClientRole::Spectator) => "spectator-badge",
212	        _ => "",
213	    };
214	
215	    let variant_label = move || match rules.get() {
216	        Some(r) => variant_slug(r.variant).to_string(),
217	        None => "‚ÄĒ".to_string(),
218	    };
219	
220	    let turn_label = move || match view_signal.get() {
221	        Some(v) => match v.side_to_move {
222	            Side::RED => "Red ÁīÖ to move",
223	            Side::BLACK => "Black ťĽĎ to move",
224	            _ => "Green Á∂† to move",
225	        }
226	        .to_string(),
227	        None => "‚ÄĒ".to_string(),
228	    };
229	
230	    let game_over = move || {
231	        matches!(
232	            view_signal.get().map(|v| v.status),
233	            Some(GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. })
234	        )
235	    };
236	    let is_player = move || role.get().map(|r| r.is_player()).unwrap_or(false);
237	    let resign_disabled = move || !is_player() || game_over();
238	    let rematch_disabled = move || !is_player() || !game_over();
239	
240	    let conn_label = move || match conn.get() {
241	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting‚Ä¶",
242	        ConnState::Open => "Connected",
243	        ConnState::Closed => "Disconnected",
244	        ConnState::Error => "Error",
245	    };
246	
247	    let resign = {
248	        let handle = handle.clone();
249	        move |_| {
250	            handle.send(ClientMsg::Resign);
251	        }
252	    };
253	    let rematch = {
254	        let handle = handle.clone();
255	        move |_| {
256	            handle.send(ClientMsg::Rematch);
257	        }
258	    };
259	
260	    view! {
261	        <aside class="sidebar">
262	            <h3 class="variant-label">{format!("Online ‚ÄĒ room {}", room)}</h3>
263	            <p class="muted">{variant_label}</p>
264	            <p class=role_class>{role_label}</p>
265	            <p>{turn_label}</p>
266	            <p class="muted">{conn_label}</p>
267	            <div class="sidebar-actions">
268	                <button class="btn" on:click=resign disabled=resign_disabled>"Resign"</button>
269	                <button class="btn" on:click=rematch disabled=rematch_disabled>"Rematch"</button>
270	            </div>
271	            <a class="back-link" href="/lobby">"‚Üź Back to lobby"</a>
272	        </aside>
273	    }
274	}
275

8	#[component]
9	pub fn LobbyPage() -> impl IntoView {
10	    let (rooms, set_rooms) = create_signal::<Vec<RoomSummary>>(vec![]);
11	    let (room_input, set_room_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
12	    let (password_input, set_password_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
13	
14	    let (handle, incoming, conn) = connect(lobby_url());
15	
16	    // Fan ServerMsg::Rooms into the rooms signal.
17	    create_effect(move |_| {
18	        if let Some(ServerMsg::Rooms { rooms: list }) = incoming.get() {
19	            set_rooms.set(list);
20	        }
21	    });
22	
23	    let refresh_handle = handle.clone();
24	    let refresh = move |_| {
25	        refresh_handle.send(ClientMsg::ListRooms);
26	    };
27	
28	    let navigate = use_navigate();
29	    let nav_for_join = navigate.clone();
30	    let go_to_room: Callback<String> = Callback::new(move |id: String| {
31	        let pw = password_input.get_untracked();
32	        let target = if pw.is_empty() {
33	            format!("/play/{}", id)
34	        } else {
35	            format!("/play/{}?password={}", id, urlencode(&pw))
36	        };
37	        nav_for_join(&target, Default::default());
38	    });
39	
40	    let nav_for_watch = navigate;
41	    let watch_room: Callback<String> = Callback::new(move |id: String| {
42	        let pw = password_input.get_untracked();
43	        let qs = if pw.is_empty() {
44	            "role=spectator".to_string()
45	        } else {
46	            format!("role=spectator&password={}", urlencode(&pw))
47	        };
48	        nav_for_watch(&format!("/play/{}?{}", id, qs), Default::default());
49	    });
50	
51	    let create_or_join = move |_| {
52	        let id = room_input.get_untracked().trim().to_string();
53	        if id.is_empty() {
54	            return;
55	        }
56	        go_to_room.call(id);
57	    };
58	
59	    view! {
60	        <section class="lobby">
61	            <a href="/" class="back-link">"‚Üź Back to picker"</a>
62	            <h2>"Online lobby"</h2>
63	            <p class="subtitle">"Pick an existing room or create a new one. The server's variant + house rules apply to every room it hosts."</p>
64	
65	            <ConnBanner conn=conn/>
66	
67	            <div class="lobby-form">
68	                <input
69	                    class="text-input"
70	                    type="text"
71	                    placeholder="room name (e.g. 'a-friendly-game')"
72	                    prop:value=room_input
73	                    on:input=move |ev| set_room_input.set(event_target_value(&ev))
74	                />
75	                <input
76	                    class="text-input"
77	                    type="password"
78	                    placeholder="password (optional)"
79	                    prop:value=password_input
80	                    on:input=move |ev| set_password_input.set(event_target_value(&ev))
81	                />
82	                <button class="btn btn-primary" on:click=create_or_join>
83	                    "Create / Join"
84	                </button>
85	                <button class="btn" on:click=refresh>"Refresh"</button>
86	            </div>
87	
88	            <h3>"Active rooms"</h3>
89	            <Show
90	                when=move || !rooms.get().is_empty()
91	                fallback=|| view! { <p class="muted">"No rooms yet. Create one above."</p> }
92	            >
93	                <ul class="rooms-list">
94	                    <For
95	                        each=move || rooms.get()
96	                        key=|r| format!(
97	                            "{}|{}|{}|{:?}",
98	                            r.id,
99	                            r.seats,
100	                            r.spectators,
101	                            r.status
102	                        )
103	                        children=move |room| {
104	                            let id_join = room.id.clone();
105	                            let id_watch = room.id.clone();
106	                            let seats_label = if room.spectators > 0 {
107	                                format!("{}/2 seats ¬∑ {} ūüĎĀ", room.seats, room.spectators)
108	                            } else {
109	                                format!("{}/2 seats", room.seats)
110	                            };
111	                            let join_disabled = room.seats >= 2;
112	                            view! {
113	                                <li class="room-row">
114	                                    <span class="room-id">{room.id.clone()}</span>
115	                                    <span class="room-variant">{room.variant.clone()}</span>
116	                                    <span class="room-seats">{seats_label}</span>
117	                                    {if room.has_password {
118	                                        view! { <span class="room-lock" title="password protected">"ūüĒí"</span> }.into_view()
119	                                    } else { ().into_view() }}

121	                                    <span class="room-actions">
122	                                        <button

146	#[component]
147	fn ConnBanner(conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>) -> impl IntoView {
148	    let label = move || match conn.get() {
149	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting‚Ä¶",
150	        ConnState::Open => "Connected",
151	        ConnState::Closed => "Disconnected ‚ÄĒ refresh to reconnect.",
152	        ConnState::Error => "Connection error ‚ÄĒ is the server running?",
153	    };
154	    let cls = move || match conn.get() {

156	        ConnState::Connecting => "conn-banner pending",
157	        _ => "conn-banner error",
158	    };
159	    view! { <p class=cls>{label}</p> }
160	}

162	fn room_status_label(s: RoomStatus) -> &'static str {

173	        RoomStatus::Playing => "room-status playing",
174	        RoomStatus::Finished => "room-status finished",
175	    }
176	}

27	pub fn draw(frame: &mut Frame, app: &mut AppState) {
28	    let area = frame.area();
29	    match &app.screen {
30	        Screen::Picker(p) => draw_picker(frame, area, p),
31	        Screen::Game(_) => draw_game(frame, area, app),
32	        Screen::Net(_) => draw_net(frame, area, app),
33	        Screen::HostPrompt(h) => draw_host_prompt(frame, area, h, app.help_open),
34	        Screen::Lobby(l) => draw_lobby(frame, area, l, app.help_open),
35	        Screen::CreateRoom(c) => draw_create_room(frame, area, c),
36	    }
37	
38	    if app.rules_open {
39	        draw_rules_overlay(frame, area, app.style);
40	    }
41	    if app.quit_confirm_open {
42	        draw_quit_confirm_overlay(frame, area);
43	    }
44	}

46	fn draw_picker(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, picker: &PickerView) {
47	    let title = Span::styled("chess-tui", TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD));
48	    let mut lines = vec![
49	        Line::from(title),
50	        Line::from(Span::raw("Choose a variant. Arrow keys + Enter; q to quit.")),
51	        Line::from(""),
52	    ];
53	    for (i, entry) in PickerEntry::ALL.iter().enumerate() {
54	        let prefix = if i == picker.cursor { "‚Ė∂ " } else { "  " };
55	        let style = if i == picker.cursor {
56	            TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)
57	        } else {
58	            TuiStyle::default()
59	        };
60	        lines.push(Line::from(vec![Span::raw(prefix), Span::styled(entry.label(), style)]));
61	    }
62	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Welcome ");
63	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).block(block).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
64	    frame.render_widget(para, area);
65	}
66	
67	fn draw_game(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &mut AppState) {
68	    let chunks = Layout::default()
69	        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
70	        .constraints([Constraint::Min(40), Constraint::Length(36)])
71	        .split(area);
72	
73	    let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen else {
74	        return;
75	    };
76	    let g_ref: &GameView = g.as_ref();

80	    let help_open = app.help_open;
81	
82	    let view = PlayerView::project(&g_ref.state, g_ref.state.side_to_move);
83	    let mut rect = app.board_rect;

88	        style,
89	        use_color,
90	        &view,

92	        g_ref.selected,
93	        &mut rect,
94	    );
95	    app.board_rect = rect;
96	    draw_sidebar(frame, chunks[1], g_ref, &view, style, help_open);
97	}

### 1. **Is "side X is in check" a public API?**

**YES ‚ÄĒ it's fully public.**

- **Location:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:232‚Äď234`

- **Details:** The check detector is **independent of the `xiangqi_allow_self_check` flag**‚ÄĒit's a pure state query that tests whether a general is attacked OR generals face each other on a clear file. It doesn't validate legality; it only reports truth. UIs can unconditionally call `state.is_in_check(side)` to render the "ŚįáŤĽć" banner in both standard and casual modes.
- **Banqi:** Confirmed‚ÄĒbanqi has **no check** concept (returns `false` for missing general per line 262).

### 2. **GameStatus / WinReason enums**

**Location:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:20‚Äď46`

- **GameStatus variants:**
  - `Ongoing`
  - `Won { winner: Side, reason: WinReason }`
  - `Drawn { reason: DrawReason }`

- **WinReason variants:**
  - `Checkmate` ‚ÄĒ no legal moves + general in check
  - `Stalemate` ‚ÄĒ no legal moves + general not in check (xiangqi only)
  - `Resignation`
  - `OnlyOneSideHasPieces` ‚ÄĒ banqi end state
  - `Timeout`
  - `GeneralCaptured` ‚ÄĒ casual xiangqi only (general physically captured)

- **DrawReason variants:**
  - `NoProgress` ‚ÄĒ 60 plies no capture/reveal
  - `Repetition` ‚ÄĒ threefold (not yet implemented)
  - `Agreed`
  - `InsufficientMaterial`

### 3. **PlayerView includes status?**

**YES ‚ÄĒ fully exposed over the network.**

- **Location:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/view.rs:25‚Äď35`
- **Field:** `pub status: GameStatus` (line 32)
- **Serializable:** `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]` on `PlayerView`
- The `PlayerView::project()` method (line 40) copies the full `status` from the internal state (line 66). Network clients receive the same end-game trigger signals as local play.

### 4. **Check vs casual mode interaction**

**Confirmed: `is_in_check` is independent; works identically in both modes.**

- **Standard mode** (`xiangqi_allow_self_check: false`): Legality filter rejects moves exposing the general; checkmate ends the game (`WinReason::Checkmate`).
- **Casual mode** (`xiangqi_allow_self_check: true`): Legality filter skipped (line 23‚Äď27 in xiangqi.rs). Pseudo-legal moves become legal. Game ends only when general is physically captured (`WinReason::GeneralCaptured` per line 260 in state/mod.rs).
- **In both modes:** `is_in_check(side)` returns the same truth value‚ÄĒit's called identically in `refresh_status` at line 275 to determine Checkmate vs Stalemate.

Test confirms (line 39‚Äď84 in casual_xiangqi.rs): a contrived casual position with the general capturable correctly reports `GeneralCaptured` after `refresh_status`.

**Recommendation:** Reuse the existing `GameState::is_in_check(side: Side) -> bool` public method. No new wrapper needed‚ÄĒit's already positioned correctly for UI use.

86	        </aside>

1	//! SVG board renderer. Variant-agnostic: takes a `PlayerView` + observer +
2	//! a click callback, and lays cells on the intersection grid that the TUI
3	//! also uses (see `crate::orient`).
4	
5	use chess_core::board::BoardShape;
6	use chess_core::coord::Square;
7	use chess_core::piece::Side;
8	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
9	use leptos::*;
10	
11	use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
12	use crate::orient::{display_dims, square_at_display};
13	use crate::state::legal_targets;
14	
15	pub const CELL: i32 = 60;
16	pub const PAD: i32 = 30;
17	const RIVER_TOP_ROW: u8 = 4;
18	const RIVER_BOT_ROW: u8 = 5;
19	const TILE_RADIUS: i32 = 24;
20	
21	#[component]
22	pub fn Board(
23	    shape: BoardShape,
24	    observer: Side,
25	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
26	    #[prop(into)] selected: Signal<Option<Square>>,
27	    #[prop(into)] on_click: Callback<Square>,
28	) -> impl IntoView {
29	    let (rows, cols) = display_dims(shape);
30	    let view_w = (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
31	    let view_h = (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL + PAD * 2;
32	    let viewbox = format!("0 0 {} {}", view_w, view_h);
33	    let style = Style::Cjk;
34	
35	    view! {
36	        <svg class="board" viewBox=viewbox preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet">
37	            <rect class="board-bg" x="0" y="0" width="100%" height="100%"/>
38	            <g class="grid-layer">{grid_lines(shape, rows, cols)}</g>
39	            <g class="river-layer">{river_text(shape, cols)}</g>
40	            <g class="palace-layer">{palace_diagonals(shape)}</g>
41	            <g class="overlay-layer">
42	                {move || selection_marker(selected.get(), observer, shape)}
43	                {move || move_dots_view(&view.get(), selected.get(), observer, shape)}
44	            </g>
45	            <g class="pieces-layer">
46	                {move || pieces_view(&view.get(), observer, shape, style)}
47	            </g>
48	            <g class="cells-layer">
49	                {hit_cells(rows, cols, observer, shape, on_click)}
50	            </g>
51	        </svg>
52	    }
53	}
54	
55	#[inline]
56	fn intersection(row: u8, col: u8) -> (i32, i32) {
57	    (PAD + col as i32 * CELL, PAD + row as i32 * CELL)
58	}
59	
60	fn grid_lines(shape: BoardShape, rows: u8, cols: u8) -> View {
61	    let is_xiangqi = matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10);
62	    let mut out: Vec<View> = Vec::with_capacity(rows as usize + cols as usize * 2);
63	    for r in 0..rows {
64	        let y = PAD + r as i32 * CELL;
65	        let x1 = PAD;
66	        let x2 = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL;
67	        out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x1 y1=y x2=x2 y2=y/> }.into_view());
68	    }
69	    for c in 0..cols {
70	        let x = PAD + c as i32 * CELL;
71	        let is_border = c == 0 || c == cols - 1;
72	        let y_top = PAD;
73	        let y_bot = PAD + (rows as i32 - 1) * CELL;
74	        if is_xiangqi && !is_border {
75	            let y_river_top = PAD + RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL;
76	            let y_river_bot = PAD + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL;
77	            out.push(
78	                view! {
79	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_river_top/>
80	                    <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_river_bot x2=x y2=y_bot/>
81	                }
82	                .into_view(),
83	            );
84	        } else {
85	            out.push(view! { <line class="grid" x1=x y1=y_top x2=x y2=y_bot/> }.into_view());
86	        }
87	    }
88	    out.into_view()
89	}
90	
91	fn river_text(shape: BoardShape, cols: u8) -> View {
92	    if !matches!(shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
93	        return ().into_view();
94	    }
95	    let cx = PAD + (cols as i32 - 1) * CELL / 2;
96	    let cy = PAD + (RIVER_TOP_ROW as i32 * CELL + RIVER_BOT_ROW as i32 * CELL) / 2;
97	    view! {
98	        <text class="river-text" x=cx y=cy text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="central">
99	            "ś•ö ś≤≥ śľĘ ÁēĆ"
100	        </text>
```

1	//! Per-player view projection.
2	//!
3	//! `PlayerView` is the only state the network layer should serialize and
4	//! ship to clients ‚ÄĒ it has hidden-piece identities scrubbed and (for
5	//! non-side-to-move observers) no legal-move list to leak strategy.
6	//!
7	//! See ADR-0004.
8	
9	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
10	
11	use crate::board::BoardShape;
12	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
13	use crate::piece::{PieceOnSquare, Side};
14	use crate::state::{GameState, GameStatus};
15	
16	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
17	pub enum VisibleCell {
18	    Empty,
19	    /// Banqi face-down: identity hidden from this observer.
20	    Hidden,
21	    Revealed(PieceOnSquare),
22	}
23	
24	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
25	pub struct PlayerView {
26	    pub observer: Side,
27	    pub shape: BoardShape,
28	    pub width: u8,
29	    pub height: u8,
30	    pub cells: Vec<VisibleCell>,
31	    pub side_to_move: Side,
32	    pub status: GameStatus,
33	    /// Legal moves for the observer if it's their turn; empty otherwise.
34	    pub legal_moves: MoveList,
35	}
36	
37	impl PlayerView {
38	    /// Project `state` from `observer`'s vantage point. Hidden pieces stay
39	    /// hidden; for opponents, legal-move list is empty.
40	    pub fn project(state: &GameState, observer: Side) -> Self {
41	        let board = &state.board;
42	        let cells: Vec<VisibleCell> = (0..board.cell_count())
43	            .map(|i| {
44	                let sq = crate::coord::Square(i as u16);
45	                match board.get(sq) {
46	                    None => VisibleCell::Empty,
47	                    Some(pos) if pos.revealed => VisibleCell::Revealed(pos),
48	                    Some(_) => VisibleCell::Hidden,
49	                }
50	            })
51	            .collect();
52	
53	        let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move {
54	            sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
55	        } else {
56	            MoveList::new()
57	        };
58	
59	        Self {
60	            observer,
61	            shape: board.shape(),
62	            width: board.width(),
63	            height: board.height(),
64	            cells,
65	            side_to_move: state.side_to_move,
66	            status: state.status,
67	            legal_moves,
68	        }
69	    }
70	}
71	
72	/// Strip identity from `Reveal` moves before they reach the network.
73	/// Even the side-to-move sees `revealed: None` because the engine resolves
74	/// the identity authoritatively when the move is applied.
75	fn sanitize_for_observer(moves: MoveList, _observer: Side) -> MoveList {
76	    moves
77	        .into_iter()
78	        .map(|m| match m {
79	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: _ } => Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None },
80	            other => other,
81	        })
82	        .collect()
83	}
84	
85	#[cfg(test)]
86	mod tests {
87	    use super::*;
88	    use crate::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
89	
90	    #[test]
91	    fn fresh_banqi_view_has_all_hidden_for_any_observer() {
92	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 7));
93	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
94	        assert_eq!(view.cells.len(), 32);
95	        assert!(view.cells.iter().all(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Hidden)));
96	    }
97	
98	    #[test]
99	    fn xiangqi_view_shows_revealed_pieces() {
100	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
101	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
102	        let revealed: Vec<_> =
103	            view.cells.iter().filter(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))).collect();
104	        assert_eq!(revealed.len(), 32);
105	    }
106	
107	    #[test]
108	    fn opponent_view_has_empty_legal_moves() {
109	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
110	        let red_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
111	        let black_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
112	        assert!(!red_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "side-to-move sees moves");
113	        assert!(black_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "opponent doesn't");
114	    }
115	
116	    #[test]
117	    fn reveal_moves_have_no_identity_in_view() {
118	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 1));
119	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
120	        for m in &view.legal_moves {
121	            if let Move::Reveal { revealed, .. } = m {
122	                assert!(revealed.is_none(), "Reveal payload must be stripped in view");
123	            }
124	        }
125	    }
126	
127	    #[test]
128	    fn no_hidden_identity_in_serialized_view() {
129	        // The smoke-level version of the proptest in tests/view_projection.rs.
130	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 42));
131	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
132	        let json = serde_json::to_string(&view).unwrap();
133	        // None of the piece-kind names should appear in any cell ‚ÄĒ they're all hidden.
134	        // (Empty cells trivially never contain a kind; we just confirm the doc-level
135	        // invariant.)
136	        for kind_name in ["General", "Advisor", "Elephant", "Chariot", "Horse", "Cannon", "Soldier"]
137	        {
138	            assert!(
139	                !json.contains(kind_name),
140	                "fresh banqi view must not leak any piece kind ({kind_name})"
141	            );
142	        }
143	    }
144	}
145

1	use chess_core::coord::Square;
2	use chess_core::piece::Side;
3	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
4	use chess_core::state::GameState;
5	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};

13	
14	#[component]
15	pub fn LocalPage() -> impl IntoView {
16	    let params = use_params_map();
17	    let query = use_query_map();
18	    let resolved = move || -> Result<(Variant, RuleSet, bool), String> {
19	        let slug = params.with(|p| p.get("variant").cloned().unwrap_or_default());
20	        let variant =
21	            parse_variant_slug(&slug).ok_or_else(|| format!("Unknown variant: {}", slug))?;
22	        let parsed = query.with(|q| parse_local_rules(|k| q.get(k).cloned()));
23	        let rules = build_rule_set(variant, &parsed);
24	        // Three-kingdom: engine ships an empty 4√ó8 board. Render it but
25	        // disable interaction and overlay a "WIP" banner ‚ÄĒ see
26	        // `backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md` and the gotcha in CLAUDE.md.
27	        let wip = matches!(variant, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi);
28	        Ok((variant, rules, wip))
29	    };
30	
31	    move || match resolved() {
32	        Ok((variant, rules, wip)) => {
33	            view! { <LocalGame variant=variant rules=rules wip=wip/> }.into_view()
34	        }
35	        Err(msg) => view! {
36	            <section class="game-page game-page--single">
37	                <div>
38	                    <a href="/" class="back-link">"‚Üź Back to picker"</a>
39	                    <h2>"Variant unavailable"</h2>
40	                    <p class="subtitle">{msg}</p>
41	                </div>
42	            </section>
43	        }
44	        .into_view(),
45	    }
46	}
47	
48	#[component]
49	fn LocalGame(variant: Variant, rules: RuleSet, wip: bool) -> impl IntoView {
50	    let initial_rules = rules.clone();
51	    let state = create_rw_signal(GameState::new(rules));
52	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
53	
54	    // Local pass-and-play renders from a fixed Red-side observer (board never
55	    // flips on turn change). Legal moves come from the projection for the
56	    // *current* side-to-move so click-to-move uses the right list.
57	    let observer = Side::RED;
58	    let shape = state.with_untracked(|s| s.board.shape());
59	
60	    let view = create_memo(move |_| state.with(|s| PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)));
61	
62	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
63	        if wip {
64	            return;
65	        }
66	        let v = view.get();
67	        let cur = selected.get();
68	
69	        if cur == Some(sq) {
70	            selected.set(None);
71	            return;
72	        }
73	
74	        if let Some(from) = cur {
75	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
76	                state.update(|s| {
77	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal move from view");
78	                    s.refresh_status();
79	                });
80	                selected.set(None);

```text
1	//! Pure-logic helpers for the client's view of a game. Native-testable ‚ÄĒ
2	//! no Leptos signals or browser deps live here.
3	
4	use chess_core::coord::Square;
5	use chess_core::moves::Move;
6	use chess_core::piece::Side;
7	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
8	
9	/// Role assigned by the server on `Hello` (player) or `Spectating`
10	/// (read-only). Drives whether the play page renders move/resign/rematch
11	/// affordances and whether the chat input is enabled.
12	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
13	pub enum ClientRole {
14	    Player(Side),
15	    Spectator,
16	}
17	
18	impl ClientRole {
19	    pub fn is_player(self) -> bool {
20	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Player(_))
21	    }
22	
23	    pub fn is_spectator(self) -> bool {
24	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Spectator)
25	    }
26	
27	    pub fn observer(self) -> Side {
28	        // Spectators render from RED's POV ‚ÄĒ matches what chess-net's
29	        // `broadcast_update` projects for spectator updates.
30	        match self {
31	            ClientRole::Player(s) => s,
32	            ClientRole::Spectator => Side::RED,
33	        }
34	    }
35	}
36	
37	/// Trim a vec from the front so it holds at most `max` entries. Used for
38	/// the per-page chat ring buffer that mirrors the server's 50-line cap.
39	pub fn truncate_front<T>(buf: &mut Vec<T>, max: usize) {
40	    if buf.len() > max {
41	        let drop_count = buf.len() - max;
42	        buf.drain(0..drop_count);
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// Find the legal `Move` (if any) whose origin is `from` and whose final
47	/// destination is `to`. Reveal moves match when `from == to == at`.
48	pub fn find_move(view: &PlayerView, from: Square, to: Square) -> Option<Move> {
49	    view.legal_moves.iter().find(|m| matches_endpoints(m, from, to)).cloned()
50	}
51	
52	fn matches_endpoints(mv: &Move, from: Square, to: Square) -> bool {
53	    if mv.origin_square() != from {
54	        return false;
55	    }
56	    match mv.to_square() {
57	        Some(t) => t == to,
58	        None => matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { at, .. } if *at == to),
59	    }
60	}
```

56	pub struct GameState {
57	    pub rules: RuleSet,
58	    pub board: Board,
59	    pub side_to_move: Side,
60	    pub turn_order: TurnOrder,
61	    pub history: Vec<MoveRecord>,
62	    pub status: GameStatus,
63	    /// Banqi only: established after first flip. `None` until then.
64	    pub side_assignment: Option<SideAssignment>,
65	    pub no_progress_plies: u16,
66	}

68	impl GameState {
69	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
70	        crate::setup::build_initial_state(rules)
71	    }
72	
73	    /// Generate all legal moves for the side to move.
74	    pub fn legal_moves(&self) -> MoveList {
75	        let mut out = MoveList::new();
76	        crate::rules::generate_moves(self, &mut out);
77	        out
78	    }
79	
80	    /// Apply a move. Caller is responsible for legality (use `legal_moves`).
81	    /// Returns the move pushed to history (with `Reveal` payloads filled in).
82	    pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
83	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
84	            return Err(CoreError::GameOver);
85	        }
86	        let mover = self.side_to_move;
87	        let no_progress_before = self.no_progress_plies;
88	
89	        // Normalize the move (fill in Reveal payload from board if missing).
90	        let normalized = self.normalize_for_apply(m)?;
91	        self.apply_inner(&normalized)?;
92	
93	        self.history.push(MoveRecord { mover, the_move: normalized.clone(), no_progress_before });
94	
95	        if normalized.resets_no_progress() {
96	            self.no_progress_plies = 0;
97	        } else {
98	            self.no_progress_plies = self.no_progress_plies.saturating_add(1);
99	        }
100	
101	        self.turn_order.advance();
102	        self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
103	
104	        Ok(())
105	    }
106	
107	    /// Undo the last move.
108	    pub fn unmake_move(&mut self) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
109	        let rec = self.history.pop().ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("no move to undo"))?;
110	        self.unapply_inner(&rec.the_move)?;
111	        self.no_progress_plies = rec.no_progress_before;
112	        // Rewind turn order
113	        if self.turn_order.current == 0 {
114	            self.turn_order.current = (self.turn_order.seats.len() as u8) - 1;
115	        } else {
116	            self.turn_order.current -= 1;
117	        }
118	        self.side_to_move = self.turn_order.current_side();
119	        // Game status reset to Ongoing ‚ÄĒ caller may recompute.
120	        self.status = GameStatus::Ongoing;
121	        Ok(())
122	    }
123	
124	    fn normalize_for_apply(&self, m: &Move) -> Result<Move, CoreError> {
125	        match m {
126	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None } => {
127	                let pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
128	                if pos.revealed {
129	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
130	                }
131	                Ok(Move::Reveal { at: *at, revealed: Some(pos.piece) })
132	            }
133	            _ => Ok(m.clone()),
134	        }
135	    }
136	
137	    fn apply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
138	        match m {
139	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed } => {
140	                let mut pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
141	                if pos.revealed {
142	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
143	                }
144	                pos.revealed = true;
145	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
146	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment.
147	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
148	                    if let Some(p) = revealed {
149	                        self.side_assignment =
150	                            Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, p.side));
151	                    }
152	                }
153	            }
154	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
155	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Step: empty from"))?;
156	                if self.board.get(*to).is_some() {
157	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Step: occupied to"));
158	                }
159	                self.board.set(*from, None);
160	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
161	            }
162	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured: _ } => {
163	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Capture: empty from"))?;
164	                self.board.set(*from, None);
165	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
166	            }
167	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured: _ } => {
168	                let p =
169	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("CannonJump: empty from"))?;
170	                self.board.set(*from, None);
171	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
172	            }
173	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
174	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty from"))?;
175	                if path.is_empty() {
176	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty path"));
177	                }
178	                self.board.set(*from, None);
179	                for hop in &path[..path.len() - 1] {
180	                    self.board.set(hop.to, None);
181	                }
182	                self.board.set(path.last().unwrap().to, Some(p));
183	            }
184	        }
185	        Ok(())
186	    }
187	
188	    fn unapply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
189	        match m {
190	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => {
191	                let mut pos =
192	                    self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Reveal: empty"))?;
193	                pos.revealed = false;
194	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
195	                // First-flip might have set side_assignment; if this was the first move, clear it.
196	                if self.history.is_empty() {
197	                    self.side_assignment = None;
198	                }
199	            }
200	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
201	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Step: empty to"))?;
202	                self.board.set(*to, None);
203	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
204	            }
205	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } => {
206	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Capture: empty to"))?;
207	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
208	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
209	            }
210	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured } => {
211	                let p = self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Jump: empty to"))?;
212	                self.board.set(*to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(*captured)));
213	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
214	            }
215	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
216	                let last_to = path.last().unwrap().to;
217	                let p =
218	                    self.board.get(last_to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Undo Chain: empty last"))?;
219	                self.board.set(last_to, None);
220	                // Restore intermediate captures (in any order).
221	                for hop in path {
222	                    self.board.set(hop.to, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(hop.captured)));
223	                }
224	                self.board.set(*from, Some(p));
225	            }
226	        }
227	        Ok(())
228	    }
229	
230	    /// Whether `side`'s general is currently under attack by the opponent.
231	    /// Xiangqi-specific; banqi has no general-in-check concept.
232	    pub fn is_in_check(&self, side: Side) -> bool {
233	        crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check(self, side)
234	    }
235	
236	    /// Recompute `status` based on legal moves and draw counters.
237	    /// Caller invokes this after `make_move`; we don't auto-call to avoid
238	    /// recursing into move generation during legality filtering.
239	    ///
240	    /// TODO (PR 2): threefold repetition. Currently only no-progress and
241	    /// no-legal-moves are detected.
242	    pub fn refresh_status(&mut self) {
243	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
244	            return;
245	        }
246	        // Casual xiangqi: a missing general means the opposite side won.
247	        // Standard rules can't reach this branch (the legality filter
248	        // forbids leaving the general capturable), but checking unconditionally
249	        // is cheap and keeps the invariant for any future variant that allows
250	        // physical king capture.
251	        if self.rules.variant == Variant::Xiangqi {
252	            for &seat in self.turn_order.seats.iter() {
253	                let has_general = find_piece(&self.board, |p| {
254	                    p.kind == crate::piece::PieceKind::General && p.side == seat
255	                })
256	                .is_some();
257	                if !has_general {
258	                    self.status = GameStatus::Won {
259	                        winner: seat.opposite(),
260	                        reason: WinReason::GeneralCaptured,
261	                    };
262	                    return;
263	                }
264	            }
265	        }
266	        if self.no_progress_plies >= self.rules.draw_policy.no_progress_plies {
267	            self.status = GameStatus::Drawn { reason: DrawReason::NoProgress };
268	            return;
269	        }
270	        let moves = self.legal_moves();
271	        if moves.is_empty() {
272	            let me = self.side_to_move;
273	            self.status = match self.rules.variant {
274	                Variant::Xiangqi => {
275	                    let reason = if self.is_in_check(me) {
276	                        WinReason::Checkmate
277	                    } else {
278	                        // Asian rules: stalemate is a loss for the stalemated side.
279	                        WinReason::Stalemate
280	                    };
281	                    GameStatus::Won { winner: me.opposite(), reason }
282	                }
283	                Variant::Banqi => GameStatus::Won {
284	                    winner: me.opposite(),
285	                    reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
286	                },
287	                Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {
288	                    // PR 2: more nuanced 3-side win/elimination logic.
289	                    GameStatus::Won {
290	                        winner: me.opposite(),
291	                        reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
292	                    }
293	                }
294	            };
295	        }
296	    }
297	
298	    pub fn default_starter(variant: Variant) -> Side {
299	        match variant {
300	            Variant::Xiangqi => Side::RED,
301	            Variant::Banqi => Side::RED,
302	            Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => Side(0),
303	        }
304	    }
305	}
306	
307	fn banqi_side_assignment(flipper: Side, revealed_side: Side) -> SideAssignment {
308	    let mut mapping = SmallVec::new();
309	    if flipper == Side::RED {
310	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
311	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
312	    } else {
313	        mapping.push(revealed_side.opposite());
314	        mapping.push(revealed_side);
315	    }
316	    SideAssignment { mapping }
317	}
318	
319	/// Find the (single) piece matching predicate. Used to locate a general.
320	pub(crate) fn find_piece<F>(board: &Board, pred: F) -> Option<crate::coord::Square>
321	where
322	    F: Fn(Piece) -> bool,
323	{
324	    for sq in board.squares() {
325	        if let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) {
326	            if pos.revealed && pred(pos.piece) {
327	                return Some(sq);
328	            }
329	        }
330	    }
331	    None
332	}
333

340	    fn opening_xiangqi_status_ongoing_after_refresh() {
341	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
342	        state.refresh_status();
343	        assert_eq!(state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing);
344	    }
345	
346	    #[test]
347	    fn no_progress_plies_advance_then_reset_on_capture() {
348	        // Synthetic: build a tiny xiangqi position where Red can capture.
349	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
350	        // Skim a move that doesn't capture: cannon h2 forward.
351	        let m = state.legal_moves().into_iter().find(|m| matches!(m, Move::Step { .. })).unwrap();
352	        state.make_move(&m).unwrap();

354	    }

359	        state.no_progress_plies = 5;

1	//! Standard xiangqi move generation.
2	//!
3	//! Pipeline: `pseudo_legal_moves` (geometry only) ‚Üí filter by self-check
4	//! (uses make/unmake on the actual state for fidelity).
5	
6	use smallvec::SmallVec;
7	
8	use crate::board::{Board, RegionKind};
9	use crate::coord::{Direction, File, Rank, Square};
10	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
11	use crate::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, Side};
12	use crate::state::GameState;
13	
14	/// Top-level: legal moves only.
15	pub fn generate(state: &GameState, out: &mut MoveList) {
16	    let mut pseudo: MoveList = MoveList::new();
17	    pseudo_legal_moves(state, &mut pseudo);
18	
19	    // Casual mode: skip the self-check legality filter. All pseudo-legal
20	    // moves are accepted; the player can move into check or expose their
21	    // general, and the game ends only when the general is actually captured
22	    // (handled by GameState::refresh_status).
23	    if state.rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check {
24	        for m in pseudo.into_iter() {
25	            out.push(m);
26	        }
27	        return;
28	    }
29	
30	    // Standard rules: clone-and-apply approach for legality. Cheap enough
31	    // for 9√ó10 and trivially correct; AI hot path can switch to make/unmake
32	    // later.
33	    for m in pseudo.into_iter() {
34	        let mut probe = state.clone();
35	        if probe.make_move(&m).is_err() {
36	            continue;
37	        }
38	        // After the move, side_to_move has flipped; the side that just
39	        // moved is the one whose general must not be in check.
40	        let mover = state.side_to_move;
41	        if !is_in_check(&probe, mover) {
42	            out.push(m);
43	        }
44	    }
45	}
46	
47	pub fn pseudo_legal_moves(state: &GameState, out: &mut MoveList) {
48	    let board = &state.board;
49	    let me = state.side_to_move;
50	    for sq in board.squares() {
51	        let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) else { continue };
52	        if pos.piece.side != me {
53	            continue;
54	        }
55	        gen_for_piece(board, sq, pos.piece, out);
56	    }
57	}
58	
59	fn gen_for_piece(board: &Board, from: Square, piece: Piece, out: &mut MoveList) {
60	    match piece.kind {
61	        PieceKind::General => gen_general(board, from, piece.side, out),
62	        PieceKind::Advisor => gen_advisor(board, from, piece.side, out),
63	        PieceKind::Elephant => gen_elephant(board, from, piece.side, out),
64	        PieceKind::Chariot => gen_chariot(board, from, piece.side, out),
65	        PieceKind::Horse => gen_horse(board, from, piece.side, out),
66	        PieceKind::Cannon => gen_cannon(board, from, piece.side, out),
67	        PieceKind::Soldier => gen_soldier(board, from, piece.side, out),
68	    }
69	}
70	
71	// ---- General -----------------------------------------------------------------
72	
73	fn gen_general(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
74	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
75	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
76	        if !board.in_region(to, RegionKind::Palace(side)) {
77	            continue;
78	        }
79	        push_step_or_capture(board, from, to, side, out);
80	    }
81	}
82	
83	// ---- Advisor -----------------------------------------------------------------
84	
85	fn gen_advisor(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
86	    for &dir in &Direction::DIAGONAL {
87	        let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) else { continue };
88	        if !board.in_region(to, RegionKind::Palace(side)) {
89	            continue;
90	        }
91	        push_step_or_capture(board, from, to, side, out);
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	// ---- Elephant ----------------------------------------------------------------
96	
97	fn gen_elephant(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
98	    for &(dx, dy) in &[(2i8, 2i8), (-2, 2), (2, -2), (-2, -2)] {
99	        let Some(mid) = step_delta(board, from, dx / 2, dy / 2) else { continue };
100	        if board.get(mid).is_some() {
101	            continue; // ŤĪ°Áúľ blocked
102	        }
103	        let Some(to) = step_delta(board, from, dx, dy) else { continue };
104	        if !board.in_region(to, RegionKind::HomeHalf(side)) {
105	            continue; // cannot cross river
106	        }
107	        push_step_or_capture(board, from, to, side, out);
108	    }
109	}
110	
111	// ---- Chariot -----------------------------------------------------------------
112	
113	fn gen_chariot(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
114	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
115	        let (walked, blocker) = board.ray(from, dir);
116	        for sq in walked {
117	            out.push(Move::Step { from, to: sq });
118	        }
119	        if let Some(blocker) = blocker {
120	            if let Some(pos) = board.get(blocker) {
121	                if pos.piece.side != side {
122	                    out.push(Move::Capture { from, to: blocker, captured: pos.piece });
123	                }
124	            }
125	        }
126	    }
127	}
128	
129	// ---- Horse -------------------------------------------------------------------
130	
131	const HORSE_MOVES: [(i8, i8, i8, i8); 8] = [
132	    (1, 2, 0, 1),
133	    (-1, 2, 0, 1),
134	    (1, -2, 0, -1),
135	    (-1, -2, 0, -1),
136	    (2, 1, 1, 0),
137	    (2, -1, 1, 0),
138	    (-2, 1, -1, 0),
139	    (-2, -1, -1, 0),
140	];
141	
142	fn gen_horse(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
143	    for &(dx, dy, lx, ly) in &HORSE_MOVES {
144	        let Some(leg) = step_delta(board, from, lx, ly) else { continue };
145	        if board.get(leg).is_some() {
146	            continue; // ť¶¨ŤÖŅ blocked
147	        }
148	        let Some(to) = step_delta(board, from, dx, dy) else { continue };
149	        push_step_or_capture(board, from, to, side, out);
150	    }
151	}
152	
153	// ---- Cannon ------------------------------------------------------------------
154	
155	fn gen_cannon(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
156	    for &dir in &Direction::ORTHOGONAL {
157	        // Phase 1: empties (non-capturing slide).
158	        let (walked, screen) = board.ray(from, dir);
159	        for sq in walked {
160	            out.push(Move::Step { from, to: sq });
161	        }
162	        // Phase 2: jump over exactly one piece (the screen) to capture.
163	        let Some(screen_sq) = screen else { continue };
164	        let mut cursor = screen_sq;
165	        while let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) {
166	            if let Some(pos) = board.get(next) {
167	                if pos.piece.side != side {
168	                    out.push(Move::CannonJump {
169	                        from,
170	                        to: next,
171	                        screen: screen_sq,
172	                        captured: pos.piece,
173	                    });
174	                }
175	                break;
176	            }
177	            cursor = next;
178	        }
179	    }
180	}
181	
182	// ---- Soldier -----------------------------------------------------------------
183	
184	fn gen_soldier(board: &Board, from: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
185	    let forward = soldier_forward(side);
186	    if let Some(to) = board.step(from, forward) {
187	        push_step_or_capture(board, from, to, side, out);
188	    }
189	    if soldier_has_crossed_river(board, from, side) {
190	        for &dir in &[Direction::E, Direction::W] {
191	            if let Some(to) = board.step(from, dir) {
192	                push_step_or_capture(board, from, to, side, out);
193	            }
194	        }
195	    }
196	}
197	
198	#[inline]
199	fn soldier_forward(side: Side) -> Direction {
200	    if side == Side::RED {
201	        Direction::N
202	    } else {
203	        Direction::S
204	    }
205	}
206	
207	fn soldier_has_crossed_river(board: &Board, sq: Square, side: Side) -> bool {
208	    !board.in_region(sq, RegionKind::HomeHalf(side))
209	}
210	
211	// ---- Shared helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
212	
213	fn push_step_or_capture(board: &Board, from: Square, to: Square, side: Side, out: &mut MoveList) {
214	    match board.get(to) {
215	        None => out.push(Move::Step { from, to }),
216	        Some(pos) if pos.piece.side != side => {
217	            out.push(Move::Capture { from, to, captured: pos.piece });
218	        }
219	        Some(_) => {} // own piece blocks
220	    }
221	}
222	
223	fn step_delta(board: &Board, from: Square, dx: i8, dy: i8) -> Option<Square> {
224	    let (file, rank) = board.file_rank(from);
225	    let nf = (file.0 as i16) + dx as i16;
226	    let nr = (rank.0 as i16) + dy as i16;
227	    if nf < 0 || nr < 0 || nf >= board.width() as i16 || nr >= board.height() as i16 {
228	        return None;
229	    }
230	    Some(board.sq(File(nf as u8), Rank(nr as u8)))
231	}
232	
233	// ---- Attack and check detection ---------------------------------------------
234	
235	/// Whether `attacker` side threatens `target` square.
236	pub fn is_attacked(board: &Board, target: Square, attacker: Side) -> bool {
237	    for sq in board.squares() {
238	        let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) else { continue };
239	        if pos.piece.side != attacker || !pos.revealed {
240	            continue;
241	        }
242	        if attacks_square(board, sq, pos.piece, target) {
243	            return true;
244	        }
245	    }
246	    false
247	}
248	
249	fn attacks_square(board: &Board, from: Square, piece: Piece, target: Square) -> bool {
250	    let mut moves: MoveList = SmallVec::new();
251	    gen_for_piece(board, from, piece, &mut moves);
252	    moves.iter().any(|m| m.to_square() == Some(target))
253	}
254	
255	/// `side`'s general is in check iff it is attacked OR the two generals
256	/// face each other on a clear file.
257	pub fn is_in_check(state: &GameState, side: Side) -> bool {
258	    let board = &state.board;
259	    let Some(my_general) =
260	        crate::state::find_piece(board, |p| p.kind == PieceKind::General && p.side == side)
261	    else {
262	        return false;
263	    };
264	
265	    if is_attacked(board, my_general, side.opposite()) {
266	        return true;
267	    }
268	    generals_face(board, my_general)
269	}
270	
271	fn generals_face(board: &Board, my_general: Square) -> bool {
272	    let (my_file, _) = board.file_rank(my_general);
273	    // Find the opposing general
274	    let Some(their_general) = crate::state::find_piece(board, |p| {
275	        p.kind == PieceKind::General
276	            && (board.get(my_general).map(|x| x.piece.side != p.side).unwrap_or(false))
277	    }) else {
278	        return false;
279	    };
280	    let (their_file, _) = board.file_rank(their_general);
281	    if my_file != their_file {
282	        return false;
283	    }
284	    // Walk between them: any piece blocks the face-off.
285	    let dir = {
286	        let (_, r1) = board.file_rank(my_general);
287	        let (_, r2) = board.file_rank(their_general);
288	        if r2.0 > r1.0 {
289	            Direction::N
290	        } else {
291	            Direction::S
292	        }
293	    };
294	    let mut cursor = my_general;
295	    loop {
296	        let Some(next) = board.step(cursor, dir) else { return false };
297	        if next == their_general {
298	            return true;
299	        }
300	        if board.get(next).is_some() {
301	            return false;
302	        }
303	        cursor = next;
304	    }
305	}
306
