```text
44	    /// Seed for banqi shuffling. `None` = nondeterministic.
45	    pub banqi_seed: Option<u64>,
46	    /// Casual / training mode for xiangqi: when true, the legality filter
47	    /// no longer rejects moves that leave the mover's own general in check.
48	    /// The game ends only when a general is actually captured
49	    /// (`WinReason::GeneralCaptured`). Default `false` (standard rules).
50	    /// `#[serde(default)]` keeps older snapshots loadable.
51	    #[serde(default)]
52	    pub xiangqi_allow_self_check: bool,
53	}
```

```text
1	//! Banqi move generation.
2	//!
3	//! Base rules (always emitted):
4	//! - `Reveal` for every face-down piece.
5	//! - 1-step orthogonal `Step` / `Capture` for face-up own pieces.
6	//! - Cannon `CannonJump` over exactly one screen (face-up or face-down).
7	//!
8	//! House-rule extensions (this PR):
9	//! - `CHAIN_CAPTURE`: emit `ChainCapture` for face-up consecutive captures
10	//!   along the same direction (length ‚Č• 2).
11	//! - `CHARIOT_RUSH`: chariot uses xiangqi-style multi-square ray for both
12	//!   slides and captures.
13	//!
14	//! Deferred to PR 2 (toggles ship as accepted-but-no-effect):
15	//! - `DARK_CHAIN`: chain through face-down squares (requires Option<Piece>
16	//!   in ChainHop and the server-authoritative reveal flow).
17	//! - `HORSE_DIAGONAL`: horse moves like xiangqi.
18	//! - `CANNON_FAST_MOVE`: cannon non-capturing slide.

31	    gen_reveals(state, out);
32

35	
36	    for sq in board.squares() {
37	        let Some(pos) = board.get(sq) else { continue };
38	        if !pos.revealed || pos.piece.side != me {
39	            continue;
40	        }
41	        gen_for_face_up_piece(state, sq, pos.piece, house, out);
42	    }
43	}
```

```text
1	//! Sync `tungstenite` ws client driven from a background OS thread, with
2	//! `std::sync::mpsc` channels to/from the (sync) TUI loop. We deliberately
3	//! keep tokio out of the TUI binary ‚ÄĒ `crossterm::event::poll` is sync, and
4	//! mixing in a runtime would force a refactor.
5	//!
6	//! The thread does a non-blocking poll cycle: try one ws read, drain pending
7	//! outbound `ClientMsg`s, sleep ~20ms. Every parsed `ServerMsg` (or fatal
8	//! error) is shipped to the TUI as a `NetEvent`.
9	
10	use std::io::ErrorKind;
11	use std::net::TcpStream;
12	use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender, TryRecvError};
13	use std::thread::{self, JoinHandle};
14	use std::time::Duration;
15	
16	use anyhow::{anyhow, Result};
17	use chess_net::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
18	use tungstenite::stream::MaybeTlsStream;
19	use tungstenite::{Message, WebSocket};
20	
21	const POLL_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_millis(20);
22	const CONNECT_ATTEMPTS: u32 = 8;
23	const CONNECT_BACKOFF: Duration = Duration::from_millis(200);
24	
25	#[derive(Debug)]
26	pub enum NetEvent {
27	    Connected,
28	    Server(Box<ServerMsg>),
29	    Disconnected(String),
30	}
31	
32	pub struct NetClient {
33	    pub cmd_tx: Sender<ClientMsg>,
34	    pub evt_rx: Receiver<NetEvent>,
35	    _handle: JoinHandle<()>,
36	}
37	
38	impl NetClient {
39	    /// Spawns the worker thread and returns immediately. The caller polls
40	    /// `evt_rx` each frame (use `try_recv`, never `recv` ‚ÄĒ the TUI loop must
41	    /// not block) and pushes outbound moves via `cmd_tx`.
42	    pub fn spawn(url: String) -> Self {
43	        let (cmd_tx, cmd_rx) = mpsc::channel::<ClientMsg>();
44	        let (evt_tx, evt_rx) = mpsc::channel::<NetEvent>();
45	        let handle = thread::spawn(move || run(url, cmd_rx, evt_tx));
46	        Self { cmd_tx, evt_rx, _handle: handle }
47	    }
48	}
49	
50	fn run(url: String, cmd_rx: Receiver<ClientMsg>, evt_tx: Sender<NetEvent>) {
51	    let mut ws = match connect_with_retry(&url) {
52	        Ok(ws) => ws,
53	        Err(e) => {
54	            let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Disconnected(format!("connect failed: {e}")));
55	            return;
56	        }
57	    };
58	    if let Err(e) = set_nonblocking(&mut ws) {
59	        let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Disconnected(format!("set_nonblocking: {e}")));
60	        return;
61	    }
62	    let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Connected);
63	
64	    loop {
65	        // Inbound: try to read one frame.
66	        match ws.read() {
67	            Ok(Message::Text(t)) => match serde_json::from_str::<ServerMsg>(t.as_str()) {
68	                Ok(msg) => {
69	                    if evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Server(Box::new(msg))).is_err() {
70	                        return; // TUI dropped the receiver
71	                    }
72	                }
73	                Err(e) => {
74	                    let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Disconnected(format!("decode ServerMsg: {e}")));
75	                    return;
76	                }
77	            },
78	            Ok(Message::Close(_)) => {
79	                let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Disconnected("server closed".into()));
80	                return;
81	            }
82	            Ok(_) => {} // ping/pong/binary ‚ÄĒ ignore, tungstenite handles ping/pong itself
83	            Err(tungstenite::Error::Io(e)) if e.kind() == ErrorKind::WouldBlock => {}
84	            Err(e) => {
85	                let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Disconnected(format!("read: {e}")));
86	                return;
87	            }
88	        }
89	
90	        // Outbound: drain whatever's queued.
91	        loop {
92	            match cmd_rx.try_recv() {
93	                Ok(msg) => {
94	                    let json = match serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
95	                        Ok(j) => j,
96	                        Err(e) => {
97	                            let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Disconnected(format!("encode: {e}")));
98	                            return;
99	                        }
100	                    };
101	                    if let Err(e) = ws.send(Message::Text(json)) {
102	                        let _ = evt_tx.send(NetEvent::Disconnected(format!("send: {e}")));
103	                        return;
104	                    }
105	                }
106	                Err(TryRecvError::Empty) => break,
107	                Err(TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
108	                    let _ = ws.close(None);
109	                    return;
110	                }
111	            }
112	        }
113	
114	        thread::sleep(POLL_INTERVAL);
115	    }
116	}
117	
118	fn connect_with_retry(url: &str) -> Result<WebSocket<MaybeTlsStream<TcpStream>>> {
119	    let mut last_err: Option<tungstenite::Error> = None;
120	    for _ in 0..CONNECT_ATTEMPTS {
121	        match tungstenite::connect(url) {
122	            Ok((ws, _resp)) => return Ok(ws),
123	            Err(e) => {
124	                last_err = Some(e);
125	                thread::sleep(CONNECT_BACKOFF);
126	            }
127	        }
128	    }
129	    Err(anyhow!(
130	        "could not reach {url} after {} attempts: {}",
131	        CONNECT_ATTEMPTS,
132	        last_err.map(|e| e.to_string()).unwrap_or_default()
133	    ))
134	}
135	
136	fn set_nonblocking(ws: &mut WebSocket<MaybeTlsStream<TcpStream>>) -> std::io::Result<()> {
137	    match ws.get_mut() {
138	        MaybeTlsStream::Plain(tcp) => tcp.set_nonblocking(true),
139	        // Other MaybeTlsStream variants (rustls/native-tls) only appear when
140	        // those features are enabled; we don't enable them.
141	        _ => Ok(()),
142	    }
143	}
144
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
27	    assert_eq!(state.legal_moves().len(), 0, "standard rules: checkmate");
28	
29	    // Switch to casual: pseudo-legal moves become legal.

31	    let casual_moves = state.legal_moves();

33	        !casual_moves.is_empty(),

43	    //   Black to move; the chariot captures Red's general.

46	    let mut board = Board::new(BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10);
47	    let red_gen = board.sq(File(4), Rank(0));
48	    let blk_gen = board.sq(File(4), Rank(9));
49	    let blk_chariot = board.sq(File(4), Rank(3));
50	
51	    board.set(red_gen, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::General))));
52	    board.set(blk_gen, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::General))));
53	    board.set(

55	        Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Chariot))),

58	    let mut state = GameState {

66	        no_progress_plies: 0,
67	    };
68	
69	    let capture = state
70	        .legal_moves()
71	        .into_iter()
72	        .find(|m| m.origin_square() == blk_chariot && m.to_square() == Some(red_gen))
73	        .expect("Black chariot should be able to capture the Red general");
74	
75	    state.make_move(&capture).expect("apply capture");
76	    state.refresh_status();
77	
78	    assert_eq!(

81	        "expected GeneralCaptured win, got {:?}",

83	    );
84	}
```

```text
/// `side`'s general is in check iff it is attacked OR the two generals
/// face each other on a clear file.
pub fn is_in_check(state: &GameState, side: Side) -> bool {
    let board = &state.board;
    let Some(my_general) =
        crate::state::find_piece(board, |p| p.kind == PieceKind::General && p.side == side)
    else {
        return false;
    };

    if is_attacked(board, my_general, side.opposite()) {
        return true;
    }
```

Perfect! I have all the information needed. Let me compile my findings:

## Findings Report

### 1. **Is "side X is in check" a public API?**

**YES ‚ÄĒ it's fully public.**

- **Location:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:232‚Äď234`
- **Signature:** `pub fn is_in_check(&self, side: Side) -> bool`
- **Implementation:** Delegates to `crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check(self, side)` at `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/rules/xiangqi.rs:257‚Äď269`
- **Details:** The check detector is **independent of the `xiangqi_allow_self_check` flag**‚ÄĒit's a pure state query that tests whether a general is attacked OR generals face each other on a clear file. It doesn't validate legality; it only reports truth. UIs can unconditionally call `state.is_in_check(side)` to render the "ŚįáŤĽć" banner in both standard and casual modes.
- **Banqi:** Confirmed‚ÄĒbanqi has **no check** concept (returns `false` for missing general per line 262).

### 2. **GameStatus / WinReason enums**

**Location:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:20‚Äď46`

- **GameStatus variants:**
  - `Ongoing`
  - `Won { winner: Side, reason: WinReason }`
  - `Drawn { reason: DrawReason }`

- **WinReason variants:**
  - `Checkmate` ‚ÄĒ no legal moves + general in check
  - `Stalemate` ‚ÄĒ no legal moves + general not in check (xiangqi only)
  - `Resignation`
  - `OnlyOneSideHasPieces` ‚ÄĒ banqi end state
  - `Timeout`
  - `GeneralCaptured` ‚ÄĒ casual xiangqi only (general physically captured)

- **DrawReason variants:**
  - `NoProgress` ‚ÄĒ 60 plies no capture/reveal
  - `Repetition` ‚ÄĒ threefold (not yet implemented)
  - `Agreed`
  - `InsufficientMaterial`

### 3. **PlayerView includes status?**

**YES ‚ÄĒ fully exposed over the network.**

- **Location:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/view.rs:25‚Äď35`
- **Field:** `pub status: GameStatus` (line 32)
- **Serializable:** `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]` on `PlayerView`
- The `PlayerView::project()` method (line 40) copies the full `status` from the internal state (line 66). Network clients receive the same end-game trigger signals as local play.

### 4. **Check vs casual mode interaction**

**Confirmed: `is_in_check` is independent; works identically in both modes.**

- **Standard mode** (`xiangqi_allow_self_check: false`): Legality filter rejects moves exposing the general; checkmate ends the game (`WinReason::Checkmate`).
- **Casual mode** (`xiangqi_allow_self_check: true`): Legality filter skipped (line 23‚Äď27 in xiangqi.rs). Pseudo-legal moves become legal. Game ends only when general is physically captured (`WinReason::GeneralCaptured` per line 260 in state/mod.rs).
- **In both modes:** `is_in_check(side)` returns the same truth value‚ÄĒit's called identically in `refresh_status` at line 275 to determine Checkmate vs Stalemate.

Test confirms (line 39‚Äď84 in casual_xiangqi.rs): a contrived casual position with the general capturable correctly reports `GeneralCaptured` after `refresh_status`.

**Recommendation:** Reuse the existing `GameState::is_in_check(side: Side) -> bool` public method. No new wrapper needed‚ÄĒit's already positioned correctly for UI use.

## Final Report: Chess-Web Crate Architecture Mapping

### 1. **End-game UX Today** (Lines: play.rs:230-235, sidebar.rs:43-64)
- **Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs:43-64`
- **Current Implementation**: `GameStatus` enum from chess-core (imported at line 9) drives three outcomes:
  - `Ongoing` ‚Üí "Ongoing" status text (line 46)
  - `Won { winner, reason }` ‚Üí formatted as "{side} wins ‚ÄĒ {reason}" (line 52) with class `status.won`
  - `Drawn { reason }` ‚Üí formatted as "Drawn ‚ÄĒ {reason}" with class `status.drawn`
- **CSS File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/style.css` (lines 290-293)
  - `.status.won { color: var(--accent); }`
  - `.status.drawn { color: var(--muted); }`
  - `.status.ongoing { color: var(--muted); font-weight: 400; }`
- **Play page**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs:230-238` also checks `GameStatus::Won | GameStatus::Drawn` to disable rematch/resign buttons
- **No banner overlay yet** ‚ÄĒ just inline text in sidebar.

---

### 2. **Initial Picker / Lobby UI** (Lines: picker.rs, lobby.rs)
- **Picker Page**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs:8-24`
  - Title: "Chinese Chess" (h1, line 11)
  - Subtitle explains URL encoding & variant selection
  - **Grid of 4 cards** (line 16): Xiangqi (strict/casual radio), Banqi (preset + house rules checkboxes), Three-Kingdom (WIP link), Online (lobby link)
  - Form-heavy UX with `.variant-card--form` class for interactive cards
- **Lobby Page**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/lobby.rs:59-86`
  - Text inputs for room name + password (plain HTML)
  - Connection banner (ConnBanner component, lines 147-160) with status class (ok/pending/error)
  - Room list shows seats, variant, status badges (lines 89-141)
  - Plain form-based, no branding imagery
- **Global Stylesheet**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/style.css`
  - CSS vars for colors (--bg, --fg, --accent, --red, --black, --green, etc.) at top
  - Picker grid: `.picker-grid` (lines 39-43), `.variant-card` (lines 45-59)
  - Lobby: `.lobby` (lines 316-327), `.rooms-list` + `.room-row` (lines 338-357)

---

### 3. **Animation Feasibility**
- **No existing @keyframes** ‚ÄĒ CSS has only `.variant-card:hover` and `.btn:hover` transitions (0.12s ease)
- **No canvas usage** ‚ÄĒ pure SVG board rendering (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/components/board.rs`)
- **Leptos signals everywhere** ‚ÄĒ view! macro with reactive `create_signal`, `create_memo`, `create_effect`
- **Feasibility**: CSS animations are natural fit; can attach Leptos signals to trigger `.confetti-burst` or similar class on game-over

---

### 4. **Check/ŚįáŤĽć Detection Plumbing**
- **Chess-core function available**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:232-233`
  ```rust
  pub fn is_in_check(&self, side: Side) -> bool {
      crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check(self, side)
  }
  ```
- **But NOT exposed in chess-web client** ‚ÄĒ neither sidebar nor board components call it
- **TUI uses it**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs:884` calls `g.state.is_in_check(view.side_to_move)` for Xiangqi only
- **Web client limitation**: `GameState` is not directly accessible from `PlayerView` (only read-only projection). Would need either:
  - Expose `is_in_check()` function in WASM bindings, OR
  - Server broadcasts check status in `ServerMsg::Update`

---

### 5. **Settings / Preferences**
- **No client-side preferences system** ‚ÄĒ no localStorage, no settings panel
- **URL-driven entirely**: 
  - Picker routes encode rules into URL: `build_local_href()` in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs`
  - Play page reads `?password=` and `?role=spectator` query params (play.rs:22-27)
- **For confetti toggle**: Would need to add query param (e.g., `?confetti=off`) or localStorage via Leptos `create_local_resource()`

---

### 6. **Three-Kingdom WIP Overlay** (REUSABLE PATTERN!)
- **Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs:136-147`
- **CSS**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/style.css:201-228`
  ```css
  .wip-overlay {
    position: absolute; inset: 0;
    background: rgba(26, 22, 20, 0.78);
    display: grid; place-items: center;
    border-radius: 0.5rem; pointer-events: none;
  }
  .wip-banner {
    background: var(--bg);
    color: var(--fg);
    border: 1px solid var(--accent);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem;
  }
  ```
- **Leptos**: `<Show when=move || wip>` guard (local.rs:136) ‚ÄĒ toggles overlay on `wip: bool` prop
- **VICTORY overlay would follow same pattern**: dark scrim + centered banner with `border: 1px solid var(--accent)`, can add `@keyframes pulse` or `scale-up` animation

---

## Quick Summary (for planning):

| Component | File Path | Key Signal | Output |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| End-game status | `sidebar.rs:43-64` | `GameStatus` enum | Text in `.status` div |
| Picker/Lobby UI | `picker.rs:8-24`, `lobby.rs:59-86` | URL query params | Form-based plain HTML |
| Global styles | `style.css` (lines 1-387) | CSS variables | Tan/burgundy theme |
| WIP overlay pattern | `local.rs:136-147` + `style.css:201-228` | Leptos `<Show>` guard | Reusable dark scrim + banner |
| Check detection | `chess-core/state/mod.rs:232` | NOT exposed to web client | Xiangqi only, needs WASM binding or server broadcast |
| Preferences | None | URL + query params only | No localStorage/settings UI yet |

```text
225	fn run_loop(terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>, app: &mut AppState) -> Result<()> {
226	    loop {
227	        // Drain ws events first so the next draw shows server-pushed state.
228	        // No-op outside Net mode.
229	        app.tick_net();
230	
231	        terminal.draw(|frame| ui::draw(frame, app))?;
232	        if app.should_quit {
233	            return Ok(());
234	        }
235	
236	        // Shorter poll in Net / Lobby modes keeps server pushes feeling
237	        // snappy (we redraw at most every poll interval). 200ms in
238	        // single-process modes is fine ‚ÄĒ no async source to drain.
239	        let poll_ms = if matches!(app.screen, app::Screen::Net(_) | app::Screen::Lobby(_)) {
240	            60
241	        } else {
242	            200
243	        };
244	        if event::poll(Duration::from_millis(poll_ms))? {
245	            let mode = app.input_mode();
246	            let quit_confirm = app.quit_confirm_open;
247	            let action = match event::read()? {
248	                Event::Key(k) if k.kind == event::KeyEventKind::Press => {
249	                    from_key(k, mode, quit_confirm)
250	                }
251	                Event::Mouse(m) => from_mouse(m),
252	                Event::Resize(_, _) => Action::None,
253	                _ => Action::None,
254	            };
255	            app.dispatch(action);
256	        }
257	    }
258	}
259
```
</details></tool-use>

## Chess-TUI Crate Exploration Report

### 1. **End-Game UX** ‚ÄĒ Win/Loss/Draw Banner Rendering

- **Draw banner** (lines 1201‚Äď1217): Renders "‚ÄĒ  DRAW  ‚ÄĒ" (yellow bold) with reason.

- **GameStatus enum:** Defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` at **line 20**. WinReason includes `Checkmate`, `Stalemate`, `Resignation`, `OnlyOneSideHasPieces`, `Timeout`, `GeneralCaptured`.
- **Net rematch flow:** In `app.rs` at **line 414**, pressing 'n' post-game sends `ClientMsg::Rematch` via `n.client.cmd_tx.send()`. Sets `last_msg = "Rematch requested. Waiting for opponent‚Ä¶"`.

### 2. **Initial Menu / Variant Picker**

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` and `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`

- **Picker UI:** `draw_picker()` at **ui.rs line 46**. Renders title "chess-tui" (bold), instructions "Choose a variant. Arrow keys + Enter; q to quit.", then 7 entries.
- **Picker entries:** Defined in `app.rs` **line 28** as `enum PickerEntry` with 7 options: `Xiangqi`, `XiangqiStrict`, `BanqiPurist`, `BanqiTaiwan`, `BanqiAggressive`, `ConnectToServer`, `Quit`. Each has a `.label()` method (lines 50‚Äď60) with Chinese + English text.
- **Display style:** Cursor prefix "‚Ė∂ " on selected row, yellow+bold for active entry, gray otherwise. No ASCII art title‚ÄĒjust text.
- **Pre-game screens:** Picker ‚Üí (if ConnectToServer) ‚Üí HostPrompt (line 166) ‚Üí LobbyView (line 199). LobbyView shows live room list, password-lock entry, and create-room flow.

### 3. **Animation / Draw Loop**

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs`

- **Draw loop:** `run_loop()` at **line 225** is **event-driven with poll intervals**, not a fixed tick timer. Each iteration:
  1. Calls `app.tick_net()` (drains WebSocket events)
  2. Calls `terminal.draw(|frame| ui::draw(frame, app))?` (redraws)
  3. Blocks on `event::poll(Duration::from_millis(poll_ms))` (lines 239‚Äď244)
- **Poll intervals:** 60ms for Net/Lobby screens (responsive to server pushes), 200ms for local games. **No timer-based confetti animation‚ÄĒonly redraws on input or poll timeout.**
- **Implication for confetti:** Would require adding a **frame counter or animation state** that advances each draw and a **shorter poll interval** during animation (or spawning a background task to trigger `should_redraw` flag every N ms).

### 4. **Check/ŚįáŤĽć Detection**

**Files:** 
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs` line 232
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-core/src/rules/xiangqi.rs` line 257

- **Net mode:** No check banner in `draw_sidebar_net()` ‚ÄĒ would need to be added if desired (currently only banners are game-over and status).

### 5. **Settings / Config Plumbing**

**Files:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs` and `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs`

- **CLI struct:** `Cli` at **main.rs line 40**. Flags like `--style {cjk,ascii}` (line 44) and `--no-color` (line 48) are parsed via `clap` and passed to `AppState::new_*()` constructors.
- **State plumbing:** Both `style: Style` and `use_color: bool` are stored as fields in `AppState` (line 212‚Äď213) and threaded through all screen constructors (`new_picker`, `new_game`, `new_net`, etc.) at lines 243‚Äď302.
- **Renderer consumption:** `ui::draw()` reads `app.style` and `app.use_color` and passes them to drawing functions. E.g., `draw_game()` at **ui.rs line 67** extracts both before calling `draw_board()` and `draw_sidebar()` (lines 78‚Äď79, 96).
- **Pattern for new flags:** Add field to `Cli`, add to `AppState`, thread through constructors, read in `ui.rs` drawing functions. A `--no-confetti` or `--no-check-banner` flag would follow this exact pattern.

---

**Key takeaway:** Confetti/banner features have all scaffolding in place. Check detection is live. Banners are rendered in sidebar. The draw loop redraw-on-timeout means animations need either (a) a per-frame counter in AppState + shorter poll interval during animation, or (b) a background timer task to force redraws. CLI flags thread cleanly through AppState to the renderer.

```text
1	//! Per-player view projection.
2	//!
3	//! `PlayerView` is the only state the network layer should serialize and
4	//! ship to clients ‚ÄĒ it has hidden-piece identities scrubbed and (for
5	//! non-side-to-move observers) no legal-move list to leak strategy.
6	//!
7	//! See ADR-0004.
8	
9	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
10	
11	use crate::board::BoardShape;
12	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
13	use crate::piece::{PieceOnSquare, Side};
14	use crate::state::{GameState, GameStatus};
15	
16	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
17	pub enum VisibleCell {
18	    Empty,
19	    /// Banqi face-down: identity hidden from this observer.
20	    Hidden,
21	    Revealed(PieceOnSquare),
22	}
23	
24	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
25	pub struct PlayerView {
26	    pub observer: Side,
27	    pub shape: BoardShape,
28	    pub width: u8,
29	    pub height: u8,
30	    pub cells: Vec<VisibleCell>,
31	    pub side_to_move: Side,
32	    pub status: GameStatus,
33	    /// Legal moves for the observer if it's their turn; empty otherwise.
34	    pub legal_moves: MoveList,
35	}
36	
37	impl PlayerView {
38	    /// Project `state` from `observer`'s vantage point. Hidden pieces stay
39	    /// hidden; for opponents, legal-move list is empty.
40	    pub fn project(state: &GameState, observer: Side) -> Self {
41	        let board = &state.board;
42	        let cells: Vec<VisibleCell> = (0..board.cell_count())
43	            .map(|i| {
44	                let sq = crate::coord::Square(i as u16);
45	                match board.get(sq) {
46	                    None => VisibleCell::Empty,
47	                    Some(pos) if pos.revealed => VisibleCell::Revealed(pos),
48	                    Some(_) => VisibleCell::Hidden,
49	                }
50	            })
51	            .collect();
52	
53	        let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move {
54	            sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
55	        } else {
56	            MoveList::new()
57	        };
58	
59	        Self {
60	            observer,
61	            shape: board.shape(),
62	            width: board.width(),
63	            height: board.height(),
64	            cells,
65	            side_to_move: state.side_to_move,
66	            status: state.status,
67	            legal_moves,
68	        }
69	    }
70	}
71	
72	/// Strip identity from `Reveal` moves before they reach the network.
73	/// Even the side-to-move sees `revealed: None` because the engine resolves
74	/// the identity authoritatively when the move is applied.
75	fn sanitize_for_observer(moves: MoveList, _observer: Side) -> MoveList {
76	    moves
77	        .into_iter()
78	        .map(|m| match m {
79	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: _ } => Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None },
80	            other => other,
81	        })
82	        .collect()
83	}
84	
85	#[cfg(test)]
86	mod tests {
87	    use super::*;
88	    use crate::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
89	
90	    #[test]
91	    fn fresh_banqi_view_has_all_hidden_for_any_observer() {
92	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 7));
93	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
94	        assert_eq!(view.cells.len(), 32);
95	        assert!(view.cells.iter().all(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Hidden)));
96	    }
97

100	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
```

```text
200	fn run(mut app: AppState) -> Result<()> {
201	    let mut terminal = setup_terminal()?;
202	    let result = run_loop(&mut terminal, &mut app);
203	    teardown_terminal(&mut terminal).ok();
204	    result
205	}
206	
207	fn setup_terminal() -> Result<Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>> {
208	    enable_raw_mode().context("enable raw mode")?;
209	    let mut stdout = io::stdout();
210	    execute!(stdout, EnterAlternateScreen, crossterm::event::EnableMouseCapture)
211	        .context("enter alt screen")?;
212	    let backend = CrosstermBackend::new(stdout);
213	    let terminal = Terminal::new(backend).context("init terminal")?;
214	    Ok(terminal)
215	}
216	
217	fn teardown_terminal(terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>) -> Result<()> {
218	    disable_raw_mode().ok();
219	    execute!(terminal.backend_mut(), LeaveAlternateScreen, crossterm::event::DisableMouseCapture)
220	        .ok();
221	    terminal.show_cursor().ok();
222	    Ok(())
223	}
224	
225	fn run_loop(terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>, app: &mut AppState) -> Result<()> {
226	    loop {
227	        // Drain ws events first so the next draw shows server-pushed state.
228	        // No-op outside Net mode.
229	        app.tick_net();
230	
231	        terminal.draw(|frame| ui::draw(frame, app))?;
232	        if app.should_quit {
233	            return Ok(());
234	        }
235	
236	        // Shorter poll in Net / Lobby modes keeps server pushes feeling
237	        // snappy (we redraw at most every poll interval). 200ms in
238	        // single-process modes is fine ‚ÄĒ no async source to drain.
239	        let poll_ms = if matches!(app.screen, app::Screen::Net(_) | app::Screen::Lobby(_)) {
240	            60
241	        } else {
242	            200
243	        };
244	        if event::poll(Duration::from_millis(poll_ms))? {
245	            let mode = app.input_mode();
246	            let quit_confirm = app.quit_confirm_open;
247	            let action = match event::read()? {
248	                Event::Key(k) if k.kind == event::KeyEventKind::Press => {
249	                    from_key(k, mode, quit_confirm)
250	                }
251	                Event::Mouse(m) => from_mouse(m),
252	                Event::Resize(_, _) => Action::None,
253	                _ => Action::None,
254	            };
255	            app.dispatch(action);
256	        }
257	    }
258	}
259
```

```text
# Implementation Plan: Victory Effects + Check Banner Toggles

## Task Sequence (Dependency Order)

### Phase 1 ‚ÄĒ Wire Protocol (foundation for both clients)

**1. Add `in_check` to `PlayerView`** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs`
- Add `pub in_check: bool` with `#[serde(default)]`. In `project()` (or wherever `PlayerView` is constructed), compute `state.is_in_check(observer_side)`. For Banqi/spectator-of-banqi, hard-code `false`.
- ~10 LOC.
- Risk: ensure `project()` is called once per observer; spectator side may need a deliberate "neutral" choice (recommend `false` for spectator since they're not in check).

**2. Bump protocol version to 4** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`
- Change `pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u32 = 3` ‚Üí `4`. Update changelog comment.
- ~3 LOC.
- Risk: confirm server-side handshake check is `==` vs `>=`; if `==`, mismatched v3 clients hard-fail (acceptable since `serde(default)` only protects field shape, not version negotiation).

**3. Tests for `in_check` projection** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs::tests`
- Add 4 small tests: ongoing-not-check, ongoing-in-check (xiangqi), banqi-always-false, post-checkmate state.
- ~40 LOC.

### Phase 2 ‚ÄĒ chess-tui

**4. ASCII banner module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs`
- Const arrays: `VICTORY_ART`, `DEFEAT_ART`, `DRAW_ART`, `CHECK_ART_EN`, `CHECK_ART_CN` (5‚Äď7 rows of `#`/`‚ĖĎ‚Ėď` block letters). Public fn `art_for(kind, style) -> &'static [&'static str]` honoring `Style::Ascii` vs `Style::Unicode`.
- ~120 LOC (mostly data).
- Risk: `ŚįáŤĽć` glyph width ‚ÄĒ verify in the existing Unicode font fallback used by `glyph.rs`; fall back to "JIANG JUN" if `--ascii` set.

**5. Confetti animation state + module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/confetti.rs`; touch `app.rs`
- Define `Particle { x, y, vx, vy, glyph: char, color: Color }` and `ConfettiAnim { particles: Vec<Particle>, started_at: Instant }`. Add `pub confetti_anim: Option<ConfettiAnim>` to `AppState` and `pub show_confetti: bool` + `pub show_check_banner: bool`.
- `confetti.rs` exports `spawn(area_w, area_h) -> ConfettiAnim` (30‚Äď50 particles, random vx in `[-0.6, 0.6]`, vy in `[-2.0, -0.8]`, glyphs `*+‚ú¶‚óÜ`, colors red/yellow/cyan). `step(&mut self)` applies `vy += 0.4`, advances pos, drops offscreen particles. Returns `done` when `Instant::elapsed() > 3s` OR `particles.is_empty()`.
- ~120 LOC.
- Risk: float coords inside ratatui's integer cell grid ‚ÄĒ round at render time, dedupe overlapping particles per cell to avoid flicker.

**6. Trigger on status transition + faster poll** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs` (around `dispatch` and `tick_net`)
- Track `prev_status: GameStatus` per render-loop iteration. When `prev != Ongoing` is false and `new != Ongoing` becomes true, call `confetti::spawn()` if `show_confetti`. In `main.rs:225` loop, when `confetti_anim.is_some()`, set `poll_ms = 50` (else keep 60/200).
- ~30 LOC.
- Risk: Net mode receives status via `PlayerView`; ensure transition is detected on first frame after the new view arrives, not on every tick.

**7. Game-over overlay + check banner render** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`
- New `draw_game_over_overlay(frame, area, kind, role)` rendering ASCII art centered over board area. Active for first 3s after end (driven by `confetti_anim` lifetime ‚ÄĒ reuse its timer). After 3s, collapse to existing sidebar `game_over_banner` only.
- Extend `draw_sidebar_net` (line 940) with same `is_in_check` line as `draw_sidebar` (line 884), reading from `view.in_check`. Gate both behind `app.show_check_banner`.

**8. CLI flags** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs:40` (`Cli`) + `app.rs:212`
- Add `#[arg(long)] no_confetti: bool` and `no_check_banner: bool`. Wire `app.show_confetti = !cli.no_confetti`, `app.show_check_banner = !cli.no_check_banner`. Default both ON.
- ~10 LOC.

**9. TUI picker polish** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs:46 draw_picker`
- Add `const PICKER_BANNER: &[&str]` (5 rows). Render at top of picker area; honor `--ascii` by using a plain `+/-` alternative.
- ~30 LOC.

### Phase 3 ‚ÄĒ chess-web

**10. Prefs module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs`; init in `app.rs`
- Two `RwSignal<bool>`: `fx_confetti`, `fx_check_banner`. Hydrate from `localStorage` keys `chess.fx.confetti`, `chess.fx.checkBanner` (default `"1"`). Persist on change via `create_effect`. `provide_context` an aggregate `Prefs` struct.

Renders dark scrim + centered card with `<h1 class="endgame-title">VICTORY|DEFEAT|DRAW</h1>` + winner + reason. Spectator role always shows neutral "Red wins"/"Black wins" (never VICTORY).

**12. CSS confetti + banner pulse** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web/style.css`
- Add `@keyframes confetti-fall` (translateY 0‚Üí110vh + rotate), `@keyframes pulse-banner` (scale 1‚ÜĒ1.05). Add `.confetti-container` (fixed inset-0, pointer-events none), `.confetti-particle` (uses `--x`, `--delay`, `--color` custom properties).

**13. Confetti component** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/components/confetti.rs`; mount alongside overlay
- Generates 40 `<div class="confetti-particle">` with random inline `style="--x: ‚Ä¶%; --delay: ‚Ä¶s; --color: ‚Ä¶"`. Triggered by `create_effect` on `view.status`. Auto-removes after 3s via `set_timeout`.

**14. Web check banner badge** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs`
- Above turn-row, conditional `<div class="check-badge">ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK</div>`. Source: Local = `state.is_in_check(view.side_to_move)`; Net = `view.in_check`. Gate on `prefs.fx_check_banner`. Add `.check-badge` CSS with subtle pulse.

**15. Web settings toggles + picker hero** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs` (or new `settings_menu.rs`) + `clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs:8`
```

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-05-07 07:45:39Z)**_

Design a step-by-step implementation plan for adding victory effects + check (ŚįáŤĽć) banner toggles to a Rust+WASM Chinese chess project. Repo root: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess`.

## User intent

Make wins/losses visually obvious in **both chess-tui (ratatui)** and **chess-web (Leptos+SVG)** clients. Add confetti-style effect on game-over plus a big "VICTORY / DEFEAT / DRAW" banner. Also a "ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK" banner that's toggleable. Plus small polish to the picker page.

## Locked decisions (already chosen)

1. **Banner text = hand-rolled ASCII art** for ~5 fixed strings (VICTORY, DEFEAT, DRAW, CHECK, ŚįáŤĽć). No new crate dep. Web-side uses scaled SVG `<text>` instead of ASCII art (cleaner for browser).
2. **Check banner default = ON** in both clients. Toggleable via `--no-check-banner` flag (TUI) and a small `localStorage` toggle button (Web).
3. **Confetti default = ON**. TUI: `--no-confetti`; Web: localStorage toggle.
4. **Spectators get confetti + neutral banner** ("Red wins" not "you won").
5. **Small menu polish in same PR**: TUI picker gets a 3‚Äď5 line ASCII title; Web picker gets a small `<header>` hero block with variant icons. No grand redesign.

## Context from exploration (use these ‚ÄĒ don't re-grep)

### chess-core (READY)
- `GameState::is_in_check(side: Side) -> bool` is public at `crates/chess-core/src/state/mod.rs:232`. Works in both strict + casual modes (tested). Banqi returns false (no general).
- `PlayerView` at `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs:25` has `status: GameStatus` on the wire. **Does NOT have an `in_check` field** ‚ÄĒ Net mode would have to add one or the client would need a way to recompute. **Add `pub in_check: bool` to `PlayerView`**, computed at `project()` time as `state.is_in_check(observer)`. `#[serde(default)]` for v3 client back-compat with new server. Bump protocol to v4.
- `GameStatus::{Ongoing, Won{winner,reason}, Drawn{reason}}` and `WinReason::{Checkmate, Stalemate, Resignation, OnlyOneSideHasPieces, Timeout, GeneralCaptured}` ‚ÄĒ all already serializable on the wire.

### chess-tui
- Draw loop at `clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs:225` is event-driven with poll timeout (60ms net / 200ms local). For confetti animation: drop poll to ~50ms while animation is active (or always; 200‚Üí50 isn't expensive); maintain a frame counter on AppState.
- Existing game-over banner: `ui.rs:1173 game_over_banner()`, rendered in `draw_sidebar` (line 834) and `draw_sidebar_net` (line 940). Sidebar ‚ÄĒ not a full-screen overlay.
- Existing check banner: `ui.rs:884` calls `g.state.is_in_check(view.side_to_move)` ‚ÄĒ but **only in `draw_sidebar` (Local), NOT in `draw_sidebar_net`** (Net). Need to extend Net.
- Picker UI: `ui.rs:46 draw_picker()`. Plain text title "chess-tui". Add 3‚Äď5 line ASCII art header above current entries.
- CLI plumbing pattern: `main.rs:40 Cli` with clap ‚Üí fields stored in `AppState` (`app.rs:212‚Äď213`) ‚Üí threaded into renderers. Add `--no-confetti`, `--no-check-banner` following the `--no-color` pattern.

### chess-web
- End-game inline text only: `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs:43-64`. **No overlay yet.**
- WIP overlay pattern is reusable: `clients/chess-web/src/pages/local.rs:136` + `style.css:201-228`. Position absolute, dark scrim, centered banner. Copy structure for VICTORY overlay.
- CSS: `clients/chess-web/style.css`. No existing @keyframes. Add `@keyframes confetti-fall` + `@keyframes pulse-banner`.
- Check detection in Web: Local mode has `GameState` directly (call `state.is_in_check(side)`). Net mode reads from `PlayerView` ‚ÄĒ needs the new `in_check` field.
- Settings: no existing system; URL/query string only. **Add minimal `localStorage` reads in `app.rs` or new `prefs.rs`** with two keys: `chess.fx.confetti` and `chess.fx.checkBanner` (both default `"1"`). Two small toggle buttons in the sidebar (or in a new tiny settings dropdown).
- Picker: `clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs:8`. Add `<header class="hero">` above `.picker-grid` with title + tagline + maybe small SVG variant glyphs.

## What to design

For each of these areas, give specific files + functions + LOC estimates + sequencing:

1. **chess-core**: add `in_check` to `PlayerView`, bump protocol version constant in `chess-net/src/protocol.rs` to 4 (`#[serde(default)]` keeps v3 servers/clients tolerant). Tests: extend `view.rs::tests` with one assertion per variant.

2. **chess-tui banners (Local + Net)**: render a *full-width top banner* (not just sidebar text) when game ends ‚ÄĒ overlay across the board area for ~3 seconds, then collapse to sidebar text. Hand-rolled ASCII art module at `clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs` with `victory_art()`, `defeat_art()`, `draw_art()`, `check_art()` returning `&'static [&'static str]`. Choose a 5‚Äď7-row block-style font (like ‚ĖĎ‚Ėď or just ASCII `#`).

3. **chess-tui confetti**: animation state on `AppState` ‚ÄĒ `Option<ConfettiAnim { particles: Vec<Particle>, started_at: Instant }>`. Particles = `(x: f32, y: f32, vx: f32, vy: f32, glyph: char, color: Color)`. ~30‚Äď50 particles. Update each frame: gravity (vy += 0.4), advance position, drop particles that fall offscreen. Render into the board area on top of the existing board (write spans into a layer drawn after `draw_board`). When all particles offscreen or 3s elapsed ‚Üí clear. Trigger: detect `prev_status == Ongoing && new_status != Ongoing` in `app.dispatch` (Local) and `app.tick_net` (Net). Drop `poll_ms` to 50 while `confetti_anim.is_some()` so animation runs ~20fps.

4. **chess-tui check banner extension**: add the same `is_in_check` line to `draw_sidebar_net` ‚ÄĒ the Net branch reads `view.in_check` (after the chess-core change above). Gate both Local + Net renders behind `app.show_check_banner` (default true; set false via `--no-check-banner`).

5. **chess-web banners**: copy the WIP-overlay pattern in `clients/chess-web/src/components/end_overlay.rs` (new). Component takes `view: Signal<PlayerView>` + `role: ClientRole`. When `view.status` transitions to Won/Drawn, show a centered card with VICTORY/DEFEAT/DRAW big text (h1 with CSS @keyframes pulse), winner side, reason. Auto-hide after 3s OR persist until dismiss button ‚ÄĒ recommend persist (user can reopen via "show again" link in sidebar). Spectators see neutral "Red wins / Black wins" never "VICTORY".

CSS @keyframes does the falling/rotation. Triggered by `create_effect` watching `view.status`. Auto-clears after 3s.

10. **Web picker polish**: add `<header class="hero">` block above `.picker-grid` in `picker.rs:11-15`. Inline SVG of a xiangqi piece + h1 + tagline. Style it in `style.css`. ~40 LOC including CSS.

# Implementation Plan: Victory Effects + Check Banner Toggles

## Task Sequence (Dependency Order)

### Phase 1 ‚ÄĒ Wire Protocol (foundation for both clients)

**1. Add `in_check` to `PlayerView`** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs`
- Add `pub in_check: bool` with `#[serde(default)]`. In `project()` (or wherever `PlayerView` is constructed), compute `state.is_in_check(observer_side)`. For Banqi/spectator-of-banqi, hard-code `false`.
- ~10 LOC.
- Risk: ensure `project()` is called once per observer; spectator side may need a deliberate "neutral" choice (recommend `false` for spectator since they're not in check).

**2. Bump protocol version to 4** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`
- Change `pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u32 = 3` ‚Üí `4`. Update changelog comment.
- ~3 LOC.
- Risk: confirm server-side handshake check is `==` vs `>=`; if `==`, mismatched v3 clients hard-fail (acceptable since `serde(default)` only protects field shape, not version negotiation).

**3. Tests for `in_check` projection** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs::tests`
- Add 4 small tests: ongoing-not-check, ongoing-in-check (xiangqi), banqi-always-false, post-checkmate state.
- ~40 LOC.

### Phase 2 ‚ÄĒ chess-tui

**4. ASCII banner module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs`
- Const arrays: `VICTORY_ART`, `DEFEAT_ART`, `DRAW_ART`, `CHECK_ART_EN`, `CHECK_ART_CN` (5‚Äď7 rows of `#`/`‚ĖĎ‚Ėď` block letters). Public fn `art_for(kind, style) -> &'static [&'static str]` honoring `Style::Ascii` vs `Style::Unicode`.
- ~120 LOC (mostly data).

**5. Confetti animation state + module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/confetti.rs`; touch `app.rs`
- Define `Particle { x, y, vx, vy, glyph: char, color: Color }` and `ConfettiAnim { particles: Vec<Particle>, started_at: Instant }`. Add `pub confetti_anim: Option<ConfettiAnim>` to `AppState` and `pub show_confetti: bool` + `pub show_check_banner: bool`.
- `confetti.rs` exports `spawn(area_w, area_h) -> ConfettiAnim` (30‚Äď50 particles, random vx in `[-0.6, 0.6]`, vy in `[-2.0, -0.8]`, glyphs `*+‚ú¶‚óÜ`, colors red/yellow/cyan). `step(&mut self)` applies `vy += 0.4`, advances pos, drops offscreen particles. Returns `done` when `Instant::elapsed() > 3s` OR `particles.is_empty()`.
- ~120 LOC.

# Confetti + Big Banner + Check Toggle (TUI & Web)

## Context

Right now both clients show end-of-game outcome as a few quiet lines in the sidebar (`game_over_banner` in chess-tui, `Sidebar` `<p class="status won">` in chess-web). The user wants wins/losses to feel obvious ‚ÄĒ a confetti effect plus a big "VICTORY / DEFEAT / DRAW" banner ‚ÄĒ and wants the existing "ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK" warning (already shown only in TUI Local for xiangqi) elevated and made toggleable across both clients. Also a small picker-page polish in the same PR. No new crate dependencies (banner ASCII art is hand-rolled; confetti uses ratatui spans / pure CSS).

Locked decisions from the clarifying round: hand-rolled ASCII art ‚ÄĘ check banner default ON ‚ÄĘ spectators get confetti with neutral "Red wins" copy (never "VICTORY") ‚ÄĘ menu polish in same PR but minimal.

## Approach

Three-phase rollout: chess-core wire change first, then chess-tui, then chess-web. Each phase is independently testable.

### Phase 1 ‚ÄĒ chess-core / chess-net (wire)

**1. Add `in_check` to `PlayerView`** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs`
- Add `pub in_check: bool` with `#[serde(default)]`. In `project()` (line 40), compute `state.is_in_check(observer)`. Returns `false` for Banqi naturally (existing `is_in_check` already handles missing-general).
- Tests: extend `view.rs::tests` with one xiangqi-not-in-check, one xiangqi-in-check, one banqi-always-false.
- ~15 LOC + 30 LOC tests.
- Wire-compatible with v3 clients via `serde(default)` ‚ÄĒ but bump anyway for clarity.

**2. Bump protocol** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`
- `pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u32 = 3` ‚Üí `4`. Update the doc-comment changelog block.
- Add a one-line ADR-0007 entry under `docs/adr/0007-spectator-check-flag.md` (3‚Äď5 sentences) noting the in_check field addition.

### Phase 2 ‚ÄĒ chess-tui

**3. Hand-rolled banner module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs`
- Five const arrays of 5‚Äď6 string rows: `VICTORY`, `DEFEAT`, `DRAW`, `CHECK_EN`, `CHECK_CN` (ŚįáŤĽć). Block-style using `‚Ėą‚Ėď‚ĖĎ` (CJK style) or `# - +` (ASCII style).
- `pub fn art(kind: BannerKind, style: Style) -> &'static [&'static str]`.
- ~150 LOC (mostly data).

**4. Confetti animation** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/confetti.rs` + `app.rs`, `main.rs`
- `Particle { x: f32, y: f32, vx: f32, vy: f32, glyph: char, color: Color }`. `ConfettiAnim { particles: Vec<Particle>, started_at: Instant }`.
- `spawn(area_w, area_h)` ‚ÄĒ 30‚Äď50 particles, vy in `[-2.0, -0.8]`, vx in `[-0.6, 0.6]`, glyphs `*‚ú¶‚óÜ‚óŹ+`, colors yellow/red/cyan/green.
- `step(&mut self) -> bool` (returns `done`): `vy += 0.4`, advance, drop offscreen, expire after 3s.
- AppState gets `pub confetti_anim: Option<ConfettiAnim>`, `pub show_confetti: bool`, `pub show_check_banner: bool`, `pub prev_status_local`/`prev_status_net` for transition detection.
- `main.rs:225` loop: when `confetti_anim.is_some()`, set `poll_ms = 50` (else 60/200 as today). Call `confetti_anim.step()` each iteration before `terminal.draw`.
- Trigger: in `app.dispatch` (Local) and `app.tick_net` (Net), detect `prev_status == Ongoing && new_status != Ongoing` ‚Üí `confetti_anim = Some(spawn(...))` if `show_confetti`.

**5. Game-over overlay + extend Net check banner** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`
- New `draw_game_over_overlay(frame, board_area, kind, role, style)` rendering ASCII art centered over the board (a `Paragraph` inside a sub-Rect). Lives ~3s ‚ÄĒ driven by the same `confetti_anim` lifetime so they appear together. After overlay collapses, existing sidebar `game_over_banner` (line 1173) keeps showing.
- Spectator branch picks neutral `BannerKind::Outcome { winner: Side }` rendering "RED WINS" instead of "VICTORY"/"DEFEAT".
- Render confetti particles **after** `draw_board` by writing into the frame's buffer (overwrite cells in board area). Skip cells outside `board_area`.
- Extend `draw_sidebar_net` (line 940): mirror the existing `is_in_check` line from `draw_sidebar` (line 884), reading from `view.in_check`. Gate both Local + Net renders behind `app.show_check_banner`.

**6. CLI flags** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs:40` (`Cli`) + `app.rs:212`
- `#[arg(long)] no_confetti: bool`, `#[arg(long)] no_check_banner: bool`. Wire `app.show_confetti = !cli.no_confetti`, `app.show_check_banner = !cli.no_check_banner`. Default both ON.

**7. TUI picker polish** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs:46 draw_picker`
- 5-row ASCII title block above current entries (use `banner::art(BannerKind::AppTitle, style)`). Adds `BannerKind::AppTitle` variant. ASCII fallback uses `+/-` chars.

### Phase 3 ‚ÄĒ chess-web

**8. Prefs module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs`; init in `app.rs`
- `RwSignal<bool>` √ó 2: `fx_confetti`, `fx_check_banner`. Hydrate from `localStorage` keys `chess.fx.confetti` / `chess.fx.checkBanner` (default `"1"`). Persist on change via `create_effect`. Provide via `provide_context(Prefs { ... })`. Wrap all `web_sys::window()` calls so native-stub crate keeps building.

**9. End-game overlay** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/components/end_overlay.rs`; mount in `pages/local.rs` + `pages/play.rs`
- Reuse WIP-overlay CSS (`style.css:201-228`) ‚ÄĒ copy `.endgame-overlay` / `.endgame-banner` from it. Big `<h1>` text "VICTORY"/"DEFEAT"/"DRAW" with `@keyframes pulse-banner`. Below: winner side + reason in smaller text. Auto-fades after 3s via `set_timeout` (kept simple; no dismiss button).
- Spectator (`role.is_spectator()`) renders neutral "Red wins" / "Black wins" ‚ÄĒ never "VICTORY"/"DEFEAT".
- Watches `view.status` via `create_effect` with a `prev_status` `RwSignal` owned by the page (survives remounts).

**10. CSS confetti + pulse** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web/style.css`
- `@keyframes confetti-fall` (translateY 0‚Üí110vh + rotate 0‚Üí720deg over 3s), `@keyframes pulse-banner` (scale 1‚ÜĒ1.05).
- `.confetti-container { position: fixed; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; z-index: 50; }`, `.confetti-particle` consumes `--x`, `--delay`, `--color`, `--rotate-end` CSS vars.
- ~60 LOC CSS.

**11. Confetti component** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/components/confetti.rs`
- Renders 40 `<div class="confetti-particle" style="--x: 23%; --delay: 0.2s; --color: #d43">`. Random params come from `js_sys::Math::random()` (already in WASM env). Mounted by `<EndOverlay>` only when `prefs.fx_confetti` is true. Auto-removes via the same 3s timer.

**12. Web check badge** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs:43-64`
- Add `<div class="check-badge">ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK</div>` above turn-row, shown when `prefs.fx_check_banner && view.in_check`. CSS uses the same `pulse-banner` @keyframes at lower amplitude.
- Local mode: feed `view` from `PlayerView::project(&state, side)` after each move (already happening per CLAUDE.md). The new `in_check` field flows through automatically.

**13. Web settings + picker hero** ‚ÄĒ sidebar (or new `settings_menu.rs`) + `pages/picker.rs:8`
- Two `<label><input type="checkbox">` toggles in sidebar footer bound to `prefs.fx_confetti` / `prefs.fx_check_banner`.
- Picker: add `<header class="hero">` above `.picker-grid` with inline SVG of a Śł• glyph + `<h1>Chinese Chess šł≠ŚúčŤĪ°ś£č</h1>` + tagline. ~50 LOC including CSS.

## Risks / callouts

- **Confetti in narrow terminals**: skip overlay if `board_area.width < art_max_width + 4`; just play confetti without big banner.
- **Net status-transition detection**: don't fire confetti on every server push ‚ÄĒ gate on `prev_status == Ongoing && new != Ongoing`. Reset `prev_status` on rematch.
- **Spectator UX**: `ClientRole::Spectator` already exists in `clients/chess-web/src/state.rs` (per CLAUDE.md); make sure overlay reads it from page state, not from a stale signal.
- **Wire compat**: `serde(default)` on `in_check` lets v3 clients deserialize v4 messages cleanly. The protocol bump is for clarity, not enforcement ‚ÄĒ the handshake already accepts version mismatches by reading what it understands.
- **localStorage in SSR / native**: gate every `web_sys::window()` access ‚ÄĒ prefs module must compile on `cargo check --workspace` (native stub).

## Approved Plan:
# Confetti + Big Banner + Check Toggle (TUI & Web)

## Context

Right now both clients show end-of-game outcome as a few quiet lines in the sidebar (`game_over_banner` in chess-tui, `Sidebar` `<p class="status won">` in chess-web). The user wants wins/losses to feel obvious ‚ÄĒ a confetti effect plus a big "VICTORY / DEFEAT / DRAW" banner ‚ÄĒ and wants the existing "ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK" warning (already shown only in TUI Local for xiangqi) elevated and made toggleable across both clients. Also a small picker-page polish in the same PR. No new crate dependencies (banner ASCII art is hand-rolled; confetti uses ratatui spans / pure CSS).

Locked decisions from the clarifying round: hand-rolled ASCII art ‚ÄĘ check banner default ON ‚ÄĘ spectators get confetti with neutral "Red wins" copy (never "VICTORY") ‚ÄĘ menu polish in same PR but minimal.

## Approach

Three-phase rollout: chess-core wire change first, then chess-tui, then chess-web. Each phase is independently testable.

### Phase 1 ‚ÄĒ chess-core / chess-net (wire)

**1. Add `in_check` to `PlayerView`** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs`
- Add `pub in_check: bool` with `#[serde(default)]`. In `project()` (line 40), compute `state.is_in_check(observer)`. Returns `false` for Banqi naturally (existing `is_in_check` already handles missing-general).
- Tests: extend `view.rs::tests` with one xiangqi-not-in-check, one xiangqi-in-check, one banqi-always-false.
- ~15 LOC + 30 LOC tests.
- Wire-compatible with v3 clients via `serde(default)` ‚ÄĒ but bump anyway for clarity.

**2. Bump protocol** ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`
- `pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u32 = 3` ‚Üí `4`. Update the doc-comment changelog block.
- Add a one-line ADR-0007 entry under `docs/adr/0007-spectator-check-flag.md` (3‚Äď5 sentences) noting the in_check field addition.

### Phase 2 ‚ÄĒ chess-tui

**3. Hand-rolled banner module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs`
- Five const arrays of 5‚Äď6 string rows: `VICTORY`, `DEFEAT`, `DRAW`, `CHECK_EN`, `CHECK_CN` (ŚįáŤĽć). Block-style using `‚Ėą‚Ėď‚ĖĎ` (CJK style) or `# - +` (ASCII style).
- `pub fn art(kind: BannerKind, style: Style) -> &'static [&'static str]`.
- ~150 LOC (mostly data).

**4. Confetti animation** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-tui/src/confetti.rs` + `app.rs`, `main.rs`
- `Particle { x: f32, y: f32, vx: f32, vy: f32, glyph: char, color: Color }`. `ConfettiAnim { particles: Vec<Particle>, started_at: Instant }`.
- `spawn(area_w, area_h)` ‚ÄĒ 30‚Äď50 particles, vy in `[-2.0, -0.8]`, vx in `[-0.6, 0.6]`, glyphs `*‚ú¶‚óÜ‚óŹ+`, colors yellow/red/cyan/green.
- `step(&mut self) -> bool` (returns `done`): `vy += 0.4`, advance, drop offscreen, expire after 3s.
- AppState gets `pub confetti_anim: Option<ConfettiAnim>`, `pub show_confetti: bool`, `pub show_check_banner: bool`, `pub prev_status_local`/`prev_status_net` for transition detection.
- `main.rs:225` loop: when `confetti_anim.is_some()`, set `poll_ms = 50` (else 60/200 as today). Call `confetti_anim.step()` each iteration before `terminal.draw`.
- Trigger: in `app.dispatch` (Local) and `app.tick_net` (Net), detect `prev_status == Ongoing && new_status != Ongoing` ‚Üí `confetti_anim = Some(spawn(...))` if `show_confetti`.

**5. Game-over overlay + extend Net check banner** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`
- New `draw_game_over_overlay(frame, board_area, kind, role, style)` rendering ASCII art centered over the board (a `Paragraph` inside a sub-Rect). Lives ~3s ‚ÄĒ driven by the same `confetti_anim` lifetime so they appear together. After overlay collapses, existing sidebar `game_over_banner` (line 1173) keeps showing.
- Spectator branch picks neutral `BannerKind::Outcome { winner: Side }` rendering "RED WINS" instead of "VICTORY"/"DEFEAT".
- Render confetti particles **after** `draw_board` by writing into the frame's buffer (overwrite cells in board area). Skip cells outside `board_area`.
- Extend `draw_sidebar_net` (line 940): mirror the existing `is_in_check` line from `draw_sidebar` (line 884), reading from `view.in_check`. Gate both Local + Net renders behind `app.show_check_banner`.

**6. CLI flags** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs:40` (`Cli`) + `app.rs:212`
- `#[arg(long)] no_confetti: bool`, `#[arg(long)] no_check_banner: bool`. Wire `app.show_confetti = !cli.no_confetti`, `app.show_check_banner = !cli.no_check_banner`. Default both ON.

**7. TUI picker polish** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs:46 draw_picker`
- 5-row ASCII title block above current entries (use `banner::art(BannerKind::AppTitle, style)`). Adds `BannerKind::AppTitle` variant. ASCII fallback uses `+/-` chars.

### Phase 3 ‚ÄĒ chess-web

**8. Prefs module** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs`; init in `app.rs`
- `RwSignal<bool>` √ó 2: `fx_confetti`, `fx_check_banner`. Hydrate from `localStorage` keys `chess.fx.confetti` / `chess.fx.checkBanner` (default `"1"`). Persist on change via `create_effect`. Provide via `provide_context(Prefs { ... })`. Wrap all `web_sys::window()` calls so native-stub crate keeps building.

**9. End-game overlay** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/components/end_overlay.rs`; mount in `pages/local.rs` + `pages/play.rs`
- Reuse WIP-overlay CSS (`style.css:201-228`) ‚ÄĒ copy `.endgame-overlay` / `.endgame-banner` from it. Big `<h1>` text "VICTORY"/"DEFEAT"/"DRAW" with `@keyframes pulse-banner`. Below: winner side + reason in smaller text. Auto-fades after 3s via `set_timeout` (kept simple; no dismiss button).
- Spectator (`role.is_spectator()`) renders neutral "Red wins" / "Black wins" ‚ÄĒ never "VICTORY"/"DEFEAT".
- Watches `view.status` via `create_effect` with a `prev_status` `RwSignal` owned by the page (survives remounts).

**10. CSS confetti + pulse** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web/style.css`
- `@keyframes confetti-fall` (translateY 0‚Üí110vh + rotate 0‚Üí720deg over 3s), `@keyframes pulse-banner` (scale 1‚ÜĒ1.05).
- `.confetti-container { position: fixed; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; z-index: 50; }`, `.confetti-particle` consumes `--x`, `--delay`, `--color`, `--rotate-end` CSS vars.
- ~60 LOC CSS.

**11. Confetti component** ‚ÄĒ new `clients/chess-web/src/components/confetti.rs`
- Renders 40 `<div class="confetti-particle" style="--x: 23%; --delay: 0.2s; --color: #d43">`. Random params come from `js_sys::Math::random()` (already in WASM env). Mounted by `<EndOverlay>` only when `prefs.fx_confetti` is true. Auto-removes via the same 3s timer.

**12. Web check badge** ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs:43-64`
- Add `<div class="check-badge">ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK</div>` above turn-row, shown when `prefs.fx_check_banner && view.in_check`. CSS uses the same `pulse-banner` @keyframes at lower amplitude.
- Local mode: feed `view` from `PlayerView::project(&state, side)` after each move (already happening per CLAUDE.md). The new `in_check` field flows through automatically.

**13. Web settings + picker hero** ‚ÄĒ sidebar (or new `settings_menu.rs`) + `pages/picker.rs:8`
- Two `<label><input type="checkbox">` toggles in sidebar footer bound to `prefs.fx_confetti` / `prefs.fx_check_banner`.
- Picker: add `<header class="hero">` above `.picker-grid` with inline SVG of a Śł• glyph + `<h1>Chinese Chess šł≠ŚúčŤĪ°ś£č</h1>` + tagline. ~50 LOC including CSS.

```text
1	//! Per-player view projection.
2	//!
3	//! `PlayerView` is the only state the network layer should serialize and
4	//! ship to clients ‚ÄĒ it has hidden-piece identities scrubbed and (for
5	//! non-side-to-move observers) no legal-move list to leak strategy.
6	//!
7	//! See ADR-0004.
8	
9	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
10	
11	use crate::board::BoardShape;
12	use crate::moves::{Move, MoveList};
13	use crate::piece::{PieceOnSquare, Side};
14	use crate::state::{GameState, GameStatus};
15	
16	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
17	pub enum VisibleCell {
18	    Empty,
19	    /// Banqi face-down: identity hidden from this observer.
20	    Hidden,
21	    Revealed(PieceOnSquare),
22	}
23	
24	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
25	pub struct PlayerView {
26	    pub observer: Side,
27	    pub shape: BoardShape,
28	    pub width: u8,
29	    pub height: u8,
30	    pub cells: Vec<VisibleCell>,
31	    pub side_to_move: Side,
32	    pub status: GameStatus,
33	    /// Legal moves for the observer if it's their turn; empty otherwise.
34	    pub legal_moves: MoveList,
35	}
36	
37	impl PlayerView {
38	    /// Project `state` from `observer`'s vantage point. Hidden pieces stay
39	    /// hidden; for opponents, legal-move list is empty.
40	    pub fn project(state: &GameState, observer: Side) -> Self {
41	        let board = &state.board;
42	        let cells: Vec<VisibleCell> = (0..board.cell_count())
43	            .map(|i| {
44	                let sq = crate::coord::Square(i as u16);
45	                match board.get(sq) {
46	                    None => VisibleCell::Empty,
47	                    Some(pos) if pos.revealed => VisibleCell::Revealed(pos),
48	                    Some(_) => VisibleCell::Hidden,
49	                }
50	            })
51	            .collect();
52	
53	        let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move {
54	            sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
55	        } else {
56	            MoveList::new()
57	        };
58	
59	        Self {
60	            observer,
61	            shape: board.shape(),
62	            width: board.width(),
63	            height: board.height(),
64	            cells,
65	            side_to_move: state.side_to_move,
66	            status: state.status,
67	            legal_moves,
68	        }
69	    }
70	}
71	
72	/// Strip identity from `Reveal` moves before they reach the network.
73	/// Even the side-to-move sees `revealed: None` because the engine resolves
74	/// the identity authoritatively when the move is applied.
75	fn sanitize_for_observer(moves: MoveList, _observer: Side) -> MoveList {
76	    moves
77	        .into_iter()
78	        .map(|m| match m {
79	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed: _ } => Move::Reveal { at, revealed: None },
80	            other => other,
81	        })
82	        .collect()
83	}
84	
85	#[cfg(test)]
86	mod tests {
87	    use super::*;
88	    use crate::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
89	
90	    #[test]
91	    fn fresh_banqi_view_has_all_hidden_for_any_observer() {
92	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 7));
93	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
94	        assert_eq!(view.cells.len(), 32);
95	        assert!(view.cells.iter().all(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Hidden)));
96	    }
97	
98	    #[test]
99	    fn xiangqi_view_shows_revealed_pieces() {
100	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
101	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
102	        let revealed: Vec<_> =
103	            view.cells.iter().filter(|c| matches!(c, VisibleCell::Revealed(_))).collect();
104	        assert_eq!(revealed.len(), 32);
105	    }
106	
107	    #[test]
108	    fn opponent_view_has_empty_legal_moves() {
109	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
110	        let red_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
111	        let black_view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::BLACK);
112	        assert!(!red_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "side-to-move sees moves");
113	        assert!(black_view.legal_moves.is_empty(), "opponent doesn't");
114	    }
115	
116	    #[test]
117	    fn reveal_moves_have_no_identity_in_view() {
118	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 1));
119	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
120	        for m in &view.legal_moves {
121	            if let Move::Reveal { revealed, .. } = m {
122	                assert!(revealed.is_none(), "Reveal payload must be stripped in view");
123	            }
124	        }
125	    }
126	
127	    #[test]
128	    fn no_hidden_identity_in_serialized_view() {
129	        // The smoke-level version of the proptest in tests/view_projection.rs.
130	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 42));
131	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
132	        let json = serde_json::to_string(&view).unwrap();
133	        // None of the piece-kind names should appear in any cell ‚ÄĒ they're all hidden.
134	        // (Empty cells trivially never contain a kind; we just confirm the doc-level
135	        // invariant.)
136	        for kind_name in ["General", "Advisor", "Elephant", "Chariot", "Horse", "Cannon", "Soldier"]
137	        {
138	            assert!(
139	                !json.contains(kind_name),
140	                "fresh banqi view must not leak any piece kind ({kind_name})"
141	            );
142	        }
143	    }
144	}
145
```

```text
230	    /// Whether `side`'s general is currently under attack by the opponent.
231	    /// Xiangqi-specific; banqi has no general-in-check concept.
232	    pub fn is_in_check(&self, side: Side) -> bool {
233	        crate::rules::xiangqi::is_in_check(self, side)
234	    }
235	
236	    /// Recompute `status` based on legal moves and draw counters.
237	    /// Caller invokes this after `make_move`; we don't auto-call to avoid
238	    /// recursing into move generation during legality filtering.
239	    ///
240	    /// TODO (PR 2): threefold repetition. Currently only no-progress and
241	    /// no-legal-moves are detected.
242	    pub fn refresh_status(&mut self) {
243	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
244	            return;
245	        }
246	        // Casual xiangqi: a missing general means the opposite side won.
247	        // Standard rules can't reach this branch (the legality filter
248	        // forbids leaving the general capturable), but checking unconditionally
249	        // is cheap and keeps the invariant for any future variant that allows
250	        // physical king capture.
251	        if self.rules.variant == Variant::Xiangqi {
252	            for &seat in self.turn_order.seats.iter() {
253	                let has_general = find_piece(&self.board, |p| {
254	                    p.kind == crate::piece::PieceKind::General && p.side == seat
255	                })
256	                .is_some();
257	                if !has_general {
258	                    self.status = GameStatus::Won {
259	                        winner: seat.opposite(),
260	                        reason: WinReason::GeneralCaptured,
261	                    };
262	                    return;
263	                }
264	            }
265	        }
266	        if self.no_progress_plies >= self.rules.draw_policy.no_progress_plies {
267	            self.status = GameStatus::Drawn { reason: DrawReason::NoProgress };
268	            return;
269	        }
270	        let moves = self.legal_moves();
271	        if moves.is_empty() {
272	            let me = self.side_to_move;
273	            self.status = match self.rules.variant {
274	                Variant::Xiangqi => {
275	                    let reason = if self.is_in_check(me) {
276	                        WinReason::Checkmate
277	                    } else {
278	                        // Asian rules: stalemate is a loss for the stalemated side.
279	                        WinReason::Stalemate
280	                    };
281	                    GameStatus::Won { winner: me.opposite(), reason }
282	                }
283	                Variant::Banqi => GameStatus::Won {
284	                    winner: me.opposite(),
285	                    reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
286	                },
287	                Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {
288	                    // PR 2: more nuanced 3-side win/elimination logic.
289	                    GameStatus::Won {
290	                        winner: me.opposite(),
291	                        reason: WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
292	                    }
293	                }
294	            };
295	        }
296	    }
```

```text
1	//! Wire protocol between `chess-net-server` and clients.
2	//!
3	//! Frames are JSON text frames, one message per frame. Tagged enums via
4	//! `#[serde(tag = "type")]` keep the schema obvious in `wscat`/`jq`.
5	//!
6	//! All payload types come straight from `chess-core` and already derive
7	//! `Serialize/Deserialize`, including the proptest-validated no-leak
8	//! projection in `PlayerView` (hidden banqi pieces stay opaque on the wire).
9	
10	use chess_core::moves::Move;
11	use chess_core::piece::Side;
12	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
13	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
14	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
15	
16	/// Bumped whenever the wire schema changes incompatibly.
17	///
18	/// v1 ‚Üí v2: added `ServerMsg::Rooms` and `ClientMsg::ListRooms` for the
19	/// multi-room lobby. Old (v1) clients still work against a v2 server in
20	/// the default room "main" ‚ÄĒ the new message variants are additive.
21	///
22	/// v2 ‚Üí v3: added `ServerMsg::{Spectating, ChatHistory, Chat}` and
23	/// `ClientMsg::Chat` for read-only spectators + in-room chat. v2 clients
24	/// keep working as players (they never request spectator role; the new
25	/// chat variants are additive). `RoomSummary` gains a `spectators` field
26	/// with `#[serde(default)]` so v2 lobby clients deserializing a v3 server
27	/// snapshot ignore the new column.
28	pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u16 = 3;
29	
30	/// Server ‚Üí client.
31	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
32	#[serde(tag = "type")]
33	pub enum ServerMsg {
34	    /// Sent once per client right after side assignment. Carries the variant
35	    /// (so the client knows what to render) plus the initial `PlayerView`.
36	    Hello { protocol: u16, observer: Side, rules: RuleSet, view: PlayerView },
37	    /// Sent to every seated client after each committed move.
38	    Update { view: PlayerView },
39	    /// Illegal move, room full, malformed message, opponent disconnect, etc.
40	    /// Non-fatal by default ‚ÄĒ clients should display and keep the connection.
41	    Error { message: String },
42	    /// Pushed on the lobby socket only ‚ÄĒ initial snapshot on connect, then
43	    /// again whenever any room's state changes (seat insertion / removal /
44	    /// game finish / GC). Game sockets never receive this variant.
45	    Rooms { rooms: Vec<RoomSummary> },
46	    /// Spectator-side counterpart to `Hello` ‚ÄĒ no `observer` because
47	    /// spectators are not seated. Carries the same `rules` + initial `view`
48	    /// (projected from `Side::RED`'s perspective so banqi hidden tiles stay
49	    /// hidden). Sent immediately after a `?role=spectator` connection
50	    /// upgrades successfully.
```

```text
1	# ADR 0006 ‚ÄĒ chess-net spectators + in-room chat (protocol v3)
2	
3	Status: accepted (shipped 2026-05-07)
4	Supersedes: nothing
5	Related: ADR-0004 (PlayerView projection), ADR-0005 (multi-room lobby)
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	After ADR-0005 the chess-net server runs multiple rooms but each one is a
10	hard 2-seat box: the third connection gets `Error{"room full"}` and is
11	dropped. There's no in-room chat either ‚ÄĒ once seated, the only client ‚Üí
12	server messages are `Move`, `Resign`, `Rematch`, `ListRooms`. Two natural
13	follow-ups landed in the same PR:
14	
15	1. Let third-and-beyond connections join as **read-only spectators** that
16	   see the board + chat but cannot move, resign, or chat.
17	2. Add a small **chat channel** between the seated players (and replayed to
18	   spectators) so opponents can talk during a game.
19	
20	Three integration shapes were considered for spectator joins:
21	
22	A. **Auto-fallback**: if the room is full and the client connects without a
23	   role flag, upgrade them to spectator silently.
24	B. **Explicit opt-in** via `?role=spectator`.
25	C. **Sniff the User-Agent** for `chess-tui/3+` and only auto-fallback then.
26	
27	For chat permissions:
28	
29	D. Players-only ‚ÄĒ spectators read but can't post.
30	E. Everyone in the room can chat (incl. spectators).
31	F. Players-only and spectators don't see the chat panel at all.
32	
33	## Decision
34	
35	**Spectator opt-in**: pick **B**. v2 clients never set `?role=spectator`,
36	so the existing "room full" path is preserved byte-identically. v3 clients
37	explicitly opt in (lobby Watch button ‚Üí URL with the param). The
38	`User-Agent` sniff (Option C) was rejected as a magic side-channel that's
39	hard to reason about.
40
```
</details></tool-use>

```md
# ADR 0007 ‚ÄĒ `PlayerView.in_check` flag (protocol v4)

Status: accepted (shipped 2026-05-07)
Supersedes: nothing
Related: ADR-0004 (PlayerView projection), ADR-0006 (spectators + chat)

## Context

`GameState::is_in_check(side)` has always been public on `chess-core`, but
the chess-tui local renderer was the only consumer (`ui.rs::draw_sidebar`
shows a "‚ö† CHECK ŚįáŤĽć" line when the side-to-move's general is attacked).
Two new clients want the same hint:

1. chess-tui in **Net mode**: `draw_sidebar_net` only has a `PlayerView` in
   hand, not a `GameState`, so it cannot call `is_in_check` itself.
2. chess-web (both Local and Net): the WASM crate operates on `PlayerView`
   for parity between modes.

Three options were considered:

A. **Send the full `GameState` over the wire** ‚ÄĒ rejected, breaks ADR-0004
   (banqi hidden-piece identity must never leave the server).
B. **Recompute on the client** by porting `is_in_check` to operate on
   `PlayerView` ‚ÄĒ possible (xiangqi has no hidden pieces), but doubles the
   surface area for a check-detection bug and risks server/client drift.
C. **Project `in_check` at the server and ship it as a derived field on
   `PlayerView`** ‚ÄĒ server already runs `is_in_check` during status refresh,
   so the marginal cost is one bool per `Update` frame.

## Decision

Pick C. Add `pub in_check: bool` (with `#[serde(default)]`) to `PlayerView`,
computed in `PlayerView::project()` as `state.is_in_check(observer)`. The
field is wire-compatible with v3 ‚ÄĒ older clients deserialize unknown fields
as ignored, and older servers' messages deserialize cleanly into v4 clients
with `in_check = false`. Bump `PROTOCOL_VERSION` to 4 for documentation.

For non-xiangqi variants, `is_in_check` already returns `false` (banqi has
no general), so observers always read `in_check = false` ‚ÄĒ no variant gate
in `project()`. Spectators project from `Side::RED` (per ADR-0006); they'll
see `in_check = true` whenever Red is in check and `false` otherwise. UIs
treating spectators as neutral observers should ignore the flag entirely
when rendering banners.

## Consequences

- One extra `bool` per server message ‚ÄĒ negligible bandwidth.
- `chess-tui` Net mode and `chess-web` (Local + Net) now share the same
  banner-trigger condition: `view.in_check && variant == Xiangqi`.
- Future variants that introduce a check-like concept (e.g. three-kingdom
  banqi if it grows a general) get the field for free once `is_in_check`
  learns about them.
- Adds the field to the `view_projection.rs` proptest's no-leak surface;
  `bool` carries no piece identity so the proptest stays happy without
  edits.

```

```rs
//! Hand-rolled block-letter ASCII art for end-of-game and check banners.
//!
//! No `figlet-rs` dep ‚ÄĒ the strings rendered are fixed (VICTORY, DEFEAT,
//! DRAW, CHECK, ŚįáŤĽć, the app title), so embedding the rasterised rows
//! is cheap and keeps the dependency graph clean. CJK style uses ‚ĖĎ‚Ėí‚Ėď
//! block fills; ASCII style uses `#` so monochrome terminals still read.
//!
//! Each `&'static [&'static str]` is the rasterised letters as one row
//! per `&'static str`. Renderers join with `'\n'` and centre.
//!
//! Width characters are ASCII only (no CJK) so each `char` is one terminal
//! cell ‚ÄĒ `width()` is `s.chars().count()`.
//!
//! See `confetti.rs` for the matching particle effect and
//! `ui::draw_game_over_overlay` for the renderer that consumes both.
//!
//! IMPORTANT: keep all rows of a given variant the same width so that
//! centering is trivial; the renderer trusts row 0's width.

use crate::glyph::Style;

#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
pub enum BannerKind {
    /// Player won (CJK: ŚčĚŚą©, ASCII: VICTORY).
    Victory,
    /// Player lost (DEFEAT / śēóŚĆó).
    Defeat,
    /// Game ended in a draw (DRAW / ŚíĆś£č).
    Draw,
    /// Spectator-style neutral outcome ‚ÄĒ caller passes the winner side.
    Outcome(NeutralSide),
    /// ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK.
    Check,
    /// chess-tui picker title.
    AppTitle,
}

/// Spectator banner uses neutral wording ("RED WINS", not "VICTORY").
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
pub enum NeutralSide {
    Red,
    Black,
    Green,
}

pub fn art(kind: BannerKind, style: Style) -> &'static [&'static str] {
    match (kind, style) {
        (BannerKind::Victory, Style::Cjk) => VICTORY_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::Victory, Style::Ascii) => VICTORY_ASCII,
        (BannerKind::Defeat, Style::Cjk) => DEFEAT_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::Defeat, Style::Ascii) => DEFEAT_ASCII,
        (BannerKind::Draw, Style::Cjk) => DRAW_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::Draw, Style::Ascii) => DRAW_ASCII,
        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Red), Style::Cjk) => RED_WINS_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Red), Style::Ascii) => RED_WINS_ASCII,
        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Black), Style::Cjk) => BLACK_WINS_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Black), Style::Ascii) => BLACK_WINS_ASCII,
        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Green), Style::Cjk) => GREEN_WINS_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Green), Style::Ascii) => GREEN_WINS_ASCII,
        (BannerKind::Check, Style::Cjk) => CHECK_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::Check, Style::Ascii) => CHECK_ASCII,
        (BannerKind::AppTitle, Style::Cjk) => APP_TITLE_BLOCK,
        (BannerKind::AppTitle, Style::Ascii) => APP_TITLE_ASCII,
    }
}

/// Maximum row width for a banner ‚ÄĒ useful for layout fits-check before
/// committing to the overlay (in narrow terminals we skip the overlay).
pub fn max_width(rows: &[&str]) -> usize {
    rows.iter().map(|r| r.chars().count()).max().unwrap_or(0)
}

// ---- CJK / block-fill style (use ‚ĖĎ‚Ėí‚Ėď shading) ---------------------------
```
