#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn each_banner_has_consistent_row_widths() {
        for (kind, style) in [
            (BannerKind::Victory, Style::Cjk),
            (BannerKind::Victory, Style::Ascii),
            (BannerKind::Defeat, Style::Cjk),
            (BannerKind::Defeat, Style::Ascii),
            (BannerKind::Draw, Style::Cjk),
            (BannerKind::Draw, Style::Ascii),
            (BannerKind::Check, Style::Cjk),
            (BannerKind::Check, Style::Ascii),
            (BannerKind::AppTitle, Style::Cjk),
            (BannerKind::AppTitle, Style::Ascii),
            (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Red), Style::Cjk),
            (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Black), Style::Cjk),
            (BannerKind::Outcome(NeutralSide::Green), Style::Cjk),
        ] {
            let rows = art(kind, style);
            assert!(!rows.is_empty(), "{kind:?}/{style:?}: empty");
            let w = rows[0].chars().count();
            for (i, r) in rows.iter().enumerate() {
                assert_eq!(r.chars().count(), w, "{kind:?}/{style:?} row {i} width drift");
            }
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn max_width_returns_widest_row() {
        let rows: &[&str] = &["abc", "abcdef", "ab"];
        assert_eq!(max_width(rows), 6);
    }
}

```rs
//! Confetti animation: a tiny particle system written specifically for the
//! ratatui draw loop. No `rand` crate dep ‚ÄĒ a small linear-congruential RNG
//! seeded from `Instant::now()` is plenty for ~40 visual particles spawned
//! once per game-end.
//!
//! Lifecycle:
//!  1. `ConfettiAnim::spawn(area)` ‚ÄĒ caller sees a status transition and
//!     creates the animation. Particles start near the top edge of the
//!     board area, biased upward (negative `vy`) so the burst rises before
//!     gravity pulls it down.
//!  2. `step()` advances each particle by `(vx, vy)` and applies gravity.
//!     Particles that leave the area on any side are dropped. Returns the
//!     `done` flag ‚ÄĒ true once the lifetime cap (3s) elapses or the vec is
//!     empty.
//!  3. `render(buf, area)` writes glyphs into the ratatui `Buffer` cells
//!     they cover. Multiple particles in the same cell silently overwrite
//!     (last write wins) ‚ÄĒ no flicker because we step + draw in one frame.
//!
//! The renderer also draws a centered ASCII-art banner from `banner.rs`
//! during the same window; together they make the game-over moment feel
//! emphatic without us shipping a sprite engine.

use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

use ratatui::buffer::Buffer;
use ratatui::layout::Rect;
use ratatui::style::{Color, Modifier, Style};

const LIFETIME: Duration = Duration::from_millis(3000);
const SPAWN_COUNT: usize = 48;
const GRAVITY: f32 = 0.35;
const GLYPHS: &[char] = &['*', '+', '‚ú¶', '‚úß', '‚óÜ', '‚óŹ', '‚úļ'];
const COLORS: &[Color] = &[
    Color::Yellow,
    Color::LightRed,
    Color::LightCyan,
    Color::LightGreen,
    Color::LightMagenta,
    Color::White,
];

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct Particle {
    pub x: f32,
    pub y: f32,
    pub vx: f32,
    pub vy: f32,
    pub glyph: char,
    pub color: Color,
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct ConfettiAnim {
    pub particles: Vec<Particle>,
    started_at: Instant,
}

impl ConfettiAnim {
    /// Spawn a fresh burst sized to fit `area`. The caller decides where the
    /// area is ‚ÄĒ typically the board sub-rect.
    pub fn spawn(area: Rect) -> Self {
        let now = Instant::now();
        let mut rng = LcgRng::seed_from_instant(now);
        let mut particles = Vec::with_capacity(SPAWN_COUNT);
        let cx = area.x as f32 + area.width as f32 / 2.0;
        let cy = area.y as f32 + area.height as f32 / 2.0;
        for _ in 0..SPAWN_COUNT {
            // Spread origin across the upper half of the area so the burst
            // doesn't all stem from a single cell.
            let x = cx + rng.range(-(area.width as f32) / 3.0, area.width as f32 / 3.0);
            let y = cy + rng.range(-(area.height as f32) / 4.0, 0.0);
            let vx = rng.range(-0.7, 0.7);
            let vy = rng.range(-1.6, -0.5);
            let glyph = GLYPHS[rng.usize_below(GLYPHS.len())];
            let color = COLORS[rng.usize_below(COLORS.len())];
            particles.push(Particle { x, y, vx, vy, glyph, color });
        }
        Self { particles, started_at: now }
    }

    /// Advance one frame. Returns `true` once the lifetime is exhausted or
    /// every particle has left the area ‚ÄĒ the caller should drop the anim
    /// when this is true.
    pub fn step(&mut self, area: Rect) -> bool {
        for p in self.particles.iter_mut() {
            p.x += p.vx;
            p.y += p.vy;
            p.vy += GRAVITY;
        }
        // Drop offscreen particles.
        let x_min = area.x as f32 - 1.0;
        let x_max = (area.x + area.width) as f32 + 1.0;
        let y_max = (area.y + area.height) as f32 + 1.0;
        self.particles
            .retain(|p| p.x >= x_min && p.x <= x_max && p.y <= y_max);
        self.is_done()
    }

    pub fn is_done(&self) -> bool {
        self.particles.is_empty() || self.started_at.elapsed() >= LIFETIME
    }

    /// Write particles into the buffer cells they cover. Cells outside
    /// `area` are skipped defensively ‚ÄĒ the spawn / step logic should keep
    /// particles inside, but a resize between frames could shrink the area.
    pub fn render(&self, buf: &mut Buffer, area: Rect, use_color: bool) {
        for p in &self.particles {
            let x = p.x.round() as i32;
            let y = p.y.round() as i32;
            if x < area.x as i32
                || x >= (area.x + area.width) as i32
                || y < area.y as i32
                || y >= (area.y + area.height) as i32
            {
                continue;
            }
            let cell = buf.get_mut(x as u16, y as u16);
            cell.set_char(p.glyph);
            if use_color {
                cell.set_style(Style::default().fg(p.color).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD));
            } else {
                cell.set_style(Style::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD));
            }
        }
    }
}

// ---- Tiny LCG -----------------------------------------------------------
//
// xorshift32 is just enough for visual jitter; it is not cryptographic and
// not seeded for reproducibility. We seed from `Instant` so each spawn
// looks different.

struct LcgRng(u32);

impl LcgRng {
    fn seed_from_instant(t: Instant) -> Self {
        // Use the elapsed-since-program-start nanos mod u32 as a seed. Two
        // bursts in the same millisecond would still differ because nanos
        // drift; if seed lands on 0 (impossibly rare) we substitute 1.
        let nanos = t.elapsed().as_nanos() as u32;
        let s = if nanos == 0 { 0x9E3779B9 } else { nanos };
        LcgRng(s)
    }

    fn next_u32(&mut self) -> u32 {
        let mut x = self.0;
        x ^= x << 13;
        x ^= x >> 17;
        x ^= x << 5;
        if x == 0 {
            x = 0x9E3779B9;
        }
        self.0 = x;
        x
    }

    fn unit_f32(&mut self) -> f32 {
        (self.next_u32() as f32) / (u32::MAX as f32)
    }

    fn range(&mut self, lo: f32, hi: f32) -> f32 {
        lo + self.unit_f32() * (hi - lo)
    }

    fn usize_below(&mut self, n: usize) -> usize {
        if n == 0 {
            return 0;
        }
        (self.next_u32() as usize) % n
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    fn area() -> Rect {
        Rect::new(0, 0, 60, 30)
    }

    #[test]
    fn spawn_creates_particles() {
        let anim = ConfettiAnim::spawn(area());
        assert!(!anim.particles.is_empty());
        assert!(anim.particles.len() <= SPAWN_COUNT);
        // All particles start inside or just above the area horizontally.
        for p in &anim.particles {
            assert!(p.x >= -1.0 && p.x <= 70.0, "x out of plausible bounds: {}", p.x);
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn step_eventually_clears_or_exits_lifetime() {
        let mut anim = ConfettiAnim::spawn(area());
        // 60 frames at default gravity should push everything off the bottom.
        for _ in 0..200 {
            if anim.step(area()) {
                break;
            }
        }
        assert!(anim.is_done(), "anim should be done after enough frames");
    }

    #[test]
    fn render_does_not_panic_on_resize() {
        let big = area();
        let small = Rect::new(0, 0, 5, 5);
        let mut anim = ConfettiAnim::spawn(big);
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(small);
        // Particles spawned for `big` should be filtered when rendering into `small`.
        anim.render(&mut buf, small, true);
    }
}

332	    pub fn new_lobby(host: String, style: Style, use_color: bool, observer: Side) -> Self {

354	
355	    /// Drain ws events from the worker thread(s) and apply them to the
356	    /// active `NetView` / `LobbyView`. Called once per main-loop tick
357	    /// (no-op outside Net / Lobby modes).
358	    pub fn tick_net(&mut self) {
359	        match &mut self.screen {
360	            Screen::Net(n) => {
361	                while let Ok(evt) = n.client.evt_rx.try_recv() {
362	                    apply_net_event(n, evt);
363	                }
364	            }
365	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
366	                while let Ok(evt) = l.client.evt_rx.try_recv() {
367	                    apply_lobby_event(l, evt);
368	                }
369	            }
370	            _ => {}
371	        }
372	    }
373	
374	    /// Compute the input mode for the current screen so main.rs can drive
375	    /// `from_key` without poking at private state.
376	    pub fn input_mode(&self) -> InputMode {
377	        match &self.screen {
378	            Screen::Picker(_) => InputMode::Picker,
379	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
380	                if l.pending_join.is_some() {
381	                    InputMode::Text
382	                } else {
383	                    InputMode::Lobby
384	                }
385	            }
386	            Screen::HostPrompt(_) | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => InputMode::Text,
387	            // While typing a chat line or a coord move, hijack the keymap so
388	            // printable chars append to the buffer instead of moving the cursor.
389	            Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() || n.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
390	            Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
391	            Screen::Game(_) | Screen::Net(_) => InputMode::Game,
392	        }
393	    }
394	
395	    pub fn dispatch(&mut self, action: Action) {
396	        match action {
397	            Action::None => {}
398	            Action::ConfirmYes => {
399	                self.quit_confirm_open = false;
400	                self.should_quit = true;
401	            }
402	            Action::ConfirmNo => self.quit_confirm_open = false,
403	            Action::Quit => {
404	                if self.is_game_in_progress() {
405	                    self.quit_confirm_open = true;
406	                } else {
407	                    self.should_quit = true;
408	                }
409	            }
410	            Action::HelpToggle => self.help_open = !self.help_open,
411	            Action::RulesToggle => self.rules_open = !self.rules_open,
412	            Action::NewGame => {
413	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Net(_)) {
414	                    // In Net mode, 'n' requests a rematch via the server
415	                    // instead of dropping the connection. Game must be over.
416	                    if let Screen::Net(n) = &mut self.screen {
417	                        if !n.role.map(|r| r.is_player()).unwrap_or(false) {
418	                            n.last_msg = Some("Spectators cannot request a rematch.".into());
419	                            return;
420	                        }
421	                        let status = n.last_view.as_ref().map(|v| v.status);
422	                        match status {
423	                            Some(GameStatus::Won { .. }) | Some(GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) => {
424	                                let _ = n.client.cmd_tx.send(ClientMsg::Rematch);
425	                                n.last_msg =
426	                                    Some("Rematch requested. Waiting for opponent‚Ä¶".into());
427	                            }
428	                            Some(GameStatus::Ongoing) => {
429	                                n.last_msg = Some(
430	                                    "'n' requests a rematch only after the game is over.".into(),
431	                                );
432	                            }
433	                            None => {
434	                                n.last_msg = Some("Not connected yet.".into());
435	                            }
436	                        }
437	                    }
438	                } else {
439	                    let style = self.style;
440	                    let use_color = self.use_color;
441	                    let observer = self.observer;
442	                    *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
443	                }
444	            }
445	            Action::Back => self.dispatch_back(),
446	            Action::PickerUp | Action::PickerDown | Action::PickerSelect => match &self.screen {
447	                Screen::Picker(_) => self.dispatch_picker(action),
448	                Screen::Lobby(_) => self.dispatch_lobby(action),
449	                _ => {}
450	            },
451	            Action::LobbyCreate | Action::LobbyRefresh => {
452	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
453	                    self.dispatch_lobby(action);
454	                }
455	            }
456	            Action::TextInput(_)
457	            | Action::TextBackspace
458	            | Action::FocusNext
459	            | Action::FocusPrev
460	            | Action::Submit => self.dispatch_text(action),
461	            Action::ChatStart => self.dispatch_chat_start(),
462	            Action::CoordStart(kind) => self.dispatch_coord_start(kind),
463	            Action::LobbyWatch => {
464	                if matches!(self.screen, Screen::Lobby(_)) {
465	                    self.dispatch_lobby_watch();
466	                }
467	            }
468	            _ => match &self.screen {
469	                Screen::Net(_) => self.dispatch_net(action),
470	                Screen::Game(_) => self.dispatch_game(action),
471	                Screen::Picker(_)
472	                | Screen::HostPrompt(_)
473	                | Screen::Lobby(_)
474	                | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => {}
475	            },
476	        }
477	    }
478	
479	    fn dispatch_back(&mut self) {
480	        let style = self.style;
481	        let use_color = self.use_color;
482	        let observer = self.observer;
483	        match &mut self.screen {
484	            Screen::HostPrompt(_) => {
485	                *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
486	            }
487	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
488	                if l.pending_join.is_some() {
489	                    l.pending_join = None;
490	                    l.last_msg = None;
491	                    return;
492	                }
493	                *self = AppState::new_picker(style, use_color, observer);
494	            }
495	            Screen::CreateRoom(c) => {
496	                let host = c.host.clone();
497	                *self = AppState::new_lobby(host, style, use_color, observer);
498	            }
499	            // Esc inside Game / Net while a coord-input prompt is open: close
500	            // the prompt and (Live mode only) restore the snapshotted cursor +
501	            // selected highlight that existed before the prompt was opened.
502	            Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some() => {
503	                let ci = g.coord_input.take().expect("guarded by is_some");
504	                if let Some((cur, sel)) = ci.snapshot {
505	                    g.cursor = cur;
506	                    g.selected = sel;
507	                }
508	                g.last_msg = None;
509	            }
510	            Screen::Net(n) if n.coord_input.is_some() => {
511	                let ci = n.coord_input.take().expect("guarded by is_some");
512	                if let Some((cur, sel)) = ci.snapshot {
513	                    n.cursor = cur;
514	                    n.selected = sel;
515	                }
516	                n.last_msg = None;
517	            }
518	            // Esc inside Net mode ‚ÄĒ if we're typing a chat line, cancel the
519	            // input rather than navigating away.

170	fn build_banqi_rules(
171	    preset: Option<&PresetArg>,
172	    house: Option<&str>,
173	    seed: Option<u64>,
174	) -> Result<RuleSet> {
175	    let mut flags = HouseRules::empty();
176	    if let Some(p) = preset {
177	        flags |= match p {
178	            PresetArg::Purist => chess_core::rules::PRESET_PURIST,
179	            PresetArg::Taiwan => chess_core::rules::PRESET_TAIWAN,
180	            PresetArg::Aggressive => chess_core::rules::PRESET_AGGRESSIVE,
181	        };
182	    }
183	    if let Some(s) = house {
184	        for tok in s.split(',') {
185	            flags |= match tok.trim() {
186	                "chain" => HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE,
187	                "dark" | "dark-chain" => HouseRules::DARK_CHAIN,
188	                "rush" | "chariot-rush" => HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH,
189	                "horse-diagonal" | "diag" => HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL,
190	                "cannon-fast" | "fast-cannon" => HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE,
191	                other => return Err(anyhow!("unknown house rule: {other}")),
192	            };
193	        }
194	    }
195	    let flags = chess_core::rules::house::normalize(flags);
196	    Ok(match seed {
197	        Some(s) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(flags, s),
198	        None => RuleSet::banqi(flags),
199	    })
200	}

206	    result

217	}

225	}

1428	fn apply_net_event(n: &mut NetView, evt: NetEvent) {
1429	    match evt {
1430	        NetEvent::Connected => {
1431	            n.connected = true;
1432	            n.last_msg = Some("Connected. Waiting for welcome‚Ä¶".into());
1433	        }
1434	        NetEvent::Server(boxed) => match *boxed {
1435	            ServerMsg::Hello { observer, rules, view, .. } => {
1436	                let was_seated = n.role.is_some();
1437	                n.role = Some(NetRole::Player(observer));
1438	                n.rules = Some(rules);
1439	                let shape = view.shape;
1440	                let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
1441	                n.cursor = (rows / 2, cols / 2);
1442	                n.selected = None;
1443	                n.last_view = Some(view);
1444	                n.connected = true;
1445	                // First Hello = "Joined as X". Subsequent Hello = rematch reset.
1446	                n.last_msg = Some(if was_seated {
1447	                    "Rematch ‚ÄĒ new game.".into()
1448	                } else {
1449	                    format!("Joined as {}.", side_label(observer))
1450	                });
1451	            }
1452	            ServerMsg::Spectating { rules, view, .. } => {
1453	                let was_welcomed = n.role.is_some();
1454	                n.role = Some(NetRole::Spectator);
1455	                n.rules = Some(rules);
1456	                let shape = view.shape;
1457	                let (rows, cols) = orient::display_dims(shape);
1458	                n.cursor = (rows / 2, cols / 2);
1459	                n.selected = None;
1460	                n.last_view = Some(view);
1461	                n.connected = true;
1462	                n.last_msg = Some(if was_welcomed {
1463	                    "Rematch ‚ÄĒ new game (spectating).".into()
1464	                } else {
1465	                    "Joined as spectator (read-only).".into()
1466	                });
1467	            }
1468	            ServerMsg::Update { view } => {
1469	                n.last_view = Some(view);
1470	                if n.last_msg.as_deref() == Some("Sent.") {
1471	                    n.last_msg = None;
1472	                }
1473	            }
1474	            ServerMsg::ChatHistory { lines } => {
1475	                n.chat.clear();
1476	                for line in lines.into_iter().take(CHAT_HISTORY_CAP) {
1477	                    n.chat.push_back(line);
1478	                }
1479	            }
1480	            ServerMsg::Chat { line } => {
1481	                n.chat.push_back(line);
1482	                while n.chat.len() > CHAT_HISTORY_CAP {
1483	                    n.chat.pop_front();
1484	                }
1485	            }
1486	            ServerMsg::Error { message } => {
1487	                n.last_msg = Some(message);
1488	            }
1489	            ServerMsg::Rooms { .. } => {
1490	                n.last_msg = Some("(unexpected lobby payload on game socket)".into());
1491	            }
1492	        },
1493	        NetEvent::Disconnected(reason) => {
1494	            n.connected = false;
1495	            n.last_msg = Some(format!("Disconnected: {reason}"));
1496	        }
1497	    }
1498	}

480	    /// Compute the input mode for the current screen so main.rs can drive
481	    /// `from_key` without poking at private state.
482	    pub fn input_mode(&self) -> InputMode {
483	        match &self.screen {
484	            Screen::Picker(_) => InputMode::Picker,
485	            Screen::Lobby(l) => {
486	                if l.pending_join.is_some() {
487	                    InputMode::Text
488	                } else {
489	                    InputMode::Lobby
490	                }
491	            }
492	            Screen::HostPrompt(_) | Screen::CreateRoom(_) => InputMode::Text,
493	            // While typing a chat line or a coord move, hijack the keymap so
494	            // printable chars append to the buffer instead of moving the cursor.
495	            Screen::Net(n) if n.chat_input.is_some() || n.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
496	            Screen::Game(g) if g.coord_input.is_some() => InputMode::Text,
497	            Screen::Game(_) | Screen::Net(_) => InputMode::Game,
498	        }
499	    }

513	                    self.should_quit = true;
514	                }
515	            }

27	pub fn draw(frame: &mut Frame, app: &mut AppState) {
28	    let area = frame.area();
29	    match &app.screen {
30	        Screen::Picker(p) => draw_picker(frame, area, p),
31	        Screen::Game(_) => draw_game(frame, area, app),
32	        Screen::Net(_) => draw_net(frame, area, app),
33	        Screen::HostPrompt(h) => draw_host_prompt(frame, area, h, app.help_open),
34	        Screen::Lobby(l) => draw_lobby(frame, area, l, app.help_open),
35	        Screen::CreateRoom(c) => draw_create_room(frame, area, c),
36	    }
37	
38	    if app.rules_open {
39	        draw_rules_overlay(frame, area, app.style);
40	    }
41	    if app.quit_confirm_open {
42	        draw_quit_confirm_overlay(frame, area);
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	fn draw_picker(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, picker: &PickerView) {
47	    let title = Span::styled("chess-tui", TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD));
48	    let mut lines = vec![
49	        Line::from(title),
50	        Line::from(Span::raw("Choose a variant. Arrow keys + Enter; q to quit.")),
51	        Line::from(""),
52	    ];
53	    for (i, entry) in PickerEntry::ALL.iter().enumerate() {
54	        let prefix = if i == picker.cursor { "‚Ė∂ " } else { "  " };
55	        let style = if i == picker.cursor {
56	            TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)
57	        } else {
58	            TuiStyle::default()
59	        };
60	        lines.push(Line::from(vec![Span::raw(prefix), Span::styled(entry.label(), style)]));
61	    }
62	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Welcome ");
63	    let para = Paragraph::new(lines).block(block).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
64	    frame.render_widget(para, area);
65	}
66	
67	fn draw_game(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, app: &mut AppState) {
68	    let chunks = Layout::default()
69	        .direction(Direction::Horizontal)
70	        .constraints([Constraint::Min(40), Constraint::Length(36)])
71	        .split(area);
72	
73	    let Screen::Game(g) = &app.screen else {
74	        return;
75	    };
76	    let g_ref: &GameView = g.as_ref();
77	    let observer = app.observer;

79	    let use_color = app.use_color;
80	    let help_open = app.help_open;
81	
82	    let view = PlayerView::project(&g_ref.state, g_ref.state.side_to_move);
83	    let mut rect = app.board_rect;

86	        chunks[0],

88	        style,
89	        use_color,
90	        &view,
91	        g_ref.cursor,

93	        &mut rect,
94	    );
95	    app.board_rect = rect;
96	    draw_sidebar(frame, chunks[1], g_ref, &view, style, help_open);
97	}

834	fn draw_sidebar(
835	    frame: &mut Frame,
836	    area: Rect,
837	    g: &GameView,
838	    view: &PlayerView,
839	    style: Style,
840	    help_open: bool,
841	) {
842	    let block = Block::default().borders(Borders::ALL).title(" Status ");
843	    let inner = block.inner(area);
844	    frame.render_widget(block, area);
845	
846	    let mut lines: Vec<Line> = Vec::new();
847	
848	    // Game-over banner (takes priority).
849	    if let Some(banner) = game_over_banner(&view.status, style) {
850	        for l in banner {
851	            lines.push(l);
852	        }
853	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
854	    }
855	
856	    let variant_label = match view.shape {
857	        BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10 => {
858	            // Casual is the TUI default; strict is the alternative.
859	            if g.state.rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check {
860	                "Xiangqi (ŤĪ°ś£č)"
861	            } else {
862	                "Xiangqi (ŤĪ°ś£č, strict)"
863	            }
864	        }
865	        BoardShape::Banqi4x8 => "Banqi (śöóś£č)",
866	        BoardShape::ThreeKingdom => "šłČŚúčśöóś£č",
867	        BoardShape::Custom { .. } => "Custom",
868	    };
869	    lines.push(line_label_value("Variant:", variant_label));
870	
871	    // Side-to-move only makes sense while the game is ongoing; once it's
872	    // Won/Drawn the banner above already shows the winner.
873	    if matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
874	        let stm_color = side_color(view.side_to_move);
875	        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
876	            Span::styled("Side to move:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
877	            Span::raw(" "),
878	            Span::styled(
879	                glyph::side_name(view.side_to_move, style),
880	                TuiStyle::default().fg(stm_color).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
881	            ),
882	        ]));
883	
884	        if matches!(view.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) && g.state.is_in_check(view.side_to_move) {
885	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
886	                "  ‚ö† CHECK ŚįáŤĽć ‚ÄĒ your general is under attack",
887	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Red).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
888	            )));
889	        }
890	    }
891	
892	    let (status_text, status_color) = format_status_short(view.status, style);
893	    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
894	        Span::styled("Status:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
895	        Span::raw(" "),
896	        Span::styled(status_text, TuiStyle::default().fg(status_color)),
897	    ]));
898	    lines.push(line_label_value("Legal moves:", &view.legal_moves.len().to_string()));
899	    lines.push(line_label_value("History:", &g.state.history.len().to_string()));
900	
901	    let selected_label = match g.selected {
902	        Some(sq) => chess_core::notation::iccs::encode_square(&g.state.board, sq),
903	        None => "‚ÄĒ".into(),
904	    };
905	    lines.push(line_label_value("Selected:", &selected_label));
906	
907	    lines.push(Line::from(""));
908	    if let Some(msg) = &g.last_msg {

911	
912	    if help_open {
913	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
914	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
915	            "Help",

918	        for ln in HELP_LINES {
919	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::raw(*ln)));

921	    } else {
922	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
923	        if let Some(ci) = &g.coord_input {
924	            lines.push(Line::from(vec![

933	        }
934	    }

1027	            Span::styled(
1028	                glyph::side_name(view.side_to_move, style),
1029	                TuiStyle::default().fg(stm_color).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1030	            ),
1031	        ]));
1032	    }
1033	
1034	    let (status_text, status_color) = format_status_short(view.status, style);
1035	    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
1036	        Span::styled("Status:", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)),
1037	        Span::raw(" "),
1038	        Span::styled(status_text, TuiStyle::default().fg(status_color)),
1039	    ]));
1040	    lines.push(line_label_value("Legal moves:", &view.legal_moves.len().to_string()));
1041	
1042	    let selected_label = match n.selected {
1043	        Some(sq) => format!("sq {}", sq.0),
1044	        None => "‚ÄĒ".into(),
1045	    };
1046	    lines.push(line_label_value("Selected:", &selected_label));
1047	
1048	    lines.push(line_label_value("Server:", &n.url));
1049	    lines.push(line_label_value("Connection:", if n.connected { "live" } else { "disconnected" }));
1050	
1051	    lines.push(Line::from(""));
1052	    if let Some(msg) = &n.last_msg {
1053	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(msg.clone(), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow))));
1054	    }
1055	
1056	    if help_open {
1057	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
1058	        lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
1059	            "Help",
1060	            TuiStyle::default().add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1061	        )));
1062	        for ln in HELP_LINES_NET {
1063	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::raw(*ln)));
1064	        }
1065	    } else {
1066	        lines.push(Line::from(""));
1067	        if let Some(ci) = &n.coord_input {
1068	            lines.push(Line::from(vec![
1069	                Span::styled("> ", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1070	                Span::styled(format!("{}_", ci.buf), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1071	            ]));
1072	        } else {
1073	            lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
1074	                "?=help, r=rules, t=chat, : / m=coord, q=quit",
1075	                TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
1076	            )));
1077	        }
1078	    }
1079	
1080	    let meta_para = Paragraph::new(lines).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
1081	    frame.render_widget(meta_para, chunks[0]);
1082	
1083	    draw_chat_pane(frame, chunks[1], n, chat_input_active);
1084	}
1085	
1086	const MIN_META_ROWS: u16 = 14;
1087	const MIN_CHAT_ROWS: u16 = 6;
1088	
1089	fn draw_chat_pane(frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect, n: &NetView, input_active: bool) {
1090	    let block = Block::default()
1091	        .borders(Borders::TOP)
1092	        .border_style(TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray))
1093	        .title(" Chat ");
1094	    let inner = block.inner(area);
1095	    frame.render_widget(block, area);
1096	    if inner.height == 0 {
1097	        return;
1098	    }
1099	
1100	    // Reserve the bottom row for the input prompt (or hint when not typing).
1101	    let log_h = inner.height.saturating_sub(1);
1102	    let log_area = Rect { x: inner.x, y: inner.y, width: inner.width, height: log_h };
1103	    let input_area = Rect { x: inner.x, y: inner.y + log_h, width: inner.width, height: 1 };
1104	
1105	    let visible = log_h as usize;
1106	    // Show the most recent N lines (cap by visible height) so the buffer
1107	    // stays scrolled to the bottom on every render.
1108	    let start = n.chat.len().saturating_sub(visible);
1109	    let mut log_lines: Vec<Line> = Vec::with_capacity(visible);
1110	    if n.chat.is_empty() {
1111	        log_lines.push(Line::from(Span::styled(
1112	            "(no messages yet)",
1113	            TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
1114	        )));
1115	    } else {
1116	        for line in n.chat.iter().skip(start) {
1117	            log_lines.push(format_chat_line(line));
1118	        }
1119	    }
1120	    let log_para = Paragraph::new(log_lines).wrap(Wrap { trim: false });
1121	    frame.render_widget(log_para, log_area);
1122	
1123	    let input_line = if input_active {
1124	        let buf = n.chat_input.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
1125	        Line::from(vec![
1126	            Span::styled("> ", TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1127	            Span::styled(format!("{}_", buf), TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::Yellow)),
1128	        ])
1129	    } else {
1130	        let hint = match n.role {
1131	            Some(NetRole::Spectator) => "(spectator ‚ÄĒ read-only)",
1132	            Some(NetRole::Player(_)) => "Press 't' to chat.",
1133	            None => "",
1134	        };
1135	        Line::from(Span::styled(hint, TuiStyle::default().fg(Color::DarkGray)))
1136	    };
1137	    frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(input_line).wrap(Wrap { trim: false }), input_area);
1138	}
1139	
1140	fn format_chat_line(line: &chess_net::ChatLine) -> Line<'static> {
1141	    let from_label = match line.from {
1142	        Side::RED => "Red:",
1143	        Side::BLACK => "Black:",
1144	        _ => "Green:",
1145	    };
1146	    let from_color = side_color(line.from);
1147	    Line::from(vec![
1148	        Span::styled(
1149	            from_label.to_string(),
1150	            TuiStyle::default().fg(from_color).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
1151	        ),
1152	        Span::raw(" "),
1153	        Span::raw(line.text.clone()),
1154	    ])
1155	}
1156	
1157	const HELP_LINES_NET: &[&str] = &[
1158	    "h j k l / ‚Üź‚Üď‚ÜĎ‚Üí  move cursor",
1159	    "Enter / Space   select / commit (sends Move to server)",
1160	    "Esc             cancel selection",
1161	    ":               coord input (instant): type ICCS (h2e2 / flip a0), Enter sends",
1162	    "m               coord input (live preview): same, with selected/cursor preview",
1163	    "f               flip (banqi)",
1164	    "n               request rematch (after game over; needs both sides)",
1165	    "t               open chat input (players only; Enter sends, Esc cancels)",
1166	    "r               toggle rules overlay",
1167	    "?               toggle this help",
1168	    "Click           select / commit",
```
</details></tool-use>

//! `chess-web` ‚ÄĒ Leptos + WASM frontend for chess-core.
//!
//! Native (`cargo check --workspace`) compiles only the pure-logic modules
//! (orient, glyph, routes, state); the leptos UI and the WS layer are
//! wasm32-gated so the workspace target check stays clean. Build for the
//! browser with `trunk serve` (or `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown
//! -p chess-web`).

pub mod glyph;
pub mod orient;
pub mod routes;
pub mod state;

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
mod app;
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
mod components;
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
mod config;
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
mod pages;
#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
mod ws;

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
use wasm_bindgen::prelude::wasm_bindgen;

#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
#[wasm_bindgen(start)]
pub fn start() {
    console_error_panic_hook::set_once();
    leptos::mount_to_body(app::App);
}
```

```rs
//! User-facing FX preferences, persisted in `localStorage`.
//!
//! Two switches, both default ON:
//! * `fx_confetti` ‚ÄĒ show end-of-game confetti + VICTORY/DEFEAT/DRAW overlay.
//! * `fx_check_banner` ‚ÄĒ show the "ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK" badge in the sidebar when
//!   the side-to-move is in check (xiangqi only).
//!
//! Storage keys are stable (`chess.fx.confetti`, `chess.fx.checkBanner`)
//! so a user toggling once carries the choice across sessions. Missing
//! values read as `true`. We persist `"1"` / `"0"` rather than booleans
//! so future debugging via DevTools is obvious.
//!
//! All `web_sys` calls are wrapped in `Option`/`Result` chains so a
//! sandboxed context with no `window` (or storage disabled) silently
//! falls back to in-memory defaults ‚ÄĒ no panics.
//!
//! This module is wasm32-only; the workspace native check skips it.

use leptos::*;

const KEY_CONFETTI: &str = "chess.fx.confetti";
const KEY_CHECK_BANNER: &str = "chess.fx.checkBanner";

#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
pub struct Prefs {
    pub fx_confetti: RwSignal<bool>,
    pub fx_check_banner: RwSignal<bool>,
}

impl Prefs {
    /// Hydrate from `localStorage` and arm `create_effect` watchers that
    /// persist any future change. Designed to be called once at app
    /// boot and `provide_context`-shared with all routes.
    pub fn hydrate() -> Self {
        let fx_confetti = create_rw_signal(read_bool(KEY_CONFETTI, true));
        let fx_check_banner = create_rw_signal(read_bool(KEY_CHECK_BANNER, true));

        // Persist on change. The closures only run inside the browser, so
        // calling `localStorage` on every flip is safe and cheap.
        create_effect(move |_| {
            let v = fx_confetti.get();
            write_bool(KEY_CONFETTI, v);
        });
        create_effect(move |_| {
            let v = fx_check_banner.get();
            write_bool(KEY_CHECK_BANNER, v);
        });

        Self { fx_confetti, fx_check_banner }
    }
}

fn local_storage() -> Option<web_sys::Storage> {
    web_sys::window().and_then(|w| w.local_storage().ok().flatten())
}

fn read_bool(key: &str, default_value: bool) -> bool {
    let Some(storage) = local_storage() else { return default_value };
    match storage.get_item(key) {
        Ok(Some(s)) => s != "0",
        _ => default_value,
    }
}

fn write_bool(key: &str, value: bool) {
    if let Some(storage) = local_storage() {
        let _ = storage.set_item(key, if value { "1" } else { "0" });
    }
}

```

```text
1	//! Pure-logic helpers for the client's view of a game. Native-testable ‚ÄĒ
2	//! no Leptos signals or browser deps live here.
3	
4	use chess_core::coord::Square;
5	use chess_core::moves::Move;
6	use chess_core::piece::Side;
7	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
8	
9	/// Role assigned by the server on `Hello` (player) or `Spectating`
10	/// (read-only). Drives whether the play page renders move/resign/rematch
11	/// affordances and whether the chat input is enabled.
12	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
13	pub enum ClientRole {
14	    Player(Side),
15	    Spectator,
16	}
17	
18	impl ClientRole {
19	    pub fn is_player(self) -> bool {
20	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Player(_))
21	    }
22	
23	    pub fn is_spectator(self) -> bool {
24	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Spectator)
25	    }
26	
27	    pub fn observer(self) -> Side {
28	        // Spectators render from RED's POV ‚ÄĒ matches what chess-net's
29	        // `broadcast_update` projects for spectator updates.
30	        match self {
31	            ClientRole::Player(s) => s,
32	            ClientRole::Spectator => Side::RED,
33	        }
34	    }
35	}
36	
37	/// Trim a vec from the front so it holds at most `max` entries. Used for
38	/// the per-page chat ring buffer that mirrors the server's 50-line cap.
39	pub fn truncate_front<T>(buf: &mut Vec<T>, max: usize) {
40	    if buf.len() > max {
41	        let drop_count = buf.len() - max;
42	        buf.drain(0..drop_count);
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	/// Find the legal `Move` (if any) whose origin is `from` and whose final
47	/// destination is `to`. Reveal moves match when `from == to == at`.
48	pub fn find_move(view: &PlayerView, from: Square, to: Square) -> Option<Move> {
49	    view.legal_moves.iter().find(|m| matches_endpoints(m, from, to)).cloned()
50	}
51	
52	fn matches_endpoints(mv: &Move, from: Square, to: Square) -> bool {
53	    if mv.origin_square() != from {
54	        return false;
55	    }
56	    match mv.to_square() {
57	        Some(t) => t == to,
58	        None => matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { at, .. } if *at == to),
59	    }
60	}
61	
62	/// All legal destination squares for a piece on `from` (used to render dots).
63	pub fn legal_targets(view: &PlayerView, from: Square) -> Vec<Square> {
64	    view.legal_moves
65	        .iter()
66	        .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from)
67	        .map(|m| match m.to_square() {
68	            Some(t) => t,
69	            None => match m {
70	                Move::Reveal { at, .. } => *at,
71	                _ => unreachable!("Move::to_square() returned None for a non-Reveal move"),
72	            },
73	        })
74	        .collect()
75	}
76	
77	#[cfg(test)]
78	mod tests {
79	    use super::*;
80	    use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
81	    use chess_core::state::GameState;
82	
83	    #[test]
84	    fn find_move_locates_xiangqi_step() {
85	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
86	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
87	        // Red soldier at file 0 rank 3 should have a step forward to rank 4.
88	        let from = Square(3 * 9); // rank 3, file 0
89	        let to = Square(4 * 9); // rank 4, file 0
90	        let mv = find_move(&view, from, to);
91	        assert!(matches!(mv, Some(Move::Step { .. })), "expected step from soldier, got {:?}", mv);
92	    }
93	
94	    #[test]
95	    fn legal_targets_for_chariot_in_corner() {
96	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
97	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
98	        // Red chariot at (file 0, rank 0) ‚ÄĒ Square(0). Should have several
99	        // legal destinations along its file (rank moves blocked by horse).
100	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(0));
101	        assert!(!targets.is_empty(), "chariot at corner must have legal moves");
102	    }
103	
104	    #[test]
105	    fn legal_targets_empty_for_non_piece_square() {
106	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
107	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
108	        // Square(5*9 + 4) ‚ÄĒ a river-ish empty square. Empty piece ‚Üí no targets.
109	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(5 * 9 + 4));
110	        assert!(targets.is_empty());
111	    }
112	
113	    #[test]
114	    fn truncate_front_drops_oldest_when_over_cap() {
115	        let mut v = vec![1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
116	        truncate_front(&mut v, 3);
117	        assert_eq!(v, vec![3, 4, 5]);
118	    }
119	
120	    #[test]
121	    fn truncate_front_noop_when_under_cap() {
122	        let mut v = vec![1, 2];
123	        truncate_front(&mut v, 5);
124	        assert_eq!(v, vec![1, 2]);
125	    }
126	
127	    #[test]
128	    fn client_role_observer_defaults_red_for_spectator() {
129	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Player(Side::BLACK).observer(), Side::BLACK);
130	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Spectator.observer(), Side::RED);
131	        assert!(ClientRole::Spectator.is_spectator());
132	        assert!(ClientRole::Player(Side::RED).is_player());
133	    }
134	}
135

1	use chess_core::coord::Square;
2	use chess_core::piece::Side;
3	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
4	use chess_core::state::GameState;
5	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
6	use leptos::*;
7	use leptos_router::{use_params_map, use_query_map};
8	
9	use crate::components::board::Board;
10	use crate::components::sidebar::Sidebar;
11	use crate::routes::{build_rule_set, parse_local_rules, parse_variant_slug};
12	use crate::state::find_move;
13	
14	#[component]
15	pub fn LocalPage() -> impl IntoView {
16	    let params = use_params_map();
17	    let query = use_query_map();
18	    let resolved = move || -> Result<(Variant, RuleSet, bool), String> {
19	        let slug = params.with(|p| p.get("variant").cloned().unwrap_or_default());
20	        let variant =
21	            parse_variant_slug(&slug).ok_or_else(|| format!("Unknown variant: {}", slug))?;
22	        let parsed = query.with(|q| parse_local_rules(|k| q.get(k).cloned()));
23	        let rules = build_rule_set(variant, &parsed);
24	        // Three-kingdom: engine ships an empty 4√ó8 board. Render it but
25	        // disable interaction and overlay a "WIP" banner ‚ÄĒ see
26	        // `backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md` and the gotcha in CLAUDE.md.
27	        let wip = matches!(variant, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi);
28	        Ok((variant, rules, wip))
29	    };
30	
31	    move || match resolved() {
32	        Ok((variant, rules, wip)) => {
33	            view! { <LocalGame variant=variant rules=rules wip=wip/> }.into_view()
34	        }
35	        Err(msg) => view! {
36	            <section class="game-page game-page--single">
37	                <div>
38	                    <a href="/" class="back-link">"‚Üź Back to picker"</a>
39	                    <h2>"Variant unavailable"</h2>
40	                    <p class="subtitle">{msg}</p>
41	                </div>
42	            </section>
43	        }
44	        .into_view(),
45	    }
46	}
47	
48	#[component]
49	fn LocalGame(variant: Variant, rules: RuleSet, wip: bool) -> impl IntoView {
50	    let initial_rules = rules.clone();
51	    let state = create_rw_signal(GameState::new(rules));
52	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
53	
54	    // Local pass-and-play renders from a fixed Red-side observer (board never
55	    // flips on turn change). Legal moves come from the projection for the
56	    // *current* side-to-move so click-to-move uses the right list.
57	    let observer = Side::RED;
58	    let shape = state.with_untracked(|s| s.board.shape());
59	
60	    let view = create_memo(move |_| state.with(|s| PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)));
61	
62	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
63	        if wip {
64	            return;
65	        }
66	        let v = view.get();
67	        let cur = selected.get();
68	
69	        if cur == Some(sq) {
70	            selected.set(None);
71	            return;
72	        }
73	
74	        if let Some(from) = cur {
75	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
76	                state.update(|s| {
77	                    s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal move from view");
78	                    s.refresh_status();
79	                });
80	                selected.set(None);
81	                return;
82	            }
83	        }
84	
85	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
86	        match cell {
87	            VisibleCell::Revealed(p) if p.piece.side == v.side_to_move => {
88	                selected.set(Some(sq));
89	            }
90	            VisibleCell::Hidden => {
91	                // Banqi flip ‚ÄĒ engine emits `Reveal { at }` whose origin and
92	                // target both equal `sq`. Apply directly.
93	                if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
94	                    state.update(|s| {
95	                        s.make_move(&mv).expect("legal reveal");
96	                        s.refresh_status();
97	                    });
98	                    selected.set(None);
99	                }
100	            }
101	            _ => {
102	                selected.set(None);
103	            }
104	        }
105	    });
106	
107	    let on_undo: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_| {
108	        if wip {
109	            return;
110	        }
111	        state.update(|s| {
112	            let _ = s.unmake_move();
113	            s.refresh_status();
114	        });
115	        selected.set(None);
116	    });
117	
118	    let on_new_game: Callback<()> = Callback::new({
119	        let initial_rules = initial_rules.clone();
120	        move |_| {
121	            state.set(GameState::new(initial_rules.clone()));
122	            selected.set(None);
123	        }
124	    });
125	
126	    view! {
127	        <section class="game-page">
128	            <div class="board-pane">
129	                <Board
130	                    shape=shape
131	                    observer=observer
132	                    view=view
133	                    selected=selected
134	                    on_click=on_click
135	                />
136	                <Show when=move || wip>
137	                    <div class="wip-overlay">
138	                        <div class="wip-banner">
139	                            <h3>"Three-Kingdom Banqi šłČŚúčśöóś£č"</h3>
140	                            <p>
141	                                "Engine still WIP ‚ÄĒ pieces, rules, and 3-seat turn order land in PR-2. "
142	                                "See "
143	                                <code>"backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md"</code>
144	                                "."
145	                            </p>
146	                        </div>
147	                    </div>
148	                </Show>
149	            </div>
150	            <Sidebar
151	                variant=variant
152	                view=view
153	                on_new_game=on_new_game
154	                on_undo=on_undo
155	                wip=wip
156	            />
157	        </section>
158	    }
159	}

44	            set_toast.set(None);

117	#[component]
118	fn ConnPlaceholder(room: String, conn: ReadSignal<ConnState>) -> impl IntoView {
119	    let label = move || match conn.get() {
120	        ConnState::Connecting => "Connecting‚Ä¶".to_string(),
121	        ConnState::Open => "Waiting for server greeting‚Ä¶".to_string(),
122	        ConnState::Closed => "Disconnected ‚ÄĒ refresh to reconnect.".to_string(),
123	        ConnState::Error => "Connection error ‚ÄĒ is the server running?".to_string(),
124	    };
125	    view! {
126	        <div class="conn-placeholder">

128	            <p>{label}</p>
129	        </div>
130	    }
131	}

133	#[component]
134	fn BoardWrapper(
135	    view_signal: ReadSignal<Option<PlayerView>>,
136	    role: ReadSignal<Option<ClientRole>>,
137	    handle: WsHandle,
138	) -> impl IntoView {
139	    let view = Signal::derive(move || view_signal.get().expect("guarded by Show"));
140	    let resolved_role = role.get_untracked().expect("guarded by Show");
141	    let obs = resolved_role.observer();
142	    let shape = view.with_untracked(|v| v.shape);
143	
144	    let selected = create_rw_signal::<Option<Square>>(None);
145	    // Spectators see-only ‚ÄĒ no click handlers on the board for them.
146	    let is_spectator = resolved_role.is_spectator();
147	
148	    let on_click: Callback<Square> = Callback::new(move |sq: Square| {
149	        if is_spectator {
150	            return;
151	        }
152	        let v = view.get();
153	        if v.observer != v.side_to_move {
154	            return;
155	        }
156	        let cur = selected.get();
157	        if cur == Some(sq) {
158	            selected.set(None);
159	            return;
160	        }
161	        if let Some(from) = cur {
162	            if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, from, sq) {
163	                handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
164	                selected.set(None);
165	                return;
166	            }
167	        }
168	        let cell = v.cells[sq.0 as usize];
169	        match cell {
170	            VisibleCell::Revealed(p) if p.piece.side == v.side_to_move => {
171	                selected.set(Some(sq));
172	            }
173	            VisibleCell::Hidden => {
174	                if let Some(mv) = find_move(&v, sq, sq) {
175	                    handle.send(ClientMsg::Move { mv });
176	                    selected.set(None);
177	                }
178	            }
179	            _ => selected.set(None),
180	        }
181	    });
182	
183	    view! {
184	        <Board
185	            shape=shape
186	            observer=obs
187	            view=view
188	            selected=selected
189	            on_click=on_click
190	        />
191	    }
192	}
193

234	        )

236	    let is_player = move || role.get().map(|r| r.is_player()).unwrap_or(false);

246	
247	    let resign = {

251	        }
252	    };
253	    let rematch = {

255	        move |_| {

257	        }
258	    };
259

270	            </div>

```rs
//! Pure-CSS confetti burst. Each particle is a `<div>` with three inline
//! CSS variables (`--x` start position, `--delay` stagger, `--color`
//! fill) consumed by the `@keyframes confetti-fall` animation in
//! `style.css`. No canvas, no JS animation loop ‚ÄĒ once mounted the
//! browser does the rest.
//!
//! Lifetime is owned by the parent: mount the component when you want
//! the burst, unmount it ~3 seconds later. The `show` signal handles the
//! mount/unmount via `<Show>` so re-firing works cleanly (mount = new
//! seed = new random positions).

use leptos::*;

const PARTICLE_COUNT: usize = 48;
const COLORS: &[&str] = &["#fcd34d", "#f87171", "#34d399", "#60a5fa", "#c084fc", "#fb923c"];

#[component]
pub fn Confetti() -> impl IntoView {
    // Generate per-particle inline-style strings once on mount. We use
    // `js_sys::Math::random` which is the same generator the browser uses
    // for its own entropy ‚ÄĒ good enough for visual jitter, no crate dep.
    let particles = (0..PARTICLE_COUNT)
        .map(|i| {
            let x = rand_f64() * 100.0;
            let delay = rand_f64() * 0.8;
            let drift = (rand_f64() - 0.5) * 60.0;
            let rotate_end = (rand_f64() - 0.5) * 1440.0;
            let color = COLORS[i % COLORS.len()];
            // Two CSS vars carry layout (start x, drift), two carry
            // animation timing (delay, end rotation). The duration stays
            // fixed at 3s so the parent's unmount lines up with the
            // visual end of the burst.
            format!(
                "--x:{x:.2}%;--drift:{drift:.0}px;--delay:{delay:.2}s;--rotate-end:{rotate_end:.0}deg;--color:{color};"
            )
        })
        .collect::<Vec<_>>();

    view! {
        <div class="confetti-container" aria-hidden="true">
            {particles
                .into_iter()
                .map(|style| view! { <div class="confetti-particle" style=style></div> })
                .collect_view()}
        </div>
    }
}

fn rand_f64() -> f64 {
    js_sys::Math::random()
}

```

```rs
//! Full-screen "VICTORY / DEFEAT / DRAW" celebration overlay.
//!
//! The overlay is gated by:
//!   1. the user's `fx_confetti` pref (default ON), and
//!   2. an Ongoing ‚Üí Won/Drawn status transition.
//!
//! Spectators (and Local pass-and-play, which has no `role`) get neutral
//! "Red Wins / Black Wins / Draw" copy. Net players see the personalised
//! "VICTORY" / "DEFEAT" framing. The overlay auto-dismisses after ~3s
//! via `set_timeout`; the matching sidebar status text stays as a
//! permanent record of the result.
//!
//! The component owns a `prev_status` signal so a remount (e.g. lobby
//! navigation) doesn't re-fire on the persistent end state. New games
//! reset the tracker via the Ongoing ‚Üí ended check.

use std::time::Duration;

use chess_core::piece::Side;
use chess_core::state::{DrawReason, GameStatus, WinReason};
use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
use leptos::*;

use crate::components::confetti::Confetti;
use crate::state::ClientRole;

#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
enum BannerKind {
    Victory,
    Defeat,
    Draw,
    NeutralWin(Side),
}

#[component]
pub fn EndOverlay(
    /// Live `PlayerView` ‚ÄĒ drives the status transition watcher.
    #[prop(into)]
    view: Signal<PlayerView>,
    /// `None` for Local pass-and-play (uses neutral copy); `Some(role)` for Net.
    #[prop(into)]
    role: Signal<Option<ClientRole>>,
    /// User pref ‚ÄĒ when false the overlay never mounts.
    #[prop(into)]
    enabled: Signal<bool>,
) -> impl IntoView {
    let (visible, set_visible) = create_signal(false);
    let (kind_signal, set_kind) = create_signal::<Option<BannerKind>>(None);
    let prev_status = create_rw_signal::<Option<GameStatus>>(None);

    create_effect(move |_| {
        let cur = view.with(|v| v.status);
        let prev = prev_status.get_untracked();
        let was_ongoing = matches!(prev, Some(GameStatus::Ongoing));
        let now_ended = matches!(cur, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. });

        if was_ongoing && now_ended && enabled.get_untracked() {
            let resolved_kind = resolve_kind(&cur, role.get_untracked());
            set_kind.set(resolved_kind);
            set_visible.set(true);
            // Auto-dismiss after the confetti animation finishes. Use
            // `set_timeout` rather than CSS animation events so the
            // banner fade and the confetti fade share one source of truth.
            set_timeout(
                move || {
                    set_visible.set(false);
                },
                Duration::from_millis(3200),
            );
        }
        prev_status.set(Some(cur));
    });

    let title = move || match kind_signal.get() {
        Some(BannerKind::Victory) => "VICTORY".to_string(),
        Some(BannerKind::Defeat) => "DEFEAT".to_string(),
        Some(BannerKind::Draw) => "DRAW".to_string(),
        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(Side::RED)) => "Red Wins ÁīÖŚčĚ".to_string(),
        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(Side::BLACK)) => "Black Wins ťĽĎŚčĚ".to_string(),
        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(_)) => "Green Wins Á∂†ŚčĚ".to_string(),
        None => String::new(),
    };

    let title_class = move || match kind_signal.get() {
        Some(BannerKind::Victory) => "endgame-title endgame-title--victory",
        Some(BannerKind::Defeat) => "endgame-title endgame-title--defeat",
        Some(BannerKind::Draw) => "endgame-title endgame-title--draw",
        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(Side::RED)) => "endgame-title endgame-title--red",
        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(Side::BLACK)) => "endgame-title endgame-title--black",
        Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(_)) => "endgame-title endgame-title--green",
        None => "endgame-title",
    };

    let reason = move || {
        view.with(|v| match v.status {
            GameStatus::Won { reason, .. } => format!("by {}", win_reason_label(reason)),
            GameStatus::Drawn { reason } => format!("by {}", draw_reason_label(reason)),
            GameStatus::Ongoing => String::new(),
        })
    };

    view! {
        <Show when=move || visible.get()>
            <div class="endgame-overlay" aria-live="polite">
                <div class="endgame-banner">
                    <h1 class=title_class>{title}</h1>
                    <p class="endgame-reason">{reason}</p>
                </div>
                <Confetti/>
            </div>
        </Show>
    }
}

fn resolve_kind(status: &GameStatus, role: Option<ClientRole>) -> Option<BannerKind> {
    match (status, role) {
        (GameStatus::Ongoing, _) => None,
        (GameStatus::Drawn { .. }, _) => Some(BannerKind::Draw),
        (GameStatus::Won { winner, .. }, Some(ClientRole::Player(seat))) => {
            if *winner == seat {
                Some(BannerKind::Victory)
            } else {
                Some(BannerKind::Defeat)
            }
        }
        // Spectator ‚ÄĒ and Local pass-and-play (`None`) ‚ÄĒ both fall through
        // to the neutral "Side Wins" framing.
        (GameStatus::Won { winner, .. }, _) => Some(BannerKind::NeutralWin(*winner)),
    }
}

fn win_reason_label(reason: WinReason) -> &'static str {
    match reason {
        WinReason::Checkmate => "checkmate (Śįáś≠Ľ)",
        WinReason::Stalemate => "stalemate (Śõįś≠Ľ)",
        WinReason::Resignation => "resignation",
        WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces => "elimination",
        WinReason::Timeout => "time forfeit",
        WinReason::GeneralCaptured => "general captured (ŚįáŤĘęŚźÉ)",
    }
}

fn draw_reason_label(reason: DrawReason) -> &'static str {
    match reason {
        DrawReason::NoProgress => "no progress (60 plies)",
        DrawReason::Repetition => "repetition",
        DrawReason::Agreed => "agreement",
        DrawReason::InsufficientMaterial => "insufficient material",
    }
}

```

/* Connection banner. */
.conn-banner { margin: 0; padding: 0.4rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.3rem; font-size: 0.9rem; }

```bash
cat >> /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/style.css << 'EOF'

/* ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
 * End-of-game overlay + confetti + check banner. See `prefs.rs` for
 * the localStorage toggles that gate these.
 * ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ */

/* Centered VICTORY/DEFEAT/DRAW banner over the board area. The container
 * is `position: absolute` so it lives inside `.board-pane`'s positioning
 * context (same as `.wip-overlay`). The banner card uses the existing
 * accent palette so it reads as part of the theme rather than a popover. */
.endgame-overlay {
    position: absolute;
    inset: 0;
    display: grid;
    place-items: center;
    z-index: 40;
    pointer-events: none;
    background: rgba(26, 22, 20, 0.45);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    animation: endgame-fade-in 0.25s ease-out;
}
.endgame-banner {
    background: var(--bg);
    border: 2px solid var(--accent);
    border-radius: 0.6rem;
    padding: 1.25rem 2rem;
    text-align: center;
    box-shadow: 0 12px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.55);
    animation: pulse-banner 1.6s ease-in-out infinite;
}
.endgame-title {
    margin: 0;
    font-size: clamp(2rem, 6vw, 3.5rem);
    letter-spacing: 0.18em;
    font-weight: 800;
}
.endgame-title--victory { color: #fcd34d; text-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(252, 211, 77, 0.5); }
.endgame-title--defeat  { color: #f87171; text-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.5); }
.endgame-title--draw    { color: var(--muted); }
.endgame-title--red     { color: var(--red); }
.endgame-title--black   { color: #f4d2a3; }
.endgame-title--green   { color: #6ad28d; }
.endgame-reason {
    margin: 0.5rem 0 0;
    color: var(--muted);
    font-size: 0.95rem;
}

/* Confetti ‚ÄĒ pure CSS particles. The container fills the viewport so
 * particles can fall past the board edge naturally. Each particle reads
 * `--x` (start x %), `--drift` (lateral drift in px), `--delay` (stagger),
 * `--rotate-end` (final rotation), `--color` (fill). */
.confetti-container {
    position: fixed;
    inset: 0;
    pointer-events: none;
    z-index: 60;
    overflow: hidden;
}
.confetti-particle {
    position: absolute;
    top: -16px;
    left: var(--x);
    width: 8px;
    height: 14px;
    background: var(--color);
    border-radius: 1px;
    transform-origin: 50% 50%;
    animation: confetti-fall 3s cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94) forwards;
    animation-delay: var(--delay);
    opacity: 0.95;
}

@keyframes confetti-fall {
    0%   { transform: translate(0, 0) rotate(0deg); opacity: 1; }
    80%  { opacity: 1; }
    100% { transform: translate(var(--drift), 110vh) rotate(var(--rotate-end)); opacity: 0; }
}

@keyframes pulse-banner {
    0%, 100% { transform: scale(1.0); }
    50%      { transform: scale(1.04); }
}

@keyframes endgame-fade-in {
    from { opacity: 0; }
    to   { opacity: 1; }
}

/* "ŚįáŤĽć / CHECK" sidebar badge ‚ÄĒ only mounts when the side-to-move's
 * general is under attack (xiangqi only) and the user hasn't disabled it. */
.check-badge {
    margin: 0.4rem 0 0.6rem;
    padding: 0.3rem 0.6rem;
    background: rgba(178, 34, 34, 0.18);
    border-left: 3px solid var(--red);
    color: #ffd3d3;
    font-weight: 700;
    border-radius: 0.25rem;
    animation: pulse-banner 1.4s ease-in-out infinite;
}

/* FX toggle row in the sidebar footer. Compact so it doesn't crowd the
 * action buttons. */
.fx-toggles {
    margin-top: 0.6rem;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.25rem;
    font-size: 0.85rem;
    color: var(--muted);
}
.fx-toggles label {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    cursor: pointer;
}

/* Picker hero ‚ÄĒ a tiny header above the variant grid so the page feels
 * like a landing screen rather than a bare form. SVG icon scales with
 * the heading; tagline stays muted. */
.picker-hero {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 1rem;
    margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
    padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(212, 165, 92, 0.15), rgba(178, 34, 34, 0.05));
    border: 1px solid rgba(212, 165, 92, 0.25);
    border-radius: 0.6rem;
}
.picker-hero__glyph {
    flex: 0 0 auto;
    width: 64px;
    height: 64px;
    border: 2px solid var(--red);
    border-radius: 50%;
    display: grid;
    place-items: center;
    background: var(--bg);
    color: var(--red);
    font-size: 2.2rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(178, 34, 34, 0.3);
}
.picker-hero__copy h1 {
    margin: 0 0 0.25rem;
    font-size: 1.6rem;
    color: var(--accent);
    letter-spacing: 0.05em;
}
.picker-hero__copy p {
    margin: 0;
    color: var(--muted);
    font-size: 0.9rem;
}
EOF

```text
1	//! Sidebar: variant + turn + status + history-length + undo / new-game buttons.
2	//!
3	//! `wip=true` (three-kingdom banqi until the engine ships) hides turn /
4	//! legal-move counts and disables the action buttons ‚ÄĒ the board overlay
5	//! tells the user what's happening; the sidebar just stays out of the way.
6	
7	use chess_core::piece::Side;
8	use chess_core::rules::Variant;
9	use chess_core::state::{DrawReason, GameStatus, WinReason};
10	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
11	use leptos::*;
12	
13	use crate::glyph::{self, Style};
14	
15	#[component]
16	pub fn Sidebar(
17	    variant: Variant,
18	    #[prop(into)] view: Signal<PlayerView>,
19	    #[prop(into)] on_new_game: Callback<()>,
20	    #[prop(into)] on_undo: Callback<()>,
21	    #[prop(default = false)] wip: bool,
22	) -> impl IntoView {
23	    let style = Style::Cjk;
24	    let variant_label = match variant {
25	        Variant::Xiangqi => "Xiangqi ŤĪ°ś£č",
26	        Variant::Banqi => "Banqi śöóś£č",
27	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "Three-Kingdom šłČŚúčśöóś£č",
28	    };
29	
30	    let turn_text = move || {
31	        let v = view.get();
32	        glyph::side_name(v.side_to_move, style).to_string()
33	    };
34	    let turn_class = move || {
35	        let v = view.get();
36	        match v.side_to_move {
37	            Side::RED => "turn-red",
38	            Side::BLACK => "turn-black",
39	            _ => "turn-green",
40	        }
41	    };
42	
43	    let status_view = move || -> View {
44	        let v = view.get();
45	        match v.status {
46	            GameStatus::Ongoing => view! { <p class="status ongoing">"Ongoing"</p> }.into_view(),
47	            GameStatus::Won { winner, reason } => {
48	                let side = glyph::side_name(winner, style);
49	                let reason = win_reason_label(reason);
50	                view! {
51	                    <p class="status won">
52	                        {format!("{} wins ‚ÄĒ {}", side, reason)}
53	                    </p>
54	                }
55	                .into_view()
56	            }
57	            GameStatus::Drawn { reason } => view! {
58	                <p class="status drawn">
59	                    {format!("Drawn ‚ÄĒ {}", draw_reason_label(reason))}
60	                </p>
61	            }
62	            .into_view(),
63	        }
64	    };
65	
66	    let legal_count = move || view.with(|v| v.legal_moves.len());
67	
68	    view! {
69	        <aside class="sidebar">
70	            <h3 class="variant-label">{variant_label}</h3>
71	            <Show when=move || !wip fallback=|| view! {
72	                <p class="status drawn">"Engine WIP ‚ÄĒ board interaction disabled."</p>
73	            }>
74	                <div class="turn-row">
75	                    <span class="turn-prefix">"Turn: "</span>
76	                    <span class=turn_class>{turn_text}</span>
77	                </div>
78	                {status_view}
79	                <p class="muted">{move || format!("{} legal moves", legal_count())}</p>
80	            </Show>
81	            <div class="sidebar-actions">
82	                <button class="btn" on:click=move |_| on_undo.call(()) disabled=move || wip>"Undo"</button>
83	                <button class="btn" on:click=move |_| on_new_game.call(()) disabled=move || wip>"New game"</button>
84	            </div>
85	            <a class="back-link" href="/">"‚Üź Back to picker"</a>
86	        </aside>
87	    }
88	}
89	
90	fn win_reason_label(reason: WinReason) -> &'static str {
91	    match reason {
92	        WinReason::Checkmate => "checkmate Śįáś≠Ľ",
93	        WinReason::Stalemate => "stalemate Śõįś≠Ľ",
94	        WinReason::Resignation => "resignation",
95	        WinReason::OnlyOneSideHasPieces => "only one side has pieces",
96	        WinReason::Timeout => "timeout",
97	        WinReason::GeneralCaptured => "general captured",
98	    }
99	}
100	
101	fn draw_reason_label(reason: DrawReason) -> &'static str {
102	    match reason {
103	        DrawReason::NoProgress => "no progress",
104	        DrawReason::Repetition => "repetition",
105	        DrawReason::Agreed => "agreed",
106	        DrawReason::InsufficientMaterial => "insufficient material",
107	    }
108	}
109
```

All 11 tasks done. Pre-push suite green: fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, all tests, wasm32 chess-core, and trunk release build (with wasm-opt). Depth-3 perft also clean.

## Summary of changes

**chess-core / chess-net (Phase 1)**
- `crates/chess-core/src/view.rs` ‚ÄĒ added `pub in_check: bool` (with `#[serde(default)]`) to `PlayerView`, computed at projection time. 3 new tests.
- `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs` ‚ÄĒ bumped `PROTOCOL_VERSION` to 4 with changelog comment.
- `docs/adr/0007-spectator-check-flag.md` ‚ÄĒ new ADR documenting the wire change.

**chess-tui (Phase 2)**
- `clients/chess-tui/src/banner.rs` ‚ÄĒ hand-rolled ASCII art for VICTORY / DEFEAT / DRAW / RED WINS / BLACK WINS / GREEN WINS / chess-tui title (CJK + ASCII variants).
- `clients/chess-tui/src/confetti.rs` ‚ÄĒ particle physics, xorshift32 RNG, ratatui `Buffer` renderer.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/app.rs` ‚ÄĒ new AppState fields (`show_confetti`, `show_check_banner`, `confetti_anim`, `confetti_pending`, `prev_status_local`, `prev_status_net`); `note_status_transition` fires after every dispatch + `tick_net`. Added `replace_preserving_prefs` helper so screen transitions carry FX choices.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs` ‚ÄĒ `--no-confetti` / `--no-check-banner` flags; `poll_ms = 50` while animation active.
- `clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` ‚ÄĒ `draw_picker` now renders the ASCII title (with single-line fallback for narrow terminals); `draw_sidebar_net` mirrors the in-check banner from local; new `render_confetti_and_banner` overlays the burst + centered ASCII banner over the board for ~3 s.

**chess-web (Phase 3)**
- `clients/chess-web/src/prefs.rs` ‚ÄĒ `Prefs::hydrate()` reads `chess.fx.confetti` and `chess.fx.checkBanner` from `localStorage`, persists via `create_effect`. `Storage` feature added to `web-sys`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/end_overlay.rs` ‚ÄĒ VICTORY/DEFEAT for Net players, neutral "Red Wins / Black Wins" for spectators and Local; auto-dismisses after 3.2 s.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/confetti.rs` ‚ÄĒ 48 CSS-driven `<div>` particles using random inline custom properties.
- `clients/chess-web/style.css` ‚ÄĒ `@keyframes confetti-fall`, `pulse-banner`, `endgame-fade-in`; styles for `.endgame-overlay`, `.endgame-banner`, `.confetti-*`, `.check-badge`, `.fx-toggles`, `.picker-hero`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs` and `pages/play.rs` ‚ÄĒ pulsing ŚįáŤĽć/CHECK badge above the turn row, two FX toggle checkboxes in the sidebar footer.
- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs` ‚ÄĒ small hero header with Śł• glyph + bilingual title.

**Optional follow-ups not in scope**
- CLAUDE.md gotcha block for the new CLI flags / localStorage keys / v4 protocol.
- An entry in `TODO.md` if you want to track the deferred "in-game settings drawer / share-result link" ideas the plan flagged as out-of-scope.

# TODO

Long-term backlog for Chinese Chess. See AGENTS.md
for the maintenance workflow that agents should follow.

> **For agents**: when the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being
> implemented this session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have",
> "Ś∑•Á®čťáŹŚ§™Ś§ßťúÄŤ¶ĀŚÜćŤ©ēšľį", "ŚÖąŤ®ėšłčšĺÜ"), add it here with priority + effort tags.
> Do not create new `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files ‚ÄĒ
> `TODO.md` is the single backlog index. Long-form research goes in
> [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/).

<!-- Use the exact section order: P1, P2, P3, P?, Done.
     The bundled scripts/todo-kanban.sh validator only inspects top-level
     `- [ ]` and `- ‚úÖ` items inside these sections. Prose paragraphs,
     blockquotes, indented sub-bullets, HTML comments, and `---` rules are
     ignored ‚ÄĒ feel free to add inline guidance like this without breaking
     machine readability. -->

## P1

Likely next batch ‚ÄĒ items you'd reach for if you sat down to work today.

- [ ] **[M] Three Kingdoms Banqi (šłČŚúčśöóś£č) implementation** ‚ÄĒ Build BoardShape::ThreeKingdom mask + rules/three_kingdom.rs with 3-side capture rules. Architecture supports it (TurnOrder, Side, BoardShape variant exist); rules logic and home-zone layout need spec from canonical source. ‚Üí [research](backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md)
- [ ] **[S] Wire DARK_CHAIN house rule** ‚ÄĒ Chain captures pass through face-down squares; auto-reveal as chain progresses. Requires changing ChainHop.captured to Option<Piece> and updating sanitize_for_observer for the network ABI. See docs/rules/banqi-house.md.
- [ ] **[S] Wire HORSE_DIAGONAL house rule** ‚ÄĒ Banqi horse moves like xiangqi horse (L-shape with ť¶¨ŤÖŅ leg block) when flag enabled. Reuse pattern from rules/xiangqi.rs::gen_horse.
- [ ] **[S] Wire CANNON_FAST_MOVE house rule** ‚ÄĒ Banqi cannon non-capturing slide goes any number of squares (capture still requires the one-piece jump). Reuse the ray walk from rules/banqi.rs::gen_chariot_rush.
- [ ] **[M] Threefold repetition draw detection** ‚ÄĒ Add Zobrist hashing on Board + (side_to_move) and a position-count map. Update on make_move/unmake_move. Triggers GameStatus::Drawn when count >= rules.draw_policy.repetition_threshold. See state/repetition.rs (currently a stub).

## P2

Worth doing, no rush.

- [ ] **[S] chess-web: in-game rule editor (gear icon ‚Üí modal)** ‚ÄĒ Picker-side form already encodes rules into the URL (`?strict=1`, `?house=chain,rush&seed=N`). Add a sidebar gear that opens a pre-filled modal reusing the picker components, navigates to the new query, and starts a fresh game. ‚Üí [research](backlog/web-ingame-rules-modal.md)
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: WS auto-reconnect with backoff** ‚ÄĒ PR-1 surfaces "Disconnected ‚ÄĒ refresh" when the WS read pump closes. Add an exponential-backoff retry that reuses the URL+password and resets the `Hello` flow on success. ‚Üí [research](backlog/web-ws-reconnect.md)
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: CJK/ASCII glyph toggle in UI** ‚ÄĒ `glyph::Style` is already plumbed; expose a sidebar toggle + persist in `localStorage`. Shares no logic with chess-tui's `--style` flag.
- [ ] **[M] Promote orient.rs / glyph.rs to a shared client crate** ‚ÄĒ Both `clients/chess-tui/src/orient.rs` and `clients/chess-web/src/orient.rs` (likewise `glyph.rs`) are byte-identical. Pull into `clients/chess-client-shared` once a third client appears or the copies first diverge. ‚Üí [research](backlog/promote-client-shared.md)
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: Playwright E2E smoke** ‚ÄĒ Headless two-tab flow that creates a room, plays a few moves, resigns, and rematches. Cheaper than `wasm-bindgen-test` for full-app smoke. ‚Üí [research](backlog/web-playwright.md)
- [ ] **[M] chess-net: mixed-variant rooms** ‚ÄĒ Today one server boots with one `--variant` and every auto-created room copies it. Move to per-room `RuleSet` (a chooser inside the `CreateRoom` form). Architecture supports it (`RoomState` already owns its `GameState`); blocks on UX decisions about a variant filter inside the lobby and what default to apply when joining via a URL with no variant hint.
- [ ] **[S] chess-net: chat moderation primitives** ‚ÄĒ Current chat is a friend-only space (50-line ring buffer, no rate limiting, no mute, no word filter). Add per-player rate limit (~1 msg/sec leaky bucket), client-side `ChatMute` toggle, and an opt-in `--chat-filter <list>` server flag for substring scrubbing. ‚Üí [research](backlog/chess-net-chat-moderation.md)
- [ ] **[S] chess-net: chat system messages** ‚ÄĒ `ChatLine.from` is `Side` today so there's no in-channel narration when seats fill / leave / rematch resets. Widen the type to an enum with a `System` variant and emit join/leave/resign system lines. Wire-breaking ‚Üí bumps protocol to v4. ‚Üí [research](backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md)
- [ ] **[S] chess-net: spectator chat permission per room** ‚ÄĒ ADR-0006 picked players-only as the default; a streamer flow wants a per-room "spectators may chat" toggle (set on room creation, read by `process_chat`'s gate). Small protocol addition next to room password.
- [ ] **[S] chess-net: reconnect-as-same-seat** ‚ÄĒ Disconnecting frees the seat; the reconnect path currently rebooks at the next free side. Add a one-shot rejoin token (issued in `Hello`, presented as `?token=...` on reconnect) so a flaky link doesn't lose your color.

Belongs in chess-net (server-authoritative tick) or as a thin state wrapper if local-only is enough. Adds GameStatus::Won { reason: Timeout } trigger ‚ÄĒ the WinReason variant already exists. Out of scope: client-side animation; servers tick authoritatively and broadcast remaining time in PlayerView.
