```text
## P3

Someday / nice-to-have.

- [ ] **[L] chess-engine + chess-ai: search and evaluation** ‚ÄĒ Alpha-beta + iterative deepening + Zobrist transposition for xiangqi. Banqi needs ISMCTS (information-set MCTS) to handle hidden info ‚ÄĒ research-heavy and a separate problem from xiangqi search. ‚Üí [research](backlog/chess-ai-search.md)
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: move animations** ‚ÄĒ CSS transitions on piece translate-translate as a `<g transform>` interpolation. Cheap visual polish; doesn't change protocol. Care needed: the SVG renders pieces by display row/col, so animation needs to track piece identity across renders (key-by-square is wrong for moves).
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: history scrubber + undo via UI** ‚ÄĒ Engine already has `unmake_move` and `Replay::play_to`. Need a sidebar timeline that replays positions visually without committing to engine state. Reuse `Replay::from_game` so server replays + client scrubbing share one path.
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: mobile layout** ‚ÄĒ Sidebar collapses below the board on narrow viewports; piece tap target enlarged. Touch input already works via SVG `on:click`; this is purely CSS + viewport meta.
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: PWA / offline cache** ‚ÄĒ Service worker caches `dist/` so local pass-and-play works offline. Online play needs the server, so PWA only helps the local-only flows.
- [ ] **[S] chess-web: i18n strings table** ‚ÄĒ All copy is currently inline English+CJK. Pull into a strings module keyed by locale, switch via `?lang=zh-Hant` or browser locale.
- [ ] **[S] Replay format (PGN-equivalent)** ‚ÄĒ Once notation is full (WXF + ICCS + banqi), the history Vec<MoveRecord> already contains everything. Add a serializer to text format + import. Unblocks observers / spectators / training data.
- [ ] **[S] insta snapshot tests for initial positions** ‚ÄĒ Lock in the visual rendering of fresh xiangqi/banqi positions. Catches regressions in setup.rs that pass type-checks but place pieces wrong. Plan flagged this; deferred from PR 1 because unit tests in setup.rs already cover key invariants.

## P?

Needs a spike before committing to a real priority. Tag as `[?/Effort]`.

- [ ] **[?/L] Wish/SSH multiplayer alternative to chess-net** ‚ÄĒ Original analysis (docs/architecture.md) noted Go+wish would give 'ssh chess.you.dev' for free. Rust equivalent: russh (less polished). Spike: is this simpler than chess-net + chess-web for the 'play with friends' scenario, or just neat?
- [ ] **[?/L] Unity renderer via FFI** ‚ÄĒ Architecture deliberately keeps chess-core platform-agnostic so Unity is reachable. Spike: does building a chess-core C ABI + Unity importer get us anything beyond 'Web with art assets via Leptos + sprites'? Likely not, but documenting the option closes the loop.
- [ ] **[?/M] WebRTC peer-to-peer fallback for chess-web** ‚ÄĒ Lets two browsers play without chess-net hosting. Needs a signalling step (could reuse chess-net briefly for handshake). Big UX delta: NAT traversal failures are the user's problem, not the server's. Worth it only if hosting cost ever becomes a concern.

## Done

- ‚úÖ [2026-05-07] [P2/M] chess-net: spectator slots + in-room chat (protocol v3) ‚ÄĒ Third+ connection joining a room with `?role=spectator` upgrades to read-only spectator (`ServerMsg::Spectating` welcome instead of `Hello`); Move/Resign/Rematch/Chat from a spectator return `Error{"spectators cannot ..."}`. Players-only chat: `ClientMsg::Chat { text }` is rate-limit free for now but trimmed to ‚Č§256 chars and stamped with `ts_ms` server-side; broadcast as `ServerMsg::Chat { line }` to seats + spectators and replayed to late joiners via `ServerMsg::ChatHistory` (50-line ring buffer, `VecDeque` per room). `RoomSummary` gains `spectators: u16` (`#[serde(default)]` for v2 back-compat); the lobby push includes the count and the chess-web lobby gains a Watch button while chess-tui gains a `w` lobby key. Spectator capacity defaults to 16 per room, `--max-spectators` / `CHESS_NET_MAX_SPECTATORS` to override. chess-tui press 't' to open a chat input editor (Enter sends, Esc cancels, players only). chess-web ships a sidebar `<ChatPanel>` with auto-scrolling log + disabled-for-spectators input. `make play-spectator` + `scripts/play-spectator.sh` boot a 2-player + 1-spectator tmux demo. ADR-0006 captures the spectator opt-in choice (`?role=spectator` rather than auto-fallback) for v2 back-compat. 8 new server-smoke tests + 5 new round-trip tests; v2 RoomSummary still decodes via `serde(default)`. Chat moderation, spectator chat permission per room, system messages (`ChatLine.from` enum widening to v4), reconnect-as-same-seat, and chess-web chat polish are separate P2 follow-ups.

- ‚úÖ [2026-05-07] [P2/M] chess-web: Leptos + WASM frontend ‚ÄĒ `clients/chess-web` ships as a single-page Leptos+Trunk SPA covering all three variants (xiangqi local + online; banqi local + online; three-kingdom renders the empty 4√ó8 stub with a "WIP" overlay per CLAUDE.md). SVG-only rendering ‚ÄĒ no art assets ‚ÄĒ code-as-presentation, mirroring chess-tui's intersection layout. Both modes share one `<Board>` component: local mode owns `GameState` and projects `PlayerView` after every move; online mode receives `PlayerView` via `gloo-net` WS to `chess-net`'s `/ws/<room>` and `/lobby` endpoints. `orient.rs` + `glyph.rs` are byte-copies of the chess-tui modules with cloned tests (drift caught by both crates' CI). chess-net gains a `static-serve` feature (default-on) and `--static-dir <path>` flag that mounts `tower_http::services::ServeDir` as the route fallback so a single binary serves the SPA + WS in production; chess-web depends on chess-net with `default-features = false` so axum/tokio do not leak into the WASM build. New `make play-web` / `make build-web` / `make stop-web` targets + `scripts/play-web.sh` mirror the existing tmux harnesses. Trunk dev-server proxies `/ws*` and `/lobby` to chess-net at :7878. Reconnect / mobile layout / move animations / Playwright E2E / promote-orient-glyph-to-shared-crate are separate follow-ups in P2/P3.

- ‚úÖ [2026-05-06] [P2/M] chess-net: multi-room lobby + optional password ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-net` server now keys rooms by string id (`Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<Mutex<RoomState>>>>>`). New routes: `GET /ws/<id>` (auto-creates the room), `GET /lobby` (live `ServerMsg::Rooms` push on every state change), `GET /rooms` (JSON snapshot for `curl`). Optional `?password=<secret>` query param locked in by the first joiner; mismatched joiners get `Error{"bad password"}` before any `Hello` and are dropped. Default room `main` (served from `/` and `/ws`) is permanent so v1 clients keep working. `chess-tui` adds `Screen::HostPrompt`, `Screen::Lobby`, `Screen::CreateRoom` plus the `Connect to server‚Ä¶` picker entry; `--lobby <ws>` and `--password <pw>` flags. Lobby uses a second sync `tungstenite` worker ‚ÄĒ no tokio added to the TUI. New ADR-0005, `make play-lobby` launcher, 8 server-smoke tests, 3 protocol round-trips. Mixed-variant servers / spectators / lobby filter / debouncing / admin tokens / TLS are separate P2 follow-ups.

- ‚úÖ [2026-05-06] [P2/M] chess-net MVP: single-room websocket server + chess-tui --connect + tmux launcher ‚ÄĒ `crates/chess-net` ships an axum-ws server (`chess-net-server`) hosting one game/room. Wire schema: tagged JSON enums `ServerMsg` (Hello/Update/Error) and `ClientMsg` (Move/Resign), `Move::Reveal` stays `revealed: None` end-to-end. First connection = Red, second = Black, third gets "room full". `chess-tui --connect ws://...` runs a sync `tungstenite` worker thread + `std::sync::mpsc` channels (no tokio in TUI). `make play-local` / `scripts/play-local.sh` opens a tmux session with two clients in window 0 and the server in window 1. 8 new tests (6 protocol round-trip + 2 end-to-end smoke). Lobby / reconnect / time controls / takeback still deferred (kept as separate TODO items).

- ‚úÖ [2026-05-06] [P2/M] chess-tui: ratatui frontend ‚ÄĒ interactive TUI for xiangqi + banqi. Vim cursor (hjkl) + arrows + mouse, Enter/Space select-or-commit, `u` undo, `f` flip (banqi). Two render styles: CJK glyphs (Śł•šĽēÁõłšŅ•ŚāĆÁāģŚÖĶ / ŚįáŚ£ęŤĪ°ŤĽäť¶¨Á†≤Śćí, default with color) and ASCII (--style ascii). Banqi displays in traditional 8√ó4 layout (transposed from 4√ó8 model). Variant picker on bare invocation; --as black flips xiangqi orientation for net-readiness. Engine stays presentation-free ‚ÄĒ orient.rs + glyph.rs live in the client.

- ‚úÖ [2026-05-06] [P1/S] End-condition unit tests (checkmate / stalemate / banqi no-moves / no-progress) ‚ÄĒ tests/end_conditions.rs verifies refresh_status produces Won{Checkmate} / Won{Stalemate} / Won{OnlyOneSideHasPieces} from .pos fixtures. Pattern reusable for endgame puzzles.
```

```text
112:- **`make_move` does NOT auto-refresh `status`.** The xiangqi legality filter calls `make_move` on a clone to test self-check; auto-refresh would recurse via `legal_moves`. Callers (CLI / TUI / future server) invoke `state.refresh_status()` after each move when they want to know if the game ended. `refresh_status` covers no-progress draws + no-legal-moves; threefold repetition is a TODO.
114:- **Three deferred house rules accept the flag but no-op**: `DARK_CHAIN`, `HORSE_DIAGONAL`, `CANNON_FAST_MOVE`. Only `CHAIN_CAPTURE` and `CHARIOT_RUSH` are wired end-to-end. Don't assume code that consumes `HouseRules` handles every flag ‚ÄĒ grep `rules/banqi.rs::generate` to confirm. The deferred ones are P1 in `TODO.md`.
116:- **`Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi` exists but produces an empty 4√ó8 board.** The types are ready (3-seat `TurnOrder`, `Side(2)`, `BoardShape::ThreeKingdom`), but the actual mask + capture rules ship in PR 2. The setup builder is `setup.rs::build_three_kingdom_stub`. See `backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md` for what the implementation needs to settle.
118:- **WASM build needs `getrandom = { features = ["js"] }`** for `wasm32-unknown-unknown` browser builds. `chess-core/Cargo.toml` adds this as a target-specific dep. Symptom and root cause documented in `pitfalls/wasm-getrandom-unresolved-imp.md`.
120:- **Move list is `SmallVec<[Move; 32]>`.** Positions exceeding 32 legal moves spill to heap once ‚ÄĒ fine for correctness but watch when doing AI work.
```
</details></tool-use>

Encoding lives in `routes.rs::{parse_local_rules, build_local_query, build_rule_set}` (pure-logic, native-tested): `?strict=1` for strict xiangqi (default = casual), `?house=chain,rush&seed=42` for banqi house rules + deterministic shuffle, `?preset=taiwan|aggressive|purist` as an alt to `house=`. Three-kingdom ignores all params. In-game rule editing (gear icon) is a P2 follow-up ‚ÄĒ see `backlog/web-ingame-rules-modal.md`.

```text
49	cargo run -p chess-tui -- --as black xiangqi              # render as Black
50	
51	# Networked play (multi-room ‚ÄĒ see ADR-0005; spectator + chat ‚ÄĒ see ADR-0006)
52	cargo run -p chess-net -- --port 7878 xiangqi             # server, all rooms = xiangqi
53	cargo run -p chess-net -- --port 7878 xiangqi --strict    # strict self-check
54	cargo run -p chess-net -- --port 7878 banqi --preset taiwan --seed 42
55	cargo run -p chess-net -- --port 7878 --max-spectators 32 xiangqi  # raise spectator cap (default 16)
56	
57	# Client ‚ÄĒ entry points to the same server:
58	cargo run -p chess-tui -- --lobby   ws://127.0.0.1:7878            # browse rooms (Enter join / w watch / c create)
59	cargo run -p chess-tui -- --connect ws://127.0.0.1:7878             # default room "main" (back-compat)
60	cargo run -p chess-tui -- --connect ws://127.0.0.1:7878/ws/myroom   # named room
61	cargo run -p chess-tui -- --connect ws://127.0.0.1:7878/ws/locked --password secret  # locked room
62	cargo run -p chess-tui -- --connect 'ws://127.0.0.1:7878/ws/myroom?role=spectator'   # watch read-only
63	
64	curl -s http://127.0.0.1:7878/rooms | jq                   # JSON room snapshot (incl. spectator counts)
65	
66	# tmux harnesses
67	make play-local                                           # 1 server + 2 --connect clients (default room)
68	make play-lobby                                           # 1 server + 3 panes (2 lobby flow + 1 watcher)
69	make play-web                                             # 1 server + Trunk dev-server (SPA on :8080)
70	make play-spectator                                       # 1 server + 2 players + 1 spectator (chat / role demo)
71	make play-local VARIANT=banqi                             # banqi
72	make play-local PORT=9000 VARIANT=xiangqi                 # custom port
```

```bash
feat(fx): victory confetti + ŚįáŤĽć toggle (TUI+Web, protocol v4)

banner overlaid on the board. Net players see personalised copy keyed
off their seat; spectators and Local pass-and-play see neutral
"Red Wins / Black Wins". The ŚįáŤĽć/CHECK warning is now toggleable in
both clients (default ON) and finally extends to chess-tui Net mode.

Wire change: PlayerView gains pub in_check: bool (#[serde(default)],
banqi always reads false). Bumps PROTOCOL_VERSION to v4 ‚ÄĒ wire-compatible
with v3 clients via the serde default. ADR-0007 captures the choice.

chess-tui ships --no-confetti / --no-check-banner CLI flags, hand-rolled
ASCII art (no figlet-rs dep), and a tiny xorshift32 particle physics
loop that runs at ~20 fps via dynamic poll_ms. Picker gains a 5-row
ASCII title (single-line fallback in narrow terminals).

chess.fx.checkBanner) wired to two sidebar checkboxes; CSS-driven
confetti (48 keyframed divs) and a Leptos overlay component. Picker
gains a hero header with Śł• glyph + bilingual title.

)" 2>&1 | tail -20
```
